title: Story Mode:A Block and a Hard Place
text: Details Episode: 4 Season: 1 Release date: December 20, 2015 Written by: Brad Kane and Laura Jacqmin Directed by: Graham Ross and Rebekah Gamin-Arcovitch A Block and a Hard Place, also known as Episode 4, is the fourth episode of Minecraft: Story Mode. It was released on December 20, 2015. The episode follows Jesse and their friends as they work with Ivor in a final effort to stop the Wither Storm. Synopsis “ Jesse discovers the truth about the Order of the Stone, gaining an unexpected ally. Armed with a new plan, Jesse and friends face the Witherstorm. „ — In-game description. Plot Jesse and their friends are forced to retreat to a safer area by horse, dodging the wither storm's attacks and building a bridge along the way. At a cavern, the group rest before they head off again. Gabriel/Petra's (whoever was trapped in the wither storm) memories begins to come back, remembering certain events from earlier episodes. Lukas may leave the group to search for his gang, while the wither sick Gabriel/Petra may be told by Jesse to stay behind to rest. Outside, Soren and Ivor are in an argument about the wither storm, but it is revealed that the wither storm was intentionally following Gabriel's amulet as Ivor had programmed, but failed to account for the amulet falling in Jesse's hands. Ivor apologizes for the trouble he caused, and says he has a solution to destroying the command block inside the wither storm. Accepting the idea, the group head off to Ivor's lab, located in the Far Lands. Axel and Magnus/Ellegaard take the amulet and split from the group. The group decides to take a rest in a swamp and find some food before heading off again. Jesse and Ivor find a nearby witch hut with a cake adorning the window, but Ivor warns them that witches hate trespassers. Soon, the witch arrives back at the hut, defending the cake by attacking Jesse with potions, but Ivor mysteriously disappears. Alternately, if Jesse chooses to return to the group with their findings (potatoes), the mobs still attack, but this time Ivor stays behind to distract them. Jesse and the group barely escape the onslaught and arrive at the Far Lands. They find an entrance that leads them to Ivor's lab, completing a maze and building a redstone contraption in the process. Inside the lab, Jesse and Soren find the five treasures of the Order (prismarine shards, sea lantern, ghast tear, packed ice, and a dragon egg), and use them to open up yet another door, leading them to a room that appears to be designed after the Order of the Stone's battle with the ender dragon. As Soren explains the battle, Jesse opens the next door, containing the enchantment book and ender crystals, which Soren claims to have been destroyed during the battle with the ender dragon. Jesse realizes that Soren had lied about the story, and he admits that the command block was used to make the dragon vanish and the Order were never truly heroes, which ultimately caused Ivor to leave the group in the first place. Ivor returns and explains that he only intended to prove that the Order were false heroes using the wither storm. Jesse and their group of friends are in total shock about this ordeal, but quickly forget it as they have to defeat the wither storm. Ivor gives Jesse the enchantment book needed in order to break the command block, uses it on a diamond tool, and offers them a new set of armor. With everyone ready to go, they head back to the main land to fight the wither storm. Back at Soren's temple, the group attempt to destroy the wither storm by provoking endermen to be attracted to it. The enderman are then able to remove blocks from the wither storm, creating a hole that allows Jesse to go inside the storm. The group use all of the items that they have to create an invention that will allow Jesse to go inside the storm. The invention works, launching Jesse and Reuben into the wither storm. Inside the storm, Jesse and Reuben find the command block. With Jesse using the enchanted weapon, the command block begins to crack, provoking the wither storm, using tentacles to defend the command block. Jesse fends off numerous tentacles, and is able to crack the command block a second time, but the tentacles grab them and force them to drop their weapon. Reuben grabs the weapon and delivers it to Jesse, but is knocked into a pit. Jesse finishes off the command block once and for all, causing the storm to explode. Back on the ground, Jesse finds a gravely injured Reuben, who dies in their arms. A week later, back at home,

title: Story Mode:A Journey's End?
text: Details Episode: 8 Season: 1 Release date: September 13, 2016 Written by: Eric Stirpe, Yale Hannon, and Erica Harrell Directed by: Vahram Antonian A Journey's End?, also known as Episode 8, is the eighth episode of Telltale Games ' Minecraft: Story Mode: Season One. It was released on September 13, 2016. Jesse’s group arrives in the deadly games of the Old Builders, where they must fight for their way home. Synopsis “ A new friend points Jesse and the gang toward the way home: a village of cutthroat competitors playing games to the death for their own chance at freedom from their powerful oppressors. „ — In-game description. Plot The five (Jesse, Petra, Lukas, Ivor and Harper) head up the stairway to a door. Harper then pushes Jesse, Petra, Lukas and Ivor off the platform, and they fall into a game of spleef in progress. After Ivor/Petra and Lukas die (depending on who was saved), Jesse begins using their sword, which, unbeknownst to them, is illegal. Hadrian, Otto, and Mevia (the rest of the Old Builders) call for the game to be stopped, informing Jesse of the "rules of the game". Fortunately, Lukas and Ivor/Petra respawn as they are forced to "go work in the mines", and Jesse and Ivor/Petra are allowed to stay in the competitor's village. In the competitor's village, Jesse and Ivor/Petra attempt to get along with their fellow competitors, with some of them mentioning a legend named Tim who supposedly was the only one to be able to win the games. After getting a piece of bread for Facemeat, one of the gladiators, he allows Lukas and Ivor/Petra out of the mines. Harper and Otto also show up, informing the four (Jesse, Lukas, Petra, and Ivor) that they are on their side, and the four then rest for the night. The next morning, Jesse and Petra/Ivor participate in the next day of the Games. The Green Team calls for a truce, which Jesse can either accept or reject. The Games then begin with a race, which eventually leads the competitors into Gladiator Junction, an area overrun by Gladiators. Jesse is barely able to make it out alive and is able to assist Nell, a Green Team member, to victory. Unbeknownst to Jesse, Emily, also from the Green team, had made a deal with Hadrian to eliminate Jesse, but she didn't follow through. Jesse is then ordered by Slab, one of the gladiators, to meet with Hadrian, and Jesse reluctantly complies. Inside Hadrian's office, Hadrian talks with Jesse about winning the Games and tells Jesse that Tim is just a story Hadrian made up to keep the competitors motivated. Breaking down a wall, Hadrian reveals that he captured Olivia, Axel, and Reuben (who turns out to be the usher from the first episode). Hadrian then suddenly changes the terms of his deal with Jesse, stating that if Jesse wins, then Olivia and Axel will work in the mines forever, but if Jesse loses, all of their friends will be able to leave, but Jesse will have to stay behind. Jesse can either agree to the deal or decline it, and is sent back to the competitor's village. At the competitor's village, Jesse informs the competitors that Hadrian has rigged the game against them, and Jesse can choose to disclose that Tim is not real. They all then rest for the night. The next morning, everyone participates in the final day of the Games, with "The Walls". Jesse is thrown into an area which basically is just dirt and gravel. After the walls come down, the participants then team up and the Gladiators defect, giving Jesse and the other participants the victory. Just as Otto is about to give Jesse the Atlas, Hadrian and Mevia trap Otto in obsidian and battle Jesse. Fortunately, Jesse wins thanks to "Tim's armor," and Jesse is rightfully given the Atlas. Otto plans to make the games fun again, and Jesse then can convince Emily to go home, help Otto with the games, or join them. The six (Jesse, Axel, Petra, Lukas, Olivia, and Ivor) then head home and put their souvenirs on display. As they are about to leave, Jesse and Lukas notice that Ivor has run off with the Atlas and the enchanted flint and steel, and Jesse can decide whether to go after him or let him be. In a post-credits scene, Hadrian and Mevia spawn in a bedrock world of chicken-sized zombies/zombie-sized chickens (depending on the first choice Jesse made in the first episode). As they both have no way out, Hadrian relieves Mevia of her duties as enforcer. Appearances Characters Hadrian Ivor Mevia Harper Otto Petra Tim Axel Olivia Lukas Nell Emily Clutch Slab Facemeat Jesse Reuben (human) Mobs Settings Portal Network Overworld ( A Journey's End? )

title: Story Mode:A Portal to Mystery
text: Details Episode: 6 Season: 1 Release date: June 7, 2016 Written by: Eric Stirpe and Timothy Williams Directed by: Sean Manning A Portal to Mystery, also known as Episode 6, is the sixth episode of Minecraft: Story Mode. It was released on June 7, 2016. Jesse and their friends enter a new dimension and become trapped in a mansion with other adventurers, where they must solve a murder mystery while uncovering the secrets of the White Pumpkin. Synopsis “ Trying to find their way home, Jesse and friends stumble across a strange mansion where a "Host" is killing off their houseguests. Jesse must find the killer and find a way out before a friend is framed for the crimes. „ — In-game description. Plot One of the portals leads the four (Jesse, Lukas, Petra and Ivor) to a cemetery. On one of the tombstones is a book, which is an invitation to the Host's mansion. The group are then surrounded by hundreds of zombies, but they escape and make it to the mansion. At the mansion, the front door is locked, so the four go around back and make it in through a kitchen window. Inside, they meet, for some reason, YouTubers TorqueDawg and CaptainSparklez. Suddenly, TorqueDawg dies after being hit by tipped arrows shot from a dispenser. LDShadowLady then greets the group, and they all head to the main hall. There, they meet Cassie Rose (along with her pet cat, Winslow), DanTDM, Stampy Cat, and stacyplays, and they all head to the dining room. In the dining room, CaptainSparklez tells everyone that TorqueDawg has died, and Jesse may show them the cause of death. Afterward, Jesse can ask anyone for information regarding TorqueDawg's behavior and personality. CaptainSparklez then tells Jesse that TorqueDawg was in search of a "rare item" - the enchanted flint and steel. Jesse may or may not show CaptainSparklez the enchanted flint and steel that he has, and CaptainSparklez informs everyone that they were brought here because someone has it. Suddenly, CaptainSparklez falls into a pit and dies from suffocation. The lights then turn off, and a figure wearing a white pumpkin on it's head appears. The White Pumpkin then informs everyone that he is seeking the treasure that he desires (the enchanted flint and steel), and that he will not rest until he gets it, even if he has to murder others. Cassie Rose tries to escape, but the mansion is surrounded by zombies. Jesse and Lukas then inspect the dining room, and find four buttons on the dining room table. They also find out that the White Pumpkin in said room was made using an armor stand with a jukebox as a voice, likely as a diversion. Jesse then interrogates four likely suspects of being the White Pumpkin; the suspects being DanTDM, LDShadowLady, Stampy Cat, and Lukas. DanTDM claims that the flint and steel is his, that he had never seen tipped arrows before, and that he is a redstone novice. He goes to Lizzie for potion information and Stampy for redstone information. LDShadowLady wants the flint and steel, claims that she is an intermediate redstoner, and that she knows what tipped arrows are. She thinks that Stampy could steal the flint and steel, and she also goes to him for potion/redstone information. Stampy doesn't want the flint and steel, claims that DanTDM made up his claim, and that he never saw tipped arrows. He goes to LDShadowLady for potion information. However, Stampy lies about redstone, saying that he is a redstone novice, while he actually was the Tri-Block Champion. Afterward, Jesse may accuse someone of being the White Pumpkin, or instead conclude that there isn't enough evidence to suspect anyone of being so. Lukas keeps an eye on the five while Jesse, Petra, and Ivor inspect the kitchen trap. Inside the kitchen, Jesse punches out a painting on the wall, revealing a passageway with a lever that supposedly controls the dispenser firing the tipped arrows. Jesse realizes that the White Pumpkin uses the passageway to move about the mansion in secret. The three go through the passageway to the library, which is filled with dozens of spiders. Depending on who you choose to attack, either DanTDM or LDShadowLady are killed by spiders or murdered by the White Pumpkin. Jesse and Lukas then race after the White Pumpkin to the east side of the mansion before he puts the mask on Lukas. With Lukas thus adorned, the other people mistakenly accuse Lukas of being the White Pumpkin. Lukas is interrogated, while Jesse and Petra find evidence to prove that Lukas was innocent. Jesse and Petra then find a secret entrance to the basement of the mansion. In the basement, Jesse and Petra find an item transportation system, a white pumpkin farm, and a central

title: Dungeons:Abomination Vine
text:
It has been suggested that this page be merged with Jungle Abomination.   [ discuss ]
If this merge affects many pages or may potentially be controversial, do not merge the page until a consensus is reached.
Reason: This is a non-attackable mob that only appears when summoned by the Jungle Abomination and should only be included in the mob's main page that summons it to be consistent with other summoned entities such as Biomines and Evoker Fangs
For other uses, see Abomination (disambiguation).
Health points: Non-attackable
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Plant
Immunities: Everything
Spawn: Summoned by the Jungle Abomination
An abomination vine is a hostile mob that can be summoned by the jungle abomination within the Jungle Awakens DLC of Minecraft Dungeons.
Behavior
Abomination vines behave similarly to quick growing vines, but they are immune to all forms of damage and can do contact damage​ [ more information needed ] to the hero when they sprout up. The jungle abomination usually summons about eight of these scattered in a large circle around itself, but can do a few other patterns such as just four of them in a small square around itself. They sink back down into the ground after a few seconds. Despite being invincible, they can still be stunned by heroes using the snowball or stunning enchantments, although it has no effect.

title: Dungeons:Jungle Abomination
text: Behavior: Hostile Classification: Boss, Golem The Jungle Abomination is the boss of the Jungle Awakens DLC for Minecraft Dungeons, fought in the Overgrown Temple mission. Lore Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Dungeons that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk! The jungle abomination was formed after the shattering of the Orb of Dominance, born of a shard buried deep within an Overgrown Temple. This shard spread its corruption across the island, giving rise to deadly vines and other destruction. This shard’s plans were ultimately cut short by a band of heroes, and peace returned to the jungle. Spawning Overgrown Temple ( Boss ) Tower ( Boss ) Behavior The jungle abomination has five different behaviors. It is the slowest and one of the largest mobs in the game, and possesses one of the largest health pools. Vulnerable stun cycle The jungle abomination may become stunned and vulnerable for five seconds after taking damage, where its defense is reduced by 60% and it does not move or attack. Attack The jungle abomination attempts to crush the hero by using its left arm with the villager statue. The jungle abomination cannot move or turn when using this attack. This attack also inflicts poison on hit. The damage of the jungle abomination's melee attack is the same as the creeper 's explosion. Summon The jungle abomination summons two to four whisperers. When below half health, it can also summon two leapleaves. Vine attack The jungle abomination whips its vine-like right arm into the ground and four to ten abomination vines rise from the ground to attack the hero. This attack seems to be used when the abomination falls below half health and deals 40% of the melee's smash per vine. Poison darts The jungle abomination can shoot out two circles of twelve poison darts from itself that poison the hero on a hit. At lower health it may combine this attack with the vine attack. Health The jungle abomination has 19,000 HP. It has 25,000 HP in The Tower. On Default I, all mobs have 9.6% health and 12% damage of what is set in the code. It has the second highest health out of all the bosses. title: Story Mode:Above and Beyond
text: Details Episode: 5 Season: 2 Release date: December 19, 2017 Written by: Adam Miller, Meredith Ainsworth, Doug Lieblich, and Nicole Martinez Directed by: Mark Droste Above and Beyond, also known as Episode 5, is the fifth episode of Telltale Games ' Minecraft: Story Mode - Season Two. It was released on December 19, 2017. The final battle with Jesse’s new enemy reaches Beacontown, where the Admin’s rule has turned their home into a place of fear and control. Synopsis “ The final battle with Jesse's new enemy brings the battle home to Beacontown and the world may never be the same. „ — In-game description. Plot The group ( Jesse, Petra, Jack, Nurm / Lluna and Radar / Binta, Cam and Kent) make their way to Beacontown underground. Upon arriving, the group attempt to head to the map shop, but have to hide as they are almost spotted by Romeo (disguised as Jesse). Romeo then blows up Champion City or kills Xara.​ [ determinant ] Jesse then takes Olivia and Axel with the group to the map shop. In the map shop, Olivia and Axel are relieved that the Admin isn’t the actual Jesse, but are worried that people would turn on Jesse. Nonetheless, the group is hopeful in stopping the Admin. Jesse looks around the map shop and finds a lever, pulling it to reveal a pair of elytra. Suddenly, the group hears some clinking noises, and when Jesse investigates, it is revealed to be Nurm / Lluna (the person that was left behind at the Sunshine Institute). Ivor also shows up, and him and Jack have an awkward conversation. The group then make a plan to stop the Admin, which involves disguising as a member of the Fireworks Guild, Axel and Olivia distracting the Admin, Binta/Radar giving Jesse a bucket of water, and Petra and Jack waiting in the Order hall. Jesse then removes his armor to disguise himself as Romeo and heads outside to begin the plan. Outside, Jesse tries his best to blend into town, but is nearly caught twice as Romeo checks on the townspeople. Nonetheless, Jesse is able to obtain the Fireworks Guild suits. That night, Jesse, Jack, Petra, and Binta / Radar make fireworks, allowing Jesse, Jack and Petra to make their way up to the tower. Inside the tower, the three are met by the Ocelots (consisting of Lukas, Stampy Cat, Stacyplays, and numerous citizens). Lukas mistakes the real Jesse for the Admin at first, but Jesse is able to talk Lukas into believing otherwise. The group then make it up to the Admin’s room, where Romeo greets them. After Jesse has a chitchat with him, Romeo thinks that he can do better “next time”, so he decides to cover Jesse’s world with bedrock. The group barely make it through the portal to the terminal. Inside the terminal, the group find numerous artifacts from their past adventures and use the word of passage to obtain a gold gauntlet, which is meant to strip the Admin of its powers. After an intense battle with Romeo, Jesse gets the upper hand and strips him of his powers. The player can choose whether to take Romeo with him, or leave him behind, and the group make it back to Beacontown through the portal. Back in Beacontown, Jesse and others do all they can to rebuild Beacontown. The player then has the choice of staying in Beacontown or hitting the road. Should the player decide to stay in Beacontown, Radar will inform Jesse that he received a letter from Aiden about “redeeming himself” and a note from “W.P.” telling him to “look out”. This is referring to two characters from the Season One DLC. Aiden was last seen on the surface below sky city and " W.C. " ( Cassie Rose/The White Pumpkin ) was last seen above a pit of endermites underground. Likewise, if the player decides to hit the road, the player can choose whether Radar can run Beacontown or not. Jesse then decides to have a race with Petra. title: Dungeons:Abyssal Monument
text: Part of: Island Realms Boss: Ancient Guardian Minimum difficulty: IV Unlock predecessor: Coral Rise Raid captains: Can very rarely have all six captains Daily trials: Can have daily trials Sunken Sprawl An unbelievable sunken temple sprawls before you, a place lost long ago to time and the changing currents of an indifferent ocean. Forgotten secrets that lie within have been warped by the Orb's corruption, protected by enemies whose weapons and wits have not dulled despite countless eons in the depths. Face the guardian within and claim its treasures for your own. Abyssal Monument is the tenth island realms mission in Minecraft Dungeons, and is part of the Hidden Depths DLC. It is unlocked after the completion of Coral Rise, and completing Abyssal Monument does not unlock any missions. Abyssal Monument is counted as a boss mission for Apocalypse Plus progression, due to it containing the Ancient Guardian. title: Dungeons:Accelerate
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Ranged weapon
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Increases attack speed for each consecutive shot. Resets one second after the attack.
Accelerate is a ranged weapon enchantment in Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 8% increase per shot, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: 10% increase per shot, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: 12% increase per shot, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Any ranged weapon enchanted with Accelerate grants the hero increased attack speed for each attack performed within a second of the previous attack. If an attack is not performed within a second, the attack speed resets to the default speed. The speed boost increases with each tier, at the maximum tier there is a 12% increase per shot. The maximum combined speed increase is 100%.
Stacking
Accelerate is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Built-into
Accelerating tier I is built into the following weapons:
Auto Crossbow
Mechanical Shortbow
Mob
Enchanted mobs with the enchantment will gain increased shooting speed within a second after hitting the hero.
Barrage and its minions spawn with Accelerate.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Accelerate.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Accelerate" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.
Gallery
Accelerate's enchantment icon.
Accelerate's shine pattern.

title: Story Mode:Access Denied
text: Details Episode: 7 Season: 1 Release date: July 26, 2016 Written by: Luke McMullen and Eric Stirpe Directed by: Rebekah Gamin Arcovitch and Jason Pyke Access Denied, also known as Episode 7, is the seventh episode of Telltale Games ' Minecraft: Story Mode: Season One. It was released on July 26, 2016. Jesse’s group arrives in a world controlled by a powerful artificial intelligence named PAMA. Synopsis “ Jesse, Petra, Lukas, and Ivor find themselves in a town controlled by a supercomputer. To free the citizens (and keep their minds), they must join forces with an unlikely new ally. „ — In-game description. Plot One of the portals leads the four (Jesse, Petra, Ivor and Lukas) to a Mesa biome. From there, Jesse and Petra find a supposed "super zombie" and kill it. After this occurs, several "super zombies" approach the group, indicating that Jesse, Lukas, Ivor, and Petra should follow them. The "super zombies" then lead the four to Crown Mesa, a town overrun by mindless people with red chips on the backs of their heads. Jesse then confronts Harper, who is believed to be behind all of this, but she escapes, and the four are brought by the townsfolk to see PAMA (Prototype Autonomous Management Agent), a supercomputer made of redstone equipment which mind-controls anything with the same chips as the townsfolk in their heads (which it describes as making them "useful"). PAMA welcomes the four to Crown Mesa but finds out that the four are not from there. It then asks them questions regarding how they got here and what their world is like, but it makes Petra and Lukas "useful" anyway. Just as Jesse is about to be made "useful", they and Ivor can either use an unsolvable math problem, a logic puzzle, or a riddle to make PAMA confused and to divert power. The two then escape alongside Harper to her secret lab. On the way to her secret lab, Jesse and Ivor find out that water destroys the chips used to control PAMA's henchmen. At the entrance, Jesse, Ivor and Harper are confronted by PAMA's henchmen, which includes Petra and Lukas. Jesse can unchip either Petra or Lukas by dowsing her or him with water. Harper gives Jesse a rose to give to "Harry" (an inanimate build with a zombie head and a jukebox voice) before she is abducted by whoever Jesse did not unchip. At the secret lab, Jesse gives "Harry" a rose, removes ice from "Margaret"'s heart, and gives a redstone torch to another one of Harper's "friends". This allows Jesse to use a headset to control one of PAMA's henchmen, but to also migrate to another one if they get surrounded. This is used to stop Harper from being made "useful". Harper then tells Jesse to head to PAMA's central core and to remove the Redstone Heart; shortly after, Harper is carried away by PAMA's henchmen. The three (Jesse, Petra/Lukas, and Ivor) then head toward PAMA's central core. Inside the core, PAMA confronts Jesse with its henchmen, but they are killed with ease. Jesse then pours water on the redstone blocks connected to the heart in order to stop it. Just as Jesse is about to remove the Redstone Heart, they are confronted by Petra/Lukas (the person that the player left chipped earlier), but Jesse fights them off and is able to remove the Redstone Heart, effectively shutting down PAMA and freeing its henchmen from his control. The group then celebrates with the people of Crown Mesa before going back to the portal room. In the portal room, Harper tells the four that she and some others made an atlas to chart routes between the portal worlds. She says that she doesn't have it, but knows someone who does, and reveals a flight of stairs. title: Dungeons:Acrobat
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Armor
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Reduces the cooldown time between your rolls.
Acrobat is an enchantment that can be found within the various armor of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 15% reduced cooldown, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: 30% reduced cooldown, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: 45% reduced cooldown, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Acrobat reduces the cooldown time between rolls. The reduction amount increases with each tier, at the maximum tier there is a 45% reduced cooldown.
Stacking
Acrobat is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Built-into

Black Wolf Armor
Living Vines Armor

title: Legends:Action
text:
The specific instructions are: Render with model and texture from game.
Details
Voice actor: Scarlet Grace
Identifier: host_action
Action is one of the three Hosts of Minecraft Legends.
Lore
Action pays close attention to the wellbeing of the Overworld entities, partly by guiding the Hero across the training area within the Well of Fate, and informing them whenever a village is targeted by a piglin raid. Despite their brutish appearance, Action is polite and has a childlike voice and disposition.
Appearance
Action is three blocks tall and equally wide, and appears to be made of cobblestone, with some of the individual rock fragments forming three large nails on the front of each limb. They have a muscular golem-like appearance, with elephantine legs, large curved arms and a wide upper body with a hunchback. Their head sits in front of their body, with two small black ears, a single large round eye, and a hinged jaw with no lips. They wear a raggedy brown shawl draped over their back and shoulders.
Behavior
Action along with the other Hosts idles around the Well of Fate. Action is invincible to all forms of damage. During the final battle with the Great Hog, Action and the Hosts will disappear and reappear once the Well of Fate is safe, and the Great Hog is defeated.
Quotes
Name: Action, Category: Overworld, Unlocked by: Automatically unlocked, Quote: "Action is the youngest at heart of the three Hosts, so they're often more quick to emotion."
“ ACTION – Action is the most outspoken Host and often speaks of their care and love for all being in the Overworld. Softly spoken and young at heart, Action can sometimes be impatient and show emotion before the other Hosts. „ — Minecraft Legends:
A Hero's Guide to Saving the Overworld

title: Dungeons:Adriene
text:
Obtaining: Automatically obtained
Level ID: Hedwig
Gender: Female
Species: Hero species
Status: Alive
Aliases: Pinky ( Karl )
Affiliations: Heroes
Allies: Hal Hex Valorie
First appearance: Minecraft Dungeons
Latest appearance: Rise of the Arch-Illager
Actor: Dan Bittner (audiobook)
Adriene, formerly known as Hedwig, is one of the four canonical heroes in Minecraft Dungeons and Minecraft Dungeons Arcade.
Appearance

She has bright pink hair, a striking contrast against her bronzed skin and dark, almost purple eyes. She wears light beige overalls with a thick belt and metallic knee pads over the green fabric. She accessorizes with white bracelets on both wrists and sturdy brown boots.
Personality

Biography
Before and during The Rise of the Arch-Illager, Adriene traveled the Mainland alongside four other heroes known as Valorie, Hex, Hal, and Karl. Due to Karl's reluctance to learn everyone's names, Adriene was often called Pinky due to her pink hair.
After setting up Camp outside the Squid Coast, they noticed Archie heading eastward toward the Soggy Swamp. Later, as the Nameless One 's undead forces carried Archie away from the Squid Coast, they hoped that he was alright.
Minecraft Dungeons Arcade
Minecraft Dungeons Arcadenavigation: #51Frisk, Adriene, #53Wolf
The player equips Adriene when the following cards are used in Minecraft Dungeons Arcade.
Adriene: EquipsAdriene

Quotes
“ Adriene is a tough-as-nails warrior. Their battlecries are legendary—especially the one for cake. „ — Minecraft Dungeons Arcade Card
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Sept 29, 2018, Hedwig appears in theMinecraft Dungeonsannouncement trailer. Hedwig is missing the hat layer.
Minecraft Dungeons: June 9, 2019, Hedwig reappears in the Minecraft Dungeons gameplay reveal trailer. Hedwig now has a hat layer.

Trivia
Adriene is seen in the Official Dungeons Banner equipped with full metal armor and a mace.
On the cover of The Rise of The Arch Illager, she appears to be wearing the mercenary armor and a great hammer.
Adriene is still known as Hedwig internally.

title: Earth:Adventure
text: The Adventure menu icon. Adventures in Minecraft Earth. An adventure was a location on the map in Minecraft Earth where one or more players could have a survival -mode adventure. In an adventure, players were allowed to fight mobs, collect additional resources that are unavailable with tappables, and collect rewards. Appearance An adventure appeared on the map as a beacon pointing into the sky. A bright outline surrounded the adventure icon when the player had approached to within the player's tappable radius. When an adventure appeared, it persisted on the map typically for 7 minutes. Some locations on the map consistently had adventures appearing on them continuously, and other locations had adventures appearing sporadically. After an adventure was completed (either by the time expiring, the players dying, or the player exiting manually), a new adventure appeared at the same location after a delay. The delay duration ranged from less than a minute to several minutes, depending on the location. Gameplay Entering an adventure. Preparation Upon tapping on the adventure location, a screen appeared showing information about the adventure. Icons at the top were able to be tapped to learn: general information about what might've been encountered, who had experienced the adventure before, time remaining before the adventure disappeared from the map. A shield-shaped icon at the lower right allowed the player to give feedback about whether the adventure location was safe and appropriate. Tapping on the hotbar let the player choose which items from the inventory were to be available in the hotbar when the adventure began. Any items placed here were lost if the player was killed during the adventure. A player was expected to have crafted the proper tools before entering an adventure. A pickaxe was needed for mining some stone blocks (a stone pickaxe was required for harvesting iron ore ), a sword was useful for attacking hostile mobs, and an axe was most efficient for breaking wood items. Food was needed to restore health during an adventure if attacked by mobs. Adventure buildplate If the player's camera aimed at the exact GPS location of the adventure, a sign appeared to indicate the location before placing down the adventure buildplate. Tapping the Play button activated the device's camera, with the game's graphics overlayed on the real-world image on the screen. The player was prompted to place a build plate at a safe location in which to play the adventure. Underground and above-ground structures were generated for the build plate. Most adventures included some structural features (such as cobblestone, grass, fences) along with animals at ground level and above, although some adventures appeared as a hole in the ground. Hostile mobs were able to appear above-ground or underground. The buildplate that appeared was typically a 4×4 or 5×5 area, but the playable volume extended beyond the visible surface boundary. The edge of the buildplate was made of bedrock, which wasn't able to be mined. However, it was often possible to mine blocks from underneath this edge. Mobs wandered around beyond the visible edge on the surface, and water and lava also flowed on the real-world surface beyond the buildplate edge. Collecting resources Dirt, gravel, sand, wood, leaves, torches, flowers, and other plants were able to be collected without tools by tapping on the "punch" icon and then hitting the desired object. Mining this way took a similar amount of time as bare-handed mining in the regular Minecraft desktop or console game. The proper tools harvested faster (for example, using an axe on wood is much quicker than punching it), and harder materials were only able to be mined with a pickaxe. Empty slots in the hotbar filled up first, and subsequent blocks and items were placed in the player's backpack inventory, which was available only during adventures, and was lost if the player dies. Many adventures allowed the player to dig underground beyond the surface boundary of the build plate. Digging underground revealed useful and rare blocks, as well as fences, planks, rails, torches, coal ore, and various stone blocks. Iron ore was rare, typically appearing in veins of 1–2 blocks in a small fraction of adventures; 3–4 iron ore blocks were extremely rare. A stone pickaxe was required for mining iron ore and lapis ore (also rare), and an iron pickaxe was required for more exotic minerals such as gold ore, redstone ore, and diamond ore. It wasn't usually possible to climb structures or descend into mines, although a player can crouch or stand tall to change elevation somewhat, which was useful for seeing, digging, and attacking under the surface boundary of the adventure's build plate. However, if the adventure was placed in a location where the player actually was able to go up or down several feet, such as inside a multi-level house or a treehouse,

title: Earth:Adventure Chest
text: Behavior: None An adventure chest was a block-like entity in Minecraft Earth that could be found in adventures, which had 4 variants plus one unused variant. Spawning In some adventures, the chest would generate with the adventure, but in others the players would have to either solve a puzzle or kill all the hostile mobs in the level for it to appear. The chest wasn't always immediately visible, so players may have had to mine in the adventure in order to uncover it. Hitting it would give all participating players a number of rare items. There were 4 types of adventure chests, with each type having a different rarity, and with rarer types containing more valuable items. title: Earth:Adventure Crystal
text:
Rarity: Various
Renewable: Yes
Stackable: Yes
An adventure crystal was an item in Minecraft Earth that allowed the player to place a private adventure at any location and invite friends to join the adventure. These adventure crystals' purpose was to create adventures without the need for the user to go outside due to the ongoing COVID-19 Pandemic (which eventually became the reason for the game's discontinuation).
Obtaining
An adventure crystal could be obtained from:
Chest tappables
Adventure chests
Daily login challenges
Seasonal challenges
Usage
Adventure crystals were required to activate adventures. Rarer crystals generated larger adventures, and contained more valuable loot in the adventure chest. The player's crystal inventory could hold a maximum of five crystals of each type. Tapping the crystal icon showed the crystal adventure interface, which showed the crystals collected by the player and their rarity. Tapping a crystal showed the adventure duration, as well as a graphic representation of the size of the adventure and the reward.
Legendary Adventure Crystal
Rarity: Legendary
Renewable: Yes
Stackable: Yes
Legendary Adventure Crystal was added in 0.19.0, but later were not used in the game, so they remained only in the game files. They were supposed to activate legendary adventures, at the end of which, respectively, player could get a legendary adventure chest.

title: Dungeons:Adventure Hub
text: The adventure hub is a totem-like golem located within the camp between the luxury merchant's stall and the map table. Spawning Camp Reward tree The adventure hub offers a tree of rewards for a seasonal adventure. There are a total of fifty three levels and new levels can be unlocked by getting adventure points. title: Dungeons:Adventure Pass
text:
For Adventure pass in Minecraft: Story Mode, see MCSM:Adventure Pass.

The adventure pass is an upgrade for Minecraft Dungeons. It is a DLC extension of the seasonal adventures that unlocks the premium track in the Adventure Hub. It is available for US$ 9.99 ( Can$ 14.29, € 9.99, £ 9.29, A$ 11.95, NZ$ 11.90, S$ 11.70). Note: The adventure pass is not to be confused as an in-game purchase although it can only be purchased once since the player will keep it infinitely. There is one per season. The things you can get are listed in the "List" section right bellow this message.
List
Cloudy Climb Adventure Pass
Luminous Night Adventure Pass
Fauna Faire Adventure Pass

title: Legends:Age of Empires 2 Collaboration
text:
Type: Collaboration
Date: June 27 - July 11, 2023
The Age of Empires 2 Collaboration was a limited time collaboration with Minecraft Legends in the video game Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition from June 27 to July 11, 2023 as the main monthly event in the game. Players could unlock features from Minecraft Legends by completing challenges on different five days.
Challenges
Day: ∞, Challenge: Sign in to Xbox Live., Reward: Unlocks a profile icon of a Legends Hero on his horse.
Day: 1, Challenge: Hunt 10 Boars with Villagers in any amount of matches., Reward: Unlocks a graphical mod where boars are replaced bywarboars.
Day: 2, Challenge: Research 15 Blacksmith upgrades in any amount of matches., Reward: Unlocks a profile icon of amace runt.
Day: 3, Challenge: Train 64 Militia-line units in any amount of matches., Reward: Unlocks a graphical mod where Militia-line units are replaced by mace runts.
Day: 4, Challenge: Gather 4096 Gold in any amount of matches., Reward: Unlocks a profile icon ofthe beast.
Day: 5, Challenge: Defeat 32 units or buildings with exploding units in any amount of matches., Reward: Unlocks a graphical mod where Petards units are replaced byLegends creepers.
Quotes
“ A portal has been opened and hordes of Piglins and Creepers are already attacking our poor villagers, it’s time to fight back! Grab your sword – it’s time to defend your empire! „ — Steam Page

title: Story Mode:Aiden
text:
Note: Empty sections
Details
Gender: Male
Species: Human
Status: Alive
Affiliations: Ocelots ​ [ formerly ] Blaze Rods
Allies: See § Allies
Enemies: See § Enemies
First appearance: The Order of the Stone
Latest appearance: Order Up!
Actor: Matthew Mercer
Aiden is a character in Minecraft: Story Mode, who caused Reuben to run away from the EnderCon Building Competition in The Order of the Stone, and the main antagonist in Order Up!. He was one of the four members of the Ocelots, a team of builders rival to Jesse's gang. He later forms his own group, the Blaze Rods. He is voiced by Matthew Mercer.
Appearance
His skin is a desaturated white color in the game version, but seems more saturated and darkened in the Netflix version. He has olive green eyes and short brown hair. He wears a black leather jacket with the face of an ocelot on it. Under this, he wears a green Magnus themed shirt with yellow buttons similar to Magnus' armor. Later in Order Up!, he wears armor similar to that of a Roman gladiator, which also resembles the arms and legs of Soren's armor.His jacket is now adorned with a logo featuring two blaze rods instead of an ocelot face.
Personality
He is very self-centered and egotistical, being rude to Jesse and their group, and risking Reuben's life in the first episode. In A Block and a Hard Place, if Jesse lets Lukas leave on good terms and lets him go after the rest of the Ocelots, Aiden will help Jesse's gang destroy the wither storm by distracting it with fireworks. Throughout Order Up!, he is shown as manipulative, selfish, bitter, and merciless until the very end. Near the end of Order Up!, if Jesse threw Aiden over the edge of Sky City, Aiden will hold a grudge until the end. However when Jesse decided to take him to safety when he surrendered, he is shown to be able to repent and then says he will become a writer and start over, he even sent Jesse a letter in Above and Beyond that stated he wants to redeem himself. The letter was sent regardless of the final choices in Order Up!
Biography

If you stay in Beacontown in Season Two, Radar states that "You received a letter from someone named Aiden? Something about wanting to redeem himself."
Relationships
Allies
Maya

Gill

Enemies
Jesse

Lukas

The rest of Jesse's gang

Isa

Reginald

Appearances
The Order of the Stone
A Block and a Hard Place
Order Up!
Above and Beyond (mentioned)
Quotes
“ You just can't stand to see someone else in the spotlight, can you? You just can't let anyone else win! „ — Aiden to Jesse.
“ Hey! Who are you calling an idiot? „ — Aiden to Jesse ​ [ determinant ]
“ Shut up! „ — Aiden to Maya or Gill on multiple occasions ​ [ determinant ]
“ Someone ordered eggs? „ — Aiden to Jesse's gang, Isa, and Milo
“ I don't give a crap about what you think! „ — Aiden to Jesse ​ [ determinant ]
“ Back down and get out of my face, Reggie! „ — Aiden to Reginald
“ I killed you once and I can do it again! „ — Aiden to Jesse
“ Just DIE ALREADY! „ — Aiden to Jesse
“ This was supposed to be my world, and you've ruined it! „ — Aiden to Jesse
“ It just drives you crazy to see someone else succeed, doesn't it?! „ — Aiden to Jesse
“ Oh uh Hi Jesse I'm so sorry. So sorry to cause all that trouble. „ — Aiden to Jesse if he took him
“ Wa... wait! I... I surrender! I surrender! We can talk about this, right? „ — Aiden to Jesse
“ STOP IGNORING ME! „ — Aiden to Jesse if Jesse chooses to walk away ​ [ determinant ]
Trivia
If Jesse chooses to help Lukas instead of Isa, they have the option to knock out one of Aiden's teeth.
If Jesse allows Aiden to help defeat the wither storm in A Block and a Hard Place, Aiden, Maya, and Gill can be seen, cheering in the crowd.
See also

title: Legends:Air Chopper
text:
Type: Piglin structure
Hordes: Horde of the Bastion Horde of the Hunt Horde of the Spore
Health: 210
Identifier: badger:tower_piglin_spore_fan
An air chopper is a piglin structure that blows away non-piglin mobs in Minecraft Legends.
Spawning
Air choppers spawn in medium- or high-level piglin bases of all hordes near the Nether portal in quantities of one to two.
Behavior
When active, air choppers turn towards their target and create a gust of sporadic wind that knocks back and damages non-piglin mobs caught in the wind. The Firsts and piglins are immune to knockback, the latter getting healed by the wind.
Quotes
Name: Air Chopper, Category: Piglins, Unlocked by: Destroying an air chopper, Quote: "The piglins cobbled together this collection of blades to blow nether spores into every corner of the Overworld. The piglin medic atop the air chopper keeps the spore bin filled and the blades whirling.Those blades aren't just for spreading spores though. The piglins use the strong gusts created by air choppers to knock their enemies around on the battlefield and heal friendly piglins with fresh puffs of nether spores. It's best to approach an air chopper when it's between air bursts, as these crude machines need time to recharge after every blast."
Sounds

Spin, Piglin_spore_fan_attack_spin

Achievement: Not a Fan, In-game description: Destroy 10 piglinair choppersin a single world., Actual requirements (if different): —, Gamerscore earned: 100G, Trophy type (PS): Bronze
History
Minecraft Legends: Minecraft Live 2023, Air choppers are announced at Minecraft Live 2023.
Minecraft Legends: November 22, 2023, Through a social media post it is revealed that air choppers heal piglins.
Minecraft Legends: TU2, 1.18.14350, Added air choppers.

title: Earth:Albino Cow
text:
Health points: 10 HP

Player journal description Paleness is quite rare in animals.
The albino cow was a variant of the cow covered in white fur and skin, exclusively found in Minecraft Earth.
Behavior
Main article: Cow § Behavior
Similar to normal cows, albino cows wandered around, mooing and breathing occasionally. They avoided water, lava, and walking off cliffs high enough to cause fall damage. They fled for a few seconds when harmed.
Obtaining
Albino cows were obtainable from cow tappables, with a 4.6% chance and an uncommon rarity. They were also previously obtained as a reward in Season 5.
Drops
When killed, albino cows dropped:
0-2 Leather
1-3 Raw Beef ( Cooked Beef if killed while on fire)
Albino cows could be milked by using a bucket on it, yielding a milk bucket.

title: Legends:Allay Chest
text:
Type: Chest
An allay chest is a chest that can be opened by gather allays within Minecraft Legends.
Spawning
Allay chests can be found in any biome, often in high up or hard to access areas. Some biomes may generate more chests than others.
Usage
One gather allay is needed in order to open Allay chests. An arrangement of resources or more allays would be obtained once the chest is open. The resources found in an allay chest depends on the biome it spawns in.
Quotes
Name: Allay Chest, Category: Overworld, Unlocked by: Opening an allay chest, Quote: "Allay chests contain rare resources like wood, iron, coal, redstone and diamond. When the piglins arrived, the allays harvested the Overworld's resources to safely store them in these chests that only they can open. Not that the piglins haven't tried."
“ ALLAY CHESTS – Located randomly around the Overworld, allay chests will routinely build up rewards for your bravery. You may also get lucky and be gifted a new gather allay or build allay to use. „ — Minecraft Legends:
A Hero's Guide to Saving the Overworld
Sounds

Achievements
Achievement: Resource Reinforcements, In-game description: Open 5allay chestsin a single world., Actual requirements (if different): —, Gamerscore earned: 30G, Trophy type (PS): Bronze

Heals Allies is an unused powerful armor enchantment that is found exclusively as an enchantment on enchanted mobs in Minecraft Dungeons. Any armor enchanted with it grants the ability to heal nearby heroes and summons.

title: Story Mode:Amulet
text: Details Status: Intact Owner: Order of the Stone ​ [ formerly ] New Order of the Stone Obtained during: The Order of the Stone Below the Bedrock Found in: Order Hall ​ [ formerly ] Oasis First appearance: The Order of the Stone Latest appearance: Above and Beyond The amulet is both a symbol and a valuable navigational device used to locate and represent the current members of the Order of the Stone. History Season One At the beginning of The Order of the Stone, Soren can be seen holding the amulet in front of a crafting table, presumably having created it. Later, as Jesse's Gang leaves the treehouse, images of the amulet on banners are shown repeatedly during EnderCon, announcing the arrival of Gabriel. After entering Gabriel's fortress and being chased by the wither storm, Gabriel reveals the amulet and urges Jesse to take it for its useful functionality in finding the Order of the Stone members. Climbing up the stairs of the Temple of the Order of the Stone, which leads to the top floor, reveals a 6×5 map and a pedestal with an image of the amulet on a banner. Jesse recalls the story of Gabriel that by finding the temple will reveal the location of the members through the amulet. In turn, Axel asks Jesse to show it in which he raises it above his head. To everyone's disappointment, nothing happens. Jesse declared that they must be "something we're missing" and to "[l]ook around and see if you can find anything." The game progresses by giving the player autonomy of Jesse to walk towards the pedestal and place the amulet. By walking further down towards the switch, a contraption made using of five levers, shows a puzzle for the player to solve when interacted with. By completing it, turns the redstone lamp on, shooting a beam of white light towards the amulet's blue center which consequently shoot out red and green beams from the gems of the same colour onto the map. Jesse deducts that these beams must represent the members, with Soren missing, concluding that green is Magnus and red is Ellegaard. If the player chose to visit Redstonia with Olivia for the next episode, Assembly Required, Jesse would reveal the amulet to Calvin in a desperate attempt to convince him to organise a meeting with Ellegaard. Instead, this convinces Calvin to send the amulet directly to Ellegaard's private office via an item transportation system with Olivia reassuring Jesse that it "might not be a bad idea." After breaking into the Dome of Concentration, Jesse and Olivia find Ellegaard and alert her of a world-threatening wither storm which she immediately disregards their message. Jesse references the command block and the Order of the Stone which gave Ellegaard a realisation that it was those two who brought the amulet to her – where the red gem glowed vibrantly with an oscillating sound as she revealed it. Ellegaard recollected – when she received the amulet – that she was "terribly confused about where it had came from...", mentioning no note came attached to it with additional talk of not seeing "one of these in a long time." Finally, this convinces Ellegaard to believe in Jesse and Olivia's story, with Olivia asking if she can return the amulet (which Ellegard was unfazed about). During the attempt to unconventionally craft a command block, it was disrupted by the wither storm (an event caused by the amulet's location tracking unbeknownst to the characters). Season Two In Below the Bedrock, the amulet was used in Xara 's portal to transport Jesse and the others to the surface. They were transported in the ruined temple of the old Order of the Stone as the amulet has deep ties to that structure and its members. Properties Each color is represented by a different member with a visible glow increasing with intensity as a member is nearer to it. Color: Blue, Old Order of the Stone: Ivor, New Order of the Stone: Jesse Color: Green, Old Order of the Stone: Magnus, New Order of the Stone: Axel Color: Cyan, Old Order of the Stone: Gabriel, New Order of the Stone: Petra Color: Red, Old Order of the Stone: Ellegaard, New Order of the Stone: Olivia Color: White, Old Order of the Stone: Soren, New Order of the Stone: Lukas Inside the Order's Temple, the amulet can be placed inside a machine and when shone light through the center, colored beams come out onto a map representing the exact locations of the order members in the Overworld. It will not create beam for a member that is currently in other dimensions such as the Nether or the End. Sounds Appearances The Order of the Stone Assembly Required The Last Place You Look A Block and

title: Dungeons:Anchor
text:
Type: Melee Weapon
Rarity: COMMON / RARE
Power: 14
Speed: 1
Area: 14
Properties: Slow but powerful Absorbs some knockback Pulls in enemies
Enchantment: Gravity
Damage type: Melee
Soul information: Does not accept soul enchantments Does not grant soul collection
Runes
Variants: Encrusted Anchor
In-game description Those strong enough to wield the Anchor in battle follow the tradition of legendary seafaring warriors.
An anchor is a melee weapon that can be found within the Hidden Depths DLC of Minecraft Dungeons. Anchors can be used to absorb knockback and deal heavy damage to enemies. Its unique variant is called the encrusted anchor.
Obtaining
Missions
Abyssal Monument (DLC)
Radiant Ravine (DLC)
Tower
Merchants
Village Merchant
Gift Wrapper
Mystery Merchant
Luxury Merchant
Piglin Merchant
Ancients
Vengeful Mariner
Usage
Anchors possess extremely high power, low speed, and high range, and attack only with one massive strike. Because of its extremely slow attack speed, enchantments like Void Strike are highly effective to provide a huge damage boost.

Slow but powerful

title: Dungeons:Ancient Guardian
text: The Ancient Guardian is a boss in Minecraft Dungeons. It can be found in the Abyssal Monument mission and in The Tower. It is the sole boss of the Hidden Depths DLC. It is a gigantic, darkened, and purple variant of the normal Guardian. Health points: 13000 Behavior: Hostile Classification: Aquatic, Boss Attack strength: Melee Attack: 200 Aoe Ram Damage: 200 Aoe Spike Damage: 160 Beam Damage: 150 Spawn: Abyssal Monument Lore Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Dungeons that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk! The ancient guardian, as its name implies, is the oldest guardian of the Abyssal Monument. After the shattering of the Orb of Dominance, one of its shards landed within the Hidden Depths, corrupting the ancient guardian. With its newfound power, the ancient guardian was emboldened and empowered, turning from a brilliant white to a corrosive purple. The shard of the Orb of Dominance attempted to use it to spread its corruption, but it was bested by heroes journeying the ocean's depths. Spawning Abyssal Monument ( Boss ) Tower ( Boss ) In the Abyssal Monument, the ancient guardian has a unique spawning cinematic where it breaks through a wall in the structure. Behavior When the hero reaches the arena, the ancient guardian enters by bursting through a wall. After taking notable damage, it can swim away from the arena to avoid further damage. In addition, it has five attacks: Spike attack The ancient guardian can attack nearby heroes or summons by piercing them with its spikes, which creates a high-damage shockwave. Spike protection The ancient guardian sometimes pops out its spikes as a means of defense. Attacking it with a melee weapon results in the attacker taking some damage back in a similar manner to the Thorns enchantment. Laser attack The ancient guardian is capable of firing a yellow beam at its target, which behaves the same way as its weaker counter parts but deals more damage. After doing this attack, the ancient guardian retracts its spikes, and can be attacked without suffering thorns damage. Summon minions The ancient guardian can summon guardians to assist itself during battle. It usually summons about three guardians near a hero, but this attack sometimes summons an elder guardian instead. It cannot summon elder guardians if there already is one alive. Summon biomines The ancient guardian summons 3-5 biomines that explode shortly afterward, although it cancels this attack after becoming sufficiently damaged. When the biomines explode, they leave behind a temporary poison cloud that deals continuous damage to nearby heroes or summons. Biomine Behavior: Hostile Attack strength: 35 A biomine is a type of guardian-shaped mob that the ancient guardian summons every so often when coming back into the arena. Behavior When summoned, biomines explode shortly afterward, though it cancels this attack after becoming sufficiently damaged. When the biomines explode, they leave behind a temporary poison cloud that deals continuous damage to nearby heroes or summons. Fake ancient guardian Health points: 1 Classification: Aquatic Spawn: Summoned by the Ancient Guardian A fake ancient guardian is the version of the boss that is seen before the boss fight. Spawning Abyssal Monument Behavior Attacking the fake ancient guardian eventually results in it disappearing and dropping an item from the Abyssal Monument loot table. title: Legends:Ancient Hoglin
text:
Details
Species: Hoglin
Status: Unknown
Residence: Nether
Enemies: The Beast Piglins
First appearance: Minecraft: Portal Dash
Latest appearance: Minecraft Legends
The Ancient Hoglin is an oversized hoglin that appears in multiple cutscenes in Minecraft Legends.
Appearance
The Ancient Hoglin is considerably larger than typical hoglins, though possessing a similar shape. It has strikemaster-colored skin that is covered in sores, similar to warboars. Its back hairs are much longer and thicker, resembles blades of grass in shape, with visible markings on the face. Most notable of all is their tusks, which are much more numerous and growing in odd places, including on the nose.
Biography
The Ancient Hoglin could be seen being chased by The Beast during multiple cutscenes alongside several smaller, normal hoglins. It managed to escape from The Beast due to it being forced to go through a portal by The Seer.
History
Minecraft: Portal Dash: Minecraft Legends
Minecraft: Portal Dash: TU0, The Ancient Hoglin appears in the board gameMinecraft Portal Dash, before their formal introduction.: 1.17.24827, Minecraft Legendsis released, featuring the Ancient Hoglin in cutscenes.

title: Dungeons:Ancient Hunt
text: Part of: Other Dimensions Minimum difficulty: I Unlock predecessor: Complete any mission on Default V Raid captains: Always has zero captains Daily trials: Cannot have daily trials Ancient Hunt is the first other dimensions mission in Minecraft Dungeons. It is unlocked after completing any mission on Default V or higher, and completing Ancient Hunt unlocks the piglin merchant. Appearance and description The ranged weapon selection interface for configuring an ancient hunt. Ancient Hunt is a sequence of three sub-missions connected by two nether portals. It can be accessed by clicking on a nether portal found within Nether Room, a sub dungeon within Camp, as well as from the other dimensions tab on the mission select map. Ancient Hunt is the only mission in the game where gold can be obtained from mobs and chests. The ordinary mobs found within Ancient Hunt are more varied than in other locations, and never drop equipment when defeated. Hunt doors lead to ancient dungeons and Gold Room. There are always three sub-missions within Ancient Hunt, and the specific sub-missions that generate are random with no duplication. The three sub-missions can contain hunt doors, which lead to Gold Room or one of the various ancient dungeons. Gold Room contains piglin chests, which reward the hero with gold. Ancient dungeons contain ancients, a type of powerful mob which drops gilded gear upon being defeated. Starting Ancient Hunt requires sacrificing equipment, and optionally up to nine enchantment points which increase the spawn rate of ancient dungeons, and in turn decrease the spawn rate of Gold Rooms. The different types of melee weapons, ranged weapons, armor, and artifacts have different runes associated with them: Specific rune combinations are required to enable specific ancient dungeons to spawn within an Ancient Hunt, and at least one ancient dungeon type must be able to spawn before the mission can be started. Ten ancients require items received through DLCs for them to be summoned. If the items are required through other means, these ancients may be summoned without owning any DLCs. Frostwarden requires items from Creeping Winter, Windbeard requires items from Howling Peaks, The Unending and Unstoppable Tusk require items from Flames of the Nether, Vengeful Mariner and Abyssal Eye require items from Hidden Depths, and Scuttling Torment, The Swarm, and Watcher of The End require items from Echoing Void. title: Dungeons:Enchanted
text: In-Game light effect of all the enchanted mobs New renders of enchanted mobs. History & Bug sections The term enchanted refers to mobs that have a glowing, slightly larger appearance than that of a regular mob in Minecraft Dungeons. Enchanted mobs have higher damage resistance, more health and one or more additional properties that make them more of a threat to heroes compared to standard mobs. Modifying any enchanted mob will crash the game when clicked on. Appearance When enchanted, the texture of mobs will be darkened. The glowing spinning effect layer of enchanted mobs, weapons or armor works the same as the Charged Creeper in vanilla Minecraft. If mobs don't have "Shadow texture" in their file, their eyes won't glow. The glow effect texture of enchanted mobs consists of these three layers: T_SmoothClouds_512 — This texture is the first layer on the enchanted, with medium rotation speed. T_Clouds_64 — This texture is the second layer and most visible of all effects, with fastest rotation speed. Good64x64TilingNoiseHighFreq — This engine rendering texture make the original texture of enchanted mobs look darker and not affected by the above two glowing cloud textures. [ verify ] Enchanted mobs Enchanted mobs obtain their enchantments from an enchanter, the Arch-Illager, or spawn with enchantments (the latter requires the minimum difficulty at Default IV). Passive mobs such as sheep and neutral mobs can also appear with enchantments. When mobs are enchanted, their health is increased by 9 times. Enchanted powerful mobs increase their health by 25%. Enchanted mobs have an enchanted glowing appearance. The more enchantments they have, the brighter their appearance. For example, a mob with 3 enchantments appears brighter than a mob with 1 enchantment. The mob also becomes slightly larger when enchanted. Enchanted mobs are the hero's hunting target for the mystery merchant to unlock more slots in his stall and to change the way his stall looks. Enchanted mobs can overwhelm the hero much faster than powerful, ancient, or boss mobs, especially on Apocalypse Plus threat levels, where they spawn in large numbers, are much faster, and have less stagger durations. On such high difficulties, these mobs are not to be approached recklessly. Heads up Display when near an enchanted Chicken Jockey. The three enchantments attached are Common enchantments as indicated by. would be used for Powerful enchantments. The Heads-up display presents the name, enchantments, and enchantment rarity (Common or Powerful) possessed by nearby enchanted mobs. Heroes can use this as an indication that enchanted mobs are close and plan ahead on how to confront them. List of mob obtainable enchantments An enchanter can apply Double Damage or Fast Attack onto nearby mobs. Heroes using the enchanter's tome artifact can apply Double Damage, Fast Attack, or Quick onto nearby summoned mobs such as the iron golem, and onto heroes. Enchanted mobs can possess any of the following properties: Burning — Mob emits a circle that continuously damages heroes within it. Chilling — Mob emits a chilling aura that slows the hero’s movement and attack speed every 2 seconds. Deflect — Mobs deflect projectiles back at the hero, dealing damage over time. Double Damage — Mobs deal 2 times as much damage to the hero. Similar to the Strength potion. Electrified — Mob emits an electrical burst every 5 seconds, dealing major damage to nearby heroes. Fast Attack — Mob attack speed increases. Fire Aspect — Mob attacks burns the hero for 3 seconds. Fire Trail — Mob leaves a trail of purple fire as it moves. Freezing — Mob slows the hero's attack speed after hitting them. Frenzied — Mob attack speed increases by 50% when below half its maximum health. Gravity Pulse — Mob emits a pulse that pulls in nearby heroes every 10 seconds. Heal Allies — Mob receiving damage heals other nearby mobs. Poison Cloud — Mob attacks create a poison cloud identical to those that witches throw wherever they land. Protection — Mob receives 50% less damage. Quick — Mobs have higher movement speed. Similar to Swiftness Radiance — Mob summons an aura that heals it and all allies when attacking, ranged mobs have Radiance Shot instead. Regeneration — Mobs regenerate health within seconds. Rush — Mobs gain increased movement speed for a short duration after receiving damage. Thorns — 12.5% of damage received applies onto the attacker. Weakening — Mob inflicts Weakened for 5 seconds, reducing the hero’s attack damage. Ranged mobs only Accelerate - Mob shooting speed increases for a second after hitting the hero. Bonus Shot — Ranged mobs fires an additional shot when shooting. Chain Reaction — Mobs projectiles splits into 5 more projectiles on impact. Critical Hit — Mob inflicts three times as much damage when they attack. Gravity — Projectiles pull in the hero and their allies to the impact point

title: Story Mode:Old Builders
text:
Status: Intact
Founded: Prior to Season One
Current members: Hadrian Harper Mevia Otto
The Old Builders was the name given to a group of legendary architects in Minecraft: Story Mode. While the name was hated by the Old Builders themselves, it was primarily used by people outside their group regardless of their preferences. The group consisted of Hadrian, Mevia, Otto, and Harper.
History

Instructions: The events that took place in the game.
The Old Builders became mythologized sometime before the events in the game. They established the Builder Games in the portal network itself, where people were able to participate. Sometime later, the power over the competitors corrupted Hadrian and Mevia, the games were no longer voluntary; they forced the competitors who lost the games to mine for quartz in the Nether until they could partake the games again. It was well known among both the competitors and the gladiators that they hated being called the Old Builders. Despite this dislike of the term, they, or just Harper, were not against using it in relation to themselves.
Besides the games, the Old Builders also made many remarkable objects including but not limited to the redstone heart, portal atlas, and many countless portal keys.
The Old Builders may have been aware of Soren the Architect of the Order of the Stone as Harper, and Hadrian each possessed at least one of his authored books in their personal libraries, such as The Redstone Heart, and A Man Forever Voyaging.
Sometime before the events of A Portal to Mystery, the Old Builders kicked Cassie Rose to a world with two moons.
Members
Hadrian
Harper
Mevia
Otto
Appearances
Access Denied
A Journey's End?
Quotes
“ That glow, that enchantment is the work of a very old group of builders. A group so old that they existed before the Order of the Stone! „ — Ivor explaining the history of the enchanted flint and steel.

title: Dungeons:Ancient Offering
text: Find out the official name in the game or the game files of the ancient offerings. An ancient offering is an item offered before starting the mission Ancient Hunt. Illageralt runes attached to items indicate what ancients that can attract. Interaction Each ancient has a combination of Illageralt runes that it responds to. Rune requirements are satisfied by offering up a set of equipment that has the required runes attached. Only one ranged weapon, one melee weapon, one armor and/or one artifact may be offered at a time. Multiple ancients can be called by a single overall offer. For example, The Seeking Flame ( ) can be called by offering up a Soul Bow ( ) and a Soul Knife ( ). The Unstoppable Tusk ( ) can be added to Ancient Hunt by additionally offering up a Splendid Robe ( ). title: Dungeons:Andesite Hills
text:
This article documents a feature that has been officially scrapped.
Minecraft Dungeons has been discontinued, and as such will not be added to the game in the foreseeable future.
Part of: Mainland
Minimum difficulty: I
Unlock predecessor: Creeper Woods
Andesite Hills is an unused mission in Minecraft Dungeons. It would have been unlocked after the completion of Creeper Woods, and completing Andesite Hills would not have unlocked any missions.
Appearance and description
Andesite Hills would have been another forest-themed mission, but was cut in favor of Soggy Swamp.
Locations

Andesite Hills has no objectives in the files.

Andesite Hills has no voiceovers associated with it in the files.
Mobs and entities

It is unknown what mobs and entities would have spawned in Andesite Hills.
Items

It is unknown what items would have been dropped in Andesite Hills.

Andesite Hills does not have any achievements associated with it, as it is unused.

Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Andesite Hills.

title: Dungeons:Anima Conduit
text:
For the consumable, see MCD:Conduit.
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Melee Weapon Ranged Weapon
Property: +1 soul gathering
Soul gear only: Yes
In-game description Each soul you absorb grants a small amount of health.
Anima Conduit is an enchantment that can be found within the various weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Anima Conduit has a chance to appear in any enchantment slot on a soul melee or ranged weapon, which includes the following weapons:
Melee weapons
Soul Knife
Eternal Knife
Truthseeker
Soul Fists
Soul Scythe
Frost Scythe
Jailor's Scythe
Skull Scythe
Moon Daggers
Ranged weapons
Soul Bow
Bow of Lost Souls
Nocturnal Bow
Shivering Bow
Soul Crossbow
Feral Soul Crossbow
Voidcaller
Soul Hunter Crossbow
Enchantments on soul items can be swapped out for Anima Conduit by rerolling them at the enchantsmith.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect (Melee): 2% of max health per soul, Effect (Ranged): 4% of max health per soul, Cost: 1 enchantment point, Cost (Gilded): 2 enchantment points
Tier: II, Effect (Melee): 4% of max health per soul, Effect (Ranged): 6% of max health per soul, Cost: 2 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 3 enchantment points
Tier: III, Effect (Melee): 6% of max health per soul, Effect (Ranged): 8% of max health per soul, Cost: 3 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 4 enchantment points
Mobs
Mobs can never be enchanted with Anima Conduit.
Stacking
Anima Conduit is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Usage
A ranged weapon with Anima Conduit can be used for passive healing if the hero mainly uses melee weapons or artifacts to damage mobs. It heals the hero a small percent of their max health upon absorbing a soul. The amount of health granted increases with each tier. Anima Conduit can appear exclusively on weapons that have soul gathering as one of their properties.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added anima conduit.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.3.2.0, Removed soul amount multiplier on soul collection.
Minecraft Dungeons: Increased effect from 1/2/3% to 2/4/6% per visual soul collected.
Minecraft Dungeons: Now triggers on any kill source.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.10.2.0, Now compatible with ranged weapons.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.16.2.0, Increased effect on range weapons from 2/4/6% to 4/6/8% per visual soul collected
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Anima Conduit" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.
Gallery

Screenshot of anima conduit.
Anima Conduit's shine pattern.
