title: Story Mode:Announcer
text:
While a name exists in the game code, it has not been used officially by Mojang or Telltale Games.
Details
Gender: Female
Species: Human
Status: Alive
Occupation: Announcer at the EnderCon Building Competition
Residence: EnderCon's host town
First appearance: The Order of the Stone
Latest appearance: A Block and a Hard Place
Actor: Erin Yvette
The announcer is a character at the EnderCon Building Competition, who also serves as the event's registrar.
Appearance
The announcer is a dark-skinned woman with green eyes and red lips. She dresses formally, wearing a dark suit and a yellow tie along with large spectacles. She also wears a small yellow and green badge.
Personality
The announcer initially seems to be a patient and calm person. She is later revealed to be a bit flamboyant when she is doing the main part of her job.
Biography

Appearances
The Order of the Stone
The Last Place You Look [ verify ]
A Block and a Hard Place
Quotes
“ Building...starts...now! „ — The EnderCon announcer at the beginning of the building contest.
“ Announcer: Name, please?
Axel: Axel, and a how do you do?
Announcer: No, your team name. „ — The announcer to Axel
“ Ladies and gentlemen... Welcome to the EnderCon Building Competition. „ — The announcer to all competitors
“ The winners of this year's competition will have their build featured at EnderCon - the winners will also meet in person, Gabriel the Warrior. „ — The announcer to all competitors
Trivia
The announcer is the name she was given in the staff roll and in the game files. This is similar to the narrator.
If Jesse does not choose a team name, the announcer calls the team "No-names."

title: Dungeons:Difficulty
text: Achievements relating to difficulty. Include Artifacts. Fill information. The Difficulty Mode selection screen. The term difficulty refers to a mechanic in Minecraft Dungeons that influences the balance by affecting the challenge of gameplay. Difficulty modes There are three sequential difficulty modes. Default Adventure Apocalypse Defeating the Arch-Illager atop Obsidian Pinnacle and completing it on a difficulty setting opens the next map. On higher difficulty modes, mobs are harder to defeat, more numerous, and enchanted mobs occur more often with new enchantments. However, equipment of higher power is rewarded to heroes as they traverse higher difficulties, alongside with new gear and artifacts drops and increased experience from mobs. Heroes can switch between unlocked difficulty modes at any time from the Select Mission Map, retaining all their equipment, and can re-play accessible missions at lower difficulty modes. The three difficulty modes are depicted by a skull icon that appears more menacing at higher difficulty. They also have their own color, mob, and sound cue to represent the difficulty in the Difficulty Mode selection screen, with Default using blue (a chicken jockey mob and a chicken sound), Adventure with pink (a necromancer mob and a vindicator sound), and Apocalypse with red (a redstone golem mob and a ghast scream sound). Difficulty exclusive equipment Main articles: Weapons, Armor, Artifacts, and Drops Difficulty influences the equipment drop pool for missions. As the difficulty increases, some equipment that was already available in default appear in more locations and new equipment that cannot be obtained in Default become available. At Adventure difficulty, the following equipment becomes available: Soul Scythe Frost Scythe Jailor's Scythe Soul Bow Bow of Lost Souls Nocturnal Bow Phantom Armor Frost Bite Plate Armor Full Metal Armor Champion's Armor Hero's Armor Squid Armor Glow Squid Armor Gong of Weakening Love Medallion Tome of Duplication At Apocalypse difficulty, the following equipment becomes available: Dark Armor Titan's Shroud Golem Kit Vexing Chant Threat levels Reason: Update the recommended power levels for difficulties. The threat level selection slider for Apocalypse difficulty. The higher the threat level, the higher the minimum and maximum power of weapons, armors, and artifacts that can drop during and after the mission. The number of enemies and the damage they inflict in a mission also increase. For each threat level, a recommended power level is assigned to provide guidance to heroes. When opening the Threat Level Panel for a mission, the game positions the slider at a recommended threat level for the hero based on the mission's recommended power and the heroes power. This practice is known as dynamic game difficulty balancing. In a multiplayer game, the game instead uses the average power of all heroes present to determine a recommended threat level. Minimum Threat Level for Obsidian Pinnacle at Adventure Difficulty is shown to be Adventure IV. Players cannot enter the mission at a lower Threat Level than the minimum. Some missions have a set minimum threat level. The only mission with no adjustable threat level is Squid Coast, the tutorial mission, which is always set at Default I and is inaccessible when set to Adventure or Apocalypse difficulty modes. Difficulty: Threat level, Default: I, Adventure: II, Apocalypse: III, IV, V, VI, VII, I, II, III, IV, V, VI, VII, I, II, III, IV, V, VI, VII Difficulty: Recommended power, Default: 1, Adventure: 4, Apocalypse: 10, 16, 22, 27, 33, 33, 39, 45, 51, 56, 62, 68, 68, 74, 80, 86, 91, 97, 103 Difficulty: Mob Health Multiplier, Default: 0.096x, Adventure: 0.1216x, Apocalypse: 0.1536x, 0.1952x, 0.2492x, 0.3424x, 0.4544x, 0.4544x, 0.5744x, 0.7264x, 0.92x, 1.2304x, 1.6416x, 2.1888x, 2.1888x, 2.7696x, 3.5056x, 4.4368x, 5.9584x, 7.7944x, 10.6576x Difficulty: Mob Damage Multiplier, Default: 0.12x, Adventure: 0.152x, Apocalypse: 0.192x, 0.244x, 0.324x, 0.428x, 0.568x, 0.568x, 0.718x, 0.908x, 1.15x, 1.538x, 2.052x, 2.736x, 2.736x, 3.462x, 4.382x, 5.546x, 7.448x, 9.968x, 13.322x Apocalypse Plus Apocalypse Plus is an extension upon the existing Apocalypse difficulty mode that aims to be, especially at its most difficult state, an "impossibly hard" challenge for players and encourage the use of "highly optimized" equipment setups. Consisting of 25 threat levels appended upon Apocalypse VII, sharing the threat level slider, Apocalypse Plus applies stat enhancing modifiers to mobs and permit equipment classified as rare to drop more frequently, in addition to increasing the maximum equipment power obtainable. Heroes unlock the first three Apocalypse Plus threat levels after completing any mission at Apocalypse VII. To gain access to the next triplet of threat levels, the hero must complete a number of boss missions at the higher end of the accessible triplet. The number of boss missions required increases, up to a maximum of 7, with each threat level Triplet. Each boss counts only once per threat level. Boss mission A boss mission is a type of mission that progresses Apocalypse Plus when completed. Not all boss missions include bosses. There are

title: Dungeons:Arcade
text: Author(s): Mojang Studios Play Mechanix Platform(s): Arcade Written in: Unknown Latest version: N/A Release date: Q3 2021 Rating(s): N/A Size: N/A License: N/A Source available: N/A Key art by Mojang Studios Minecraft Dungeons Arcade is an arcade adaptation of Minecraft Dungeons announced on March 5, 2021. The game is developed by Mojang Studios in conjunction with Play Mechanix and it was released in the third quarter of 2021. Gameplay The game offers two different modes of single and multiplayer. In multiplayer, it supports up to four players and features various weapons, armors, heroes, and mobs. Players explore various missions and defeat hostile mobs throughout their play. The game uses a side-on perspective instead of an isometric perspective as in the main game. The hero can also use physical collectible cards obtained at the beginning of every game to select their gear as well as a pet and a hero. There are mobs exclusive to the arcade version such as the obsidian monstrosity, mages, and variants of jungle zombies and frozen zombies. After starting the game, the game dispenses a card and shows players five empty slots, one for each type of card. One card of each type can be scanned in by each player, any slots left empty when moving on are assigned a random item. Next, players select a mission and the game begins. After a small cutscene, the player’s hero spawns on the left of the screen with only their armor equipped. There are three actions, melee, range, and dodge, and a joystick to aim and move around with. Heroes move right across the map fighting mobs in stages using a basic sword and bow. Mobs can drop emeralds upon defeat which count toward filling up meters for weapons, armor and pet cards; these items are activated when enough emeralds are collected for each separate item. When the melee weapon is ready it is equipped automatically, and a fifteen second countdown starts upon the weapon's first use. Every melee card gives a 150% damage boost over the basic sword, however different weapon choices can have vastly different areas of effect. Ranged weapons vary more, generally having no cooldown but instead having several arrows to use with different areas of effect upon impact or other buffs. Pets are NPCs with smaller amounts of health than heroes, which aid them in attacking mobs. Pets also continue to fight after the hero is defeated. Armor reduces damage taken and prevents the hero from taking damage to their base HP, it is equipped automatically when its gem meter is full and lasts until it is broken. As heroes travel across the map, waves of mobs become larger and introduce more difficult mobs to fight. Each mission has a short cutscene around the half-way point. At the end of each mission, heroes must defeat a boss in order to beat the mission. Upon beating two missions in a row on the same play-through, players unlock a bonus boss fight mission against the Ancient Guardian. Appearance The game has a large screen outlined with a lava texture. Right below that is the hinge that displays five cards in front of a orange background with the Play Mechanix logo on the right. The top of the cabinet has the logo for Minecraft Dungeons Arcade with four Minecraft Dungeons heroes above it. The console has four control panels, displaying characters right above each one. Each panel is colored indicating the player(s), which are blue, red, green, and yellow respectively. Below the console is where the cards are ejected from the system - the player may scan these cards after each round to play with a desired hero, item or pet. Images The four player arcade cabinet. The two player arcade cabinet. Localised Chinese cabinet. Additions The following is included within the arcade: Armor Added Ender Armor Includes Archer's Armor Battle Robe Black Wolf Armor Beenest Armor Ember Robe Fox Armor Frost Bite Full Metal Armor Ghost Kindler Glow Squid Armor Goat Gear Grim Armor Hero's Armor Highland Armor Hungry Horror Living Vines Armor Nimble Turtle Armor Ocelot Armor Phantom Armor Renegade Armor Souldancer Robe Spider Armor Squid Armor Stalwart Armor Sturdy Shulker Armor The Troubadour Titan's Shroud Unstable Robes Wither Armor Wolf Armor Artifacts Includes Blast Fungus Corrupted Beacon Eye of the Guardian Lightning Rod Satchel of Elements Heroes Added Ebo Elan Frisk Ivy Meadow Includes Archeologist Adriene Baako Dani Darian Elaine Fuego Kelptomaniac Monumental Morris Sam Sven Valorie Violet Wargen Missions Includes Squid Coast Desert Temple Dingy Jungle Creeper Woods Cacti Canyon Frozen Fjord Hidden Depths Fiery Forge Soggy Swamp Obsidian Pinnacle Pumpkin Pastures "Level 12 Coming Soon" "Level 13 Coming Soon" Entities Added Cat ( Summonable ) Chicken ( Summonable ) Chicken Jockey ( Summonable ) Baby Turtle ( Summonable ) Giant Cave Spider

title: Dungeons:Arch-Illager
text: Note: Remove the background noise of the exclusive sounds. See § Opening Cinematic Health points: 3200 Behavior: Hostile Classification: Enemies Mob category: Illager, Boss Attack strength: Projectile: 45 Melee Attack: 75 Spawn: Obsidian Pinnacle The Arch-Illager, also known by his birth name Archie, is one of the two main antagonists (alongside the Orb of Dominance ) of the game. He is the founder and leader of the Illager Empire in the game, and is a proxy for the Orb of Dominance to carry out its goals. Spawning Obsidian Pinnacle Behavior The Arch-Illager has seven behaviors. If a hero or summon comes too close to him, he begins repeatedly teleporting around the arena and firing blasts after each teleport. His health pool is the second lowest of any boss in the game, although this is mitigated by the deadly Heart of Ender battle that takes place after his health is depleted. Additionally, the Arch-Illager, when he isn't a boss, flies around summoning bosses and battles to try and thwart the hero's efforts to stop him. Basic attack The Arch-Illager fires three purple energy blasts in a horizontal swath within 2500 units (approximately 25 blocks) of a hero or summon. Deals a small amount of damage if it hits (45 per energy blast in code). Used extremely often. Each energy blast has an angle span of 20 degrees, a 0.5-second delay, and will automatically disappear after 20 seconds. Melee attack The Arch-Illager uses a melee attack to pushback heroes within 220 units (approximately 2.2 blocks), dealing a small amount of damage (75 in code) and knockback heroes. As the Arch-Illager teleports away when the hero gets close, and this behavior also uses the "Basic attack" animation, this behavior is very rare and difficult to trigger. Teleport When a hero comes within 300 units (approximately 3 blocks) of the Arch-Illager, the Arch-Illager teleports 1350 units (approximately 13.5 blocks) in a random area around the hero to avoid taking much damage. Similarly to the enderman, the arch-illager can also hide in teleportation and not reappear instantly.Teleport In and Out both have a duration of 0.9 seconds. Enhance The Arch-Illager can enchant up to 5 hostile mobs within 1100 units (approximately 11 blocks) of himself, similar to the enchanter. The enchantment has 0.9 seconds delay and 2 seconds attack rate. Enchanted mobs will only take 0.333x damage and can deal 1.75x damage, and their speed will also be increased to 2x. However, if the Arch-Illager teleports, the enchanting is broken off the mob. Summon circle Since the Arch-Illager is not much of a warrior, he is capable of summoning a group of Illagers to assist him, usually a circle of ten to twenty vindicators around the hero. This skill has a 28-second cooldown. Summon line The Arch-Illager can also summon a line of twenty pillagers through the center of the arena. This skill has a 28-second cooldown. Summon single The Arch-Illager rarely summons a single redstone golem. This mob is not powerful unlike other redstone golems, and unlike the Arch-Illager's other summoned mobs, it does not die once he is defeated. While this attack is used sparingly, it is his most powerful attack. This skill has a 28-second cooldown. Defeated in a whirlwind Once the Arch-Illager has taken enough damage, his staff slips out of his hands, sucks him in, and absorbs him while transforming into the Heart of Ender. Appearance In the opening cinematic, Archie has ragged and torn clothes and is holding a torch. When he is crowned as the Arch-Illager, he has blue robes that are similar to the evocation robe with a large chain with a ruby on it and a crown with a sapphire and a ruby on it. He also carries a staff to hold the Orb of Dominance. The Arch-Illager is also the only illager to have a horizontal head and a smaller height. In the arena, the Arch-Illager will appear in the sky above the arena in its "ghost" form and then summon enemies. In "ghost" form, its clothes will glow, and its body will have a purple enchantment glow and special effects. In the arcade edition, the top of the Arch-Illager's crown lacks a texture, only appearing black. Personality As being an illager. Archie seems to have been quite cowardly before he was corrupted by the Orb of Dominance. He often would not run into the face of danger. However, in certain situations, he has the potential to be a strong warrior. He also is shown to have violent tendencies in certain situations, such as when Thord causes him to get exiled, and when Salah causes Smacker to get him thrown out of the village. At one point, he briefly considers executing Walda for her part in Thord’s betrayal. Biography Instructions: Explain more of Rise, including

title: Dungeons:Arch Haven
text: Part of: Mainland Minimum difficulty: II Unlock predecessor: Mystery Ship scroll Pumpkin Pastures Raid captains: Never has captains Daily trials: Can have daily trials A Hidden Stash What's this? You've stumbled upon an outpost used by the Arch-Illager and his wretched band of ill willing bootlickers! Surely stealing his secret plans and grabbing his ill-gotten loot will set him back considerably! Arch Haven or Arch-Haven is the second mainland secret mission in Minecraft Dungeons. It is unlocked after collecting the scroll found in Mystery Ship and completing Pumpkin Pastures. Completing Arch Haven does not unlock any missions. Appearance and description “ ARCH-HAVEN – Once you've passed through Pumpkin Pastures, you can visit Arch-Haven - a coastal settlement at the edge of the pastures. You will begin on a ship and will need to battle your way across the beach, where the Illagers have made themselves quite at home. You must find a series of spell books situation on stole pinths throughout. There is also a cave to explore before you head back to the ship and sail away to safety. „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons: A Handbook for Heroes Arch Haven is a coastal archipelago located in the ocean south-east of Pumpkin Pastures. It contains a harbor with a beach house, a small village with three spellbooks, and a cave with a long wooden boardwalk. It features ships, acacia huts, campfires, banners, barrels, market stalls, small islands with stone ruins, and equipment stolen from farmers of Pumpkin Pastures by the Illagers. The spawn area of the mission takes place on a small ship, the same ship found in Pumpkin Pastures after having been used to travel to Arch Haven by the heroes. After crossing the beach and reaching the harbor, a certain amount of mobs must be defeated for the gate blocking the harbor to be opened. North-east of the harbor, a village can be reached that was once a thriving trading hub before the Illagers invaded. In this village, three spellbooks must be collected from atop three stone plinths in order to proceed further north towards the entrance of Arch Haven Cave. Upon exiting Arch Haven Cave, an ambush of mobs must be defeated to lower the gate that prevented access to a large ship. The ship can be used to sail away to safety, ending the mission. title: Dungeons:Arena Battle
text: An arena battle, also known as an ambush for short, is a battle between mobs and heroes. There are distinct differences between these and boss battles. Locations Arena battles can be found in every mission in the game other than???. Some missions have ambushes which spawn powerful mobs: Creeper Woods (Only with Echoing Void DLC ) Woodland Mansion Woodland Prison Soggy Swamp (Only with Echoing Void DLC ) Spider Cave Pumpkin Pastures Redstone Mines Cacti Canyon (Only with Echoing Void DLC ) Desert Temple (Only with Echoing Void DLC ) Highblock Halls (Only with Echoing Void DLC ) Frosted Fjord Lost Settlement Gauntlet of Gales Colossal Rampart Basalt Deltas Nether Fortress Crimson Forest Coral Rise Abyssal Monument Radiant Ravine The Stronghold End Wilds Broken Citadel Tower Treetop Tangle Treetop Tangle is the only mission to have an ambush which spawns a boss mob. Behavior Arena battles operate differently from boss fights, where arena battles spawn a lot more weaker mobs, then sometimes a powerful mob or a boss. The battle format also differs from boss fights, where it usually spawns in waves, such as the one in Pumpkin Pastures. The battles usually start out with several weak mobs. Over time, the waves of mobs get stronger until the battle ends, or when the boss appears. No matter what, battles always spawn mobs from the mission it takes place in. There are also similarities between arena battles and boss fights. They are both triggered by certain events like heroes entering an arena like the one in Redstone Mines, or by pressing a trigger like the bell in Pumpkin Pastures. Some other triggers include buttons, levers, and pillars. There is a chance for these ambushes to have mini bosses. There are nine missions where ambushes spawn two minibosses: the Woodland Mansion, the Pumpkin Pastures (Only with Echoing Void DLC ), the Desert Temple (Only with Echoing Void DLC ), the Basalt Deltas, the Crimson Forest, the Abyssal Monument, The Stronghold, the End Wilds and the Broken Citadel. So far, it isn't possible to have arena battles that spawn exactly three bosses, but in the Coral Rise there is an arena battle that spawns four of them. Another similarity between arena battles and boss fights is that they have their own special music. They can also both be started by the Arch-Illager, the Jungle Abomination, the Wretched Wraith, the Tempest Golem, the Ancient Guardian and the Vengeful Heart of Ender. Each of them have their own summoning animation. The Arch-Illager teleports in, summons an ambush, and then teleports out. The jungle abomination, wretched wraith, tempest golem and ancient guardian just roar to summon an ambush, producing their own effects while doing so. If the battles are a part of the mission's objectives, it is announced by the game, whereas if the battle is not part of the mission's objectives, it won't be announced. Music Arena music list If the player escapes the arena while the music is playing, the music continues until another ambush or a boss fight is cleared. Mainland and Hero Pass DLCs arena1 (Arena Music for the Creeper Woods, Creepy Crypt, Pumpkin Pastures, Arch Haven, Soggy Swamp, Soggy Cave, Redstone Mines, Cacti Canyon, Lower Temple, Obsidian Pinnacle, all Jungle Awakens missions except Treetop Tangle, and all Creeping Winter missions) arena2 (Arena Music for the Squid Coast, Desert Temple, Fiery Forge, Highblock Halls, and the Underhalls) Invocation (Arena Music for Woodland Mansion and Woodland Prison Ancient Hunts) Menta Menardi (Arena Music for Spider Cave Ancient Hunt) Jungle Awakens Summit (Arena Music for Treetop Tangle) Howling Peaks Summit (Arena Music for Windswept Peaks) Ascension (Alternate Arena Music for Windswept Peaks) Squall (Arena Music for Colossal Rampart) Flurry (Alternate Arena Music for Colossal Rampart) Rush (Arena Music for Gale Sanctum) Scud (Arena Music for Gauntlet of Gales) Flames of the Nether Routers (Arena Music for Nether Wastes, Nether Fortress and Gold Rooms in Ancient Hunts) Metaluna (Arena Music for Warped Forest and Soul Sand Valley) Torn Acinder (Arena Music for Basalt Deltas) Kermetic (Arena Music for Crimson Forest) Hidden Depths Cnidoblast (Arena Music for Coral Rise) Nematocyst (Alternate Arena Music for Coral Rise) Truched (Arena Music for Radiant Ravine) Klyka (Alternate Arena Music for Radiant Ravine) Swashes (Arena Music for Abyssal Monument) Pit Fight (Alternate Arena Music for Abyssal Monument) Echoing Void Drawn (Arena Music for The Stronghold) Closer (Alternate Arena Music for The Stronghold) Nothingness (Arena Music for End Wilds) Void (Alternate Arena Music for End Wilds) Shattered (Arena Music for Broken Citadel) Apart (Alternate Arena Music for Broken Citadel) The Tower Tower Arena (Arena Music for The Tower) Tower Boss (Arena Music for The Tower boss floors) Arena music Mainland and Hero DLC Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Arena 1, bgm_env_pupa2DArena Arena 1 and 2 end, bgm_env_generic2DArenaEnd Arena 2, bgm_env_deteBoss Spider Cave

title: Dungeons:Armor
text: The Emerald Gear and its unique variants, Opulent Armor and Gilded Glory, are examples of armors sets from Minecraft Dungeons. The term armor refers to the category of equippable items in Minecraft Dungeons that increase the hero's health, apply additional properties, and appear graphically on the hero once equipped. Armor can be obtained opening chests, defeating mobs, exchanging Emeralds with village merchant, luxury merchant, mystery merchant, piglin merchant, and from Gifts provided by heroes made by other players through the gift wrapper. Armor can have up to three player selected enchantments applied to apply additional properties. The mystery armor can have any combination of Mainland armor properties along with armor properties from DLC content if owned thus permitting unique combinations that otherwise cannot be provided by other armors. The obtainable Power level for armor has a maximum of 263. Gilded armor can be obtained by completing the Tower with a maximum power of 263 or by completing an Ancient Hunt and from the piglin merchant with a maximum power of 251. Each armor in the game has a base health value of 200. There are 71 armors, plus two unused armors. 41 of these armors are of unique rarity, seven of which are exclusive to seasonal events. title: Dungeons:Mountaineer
text:
Health points: Tier 1: 135 Tier 2: 380 Tier 3: 550
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Illager
Attack strength: Tier 1: 80 Tier 2: 90 Tier 3: 100
Equipped models: Mountaineer Axe (reskinned Axe )
A mountaineer is an illager that spawns in various locations in Minecraft Dungeons. Mountaineers have three variants, identifiable by their coat color and weapon type; the first variant wears a brown coat, the second wears gray with a green backpack, and the third one wears red with leather boots and shoulder pads.
Spawning
Missions

Lost Settlement

Gale Sanctum
Gauntlet of Gales
Colossal Rampart
End Wilds
Broken Citadel
Tower

Windbeard
Behavior
Mountaineer attack
Attack
Mountaineers behave similarly to the old AI of vindicators before 1.7.2.0, running up to the hero and attacking them via melee, with their axes. They have a delay and cannot move when they are attacking.
Brace
Mountaineer brace
Mountaineers place their axe into the ground when in contact with the wind in order to not be blown away.

Main article: Achievement Achievement: Whirlwind Warrior, In-game description: Using thetempest knife, defeat 10mountaineermobs within 10 seconds., Actual requirements (if different): Kill 10 mountaineers orarmored mountaineerswithin 10 seconds using only thetempest knife.Resolute tempest knifeorChill gale knifedoes not count., Gamerscore earned: 10G, Trophy type (PS): Bronze
Data value
In the game files, tier 1 mountaineers are referred to internally by the name Mountaineer or MountaineerVariant0.
In the game files, tier 2 mountaineers are referred to internally by the name MountaineerVariant1.
In the game files, tier 3 mountaineers are referred to internally by the name MountaineerVariant2.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: October 3, 2020, Howling Peaks announced duringMinecraft Live 2020, with the release date set to be somewhere in December 2020.
Minecraft Dungeons: October 19, 2020, A design of the third mountaineer tier that later goes unused in the game is shown.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.5.0.0, Added the textures and sound files for mountaineers and mountaineer axes, currently unused.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.7.2.0, Added mountaineers.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.10.2.0, Now appears within theFrosted Fjordand theLost Settlement.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Mountaineer" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.
Trivia
Except for when stunned, mountaineers are almost always squinting or half-closing their eyes.
In the artwork for the Howling Peaks DLC, mountaineers can be seen climbing the peaks while using axes.
Despite behaving like vindicators, mountaineers are not a vindicator variant.
Mountaineers lack the emission texture, so the buttons on tier 1 and 2 mountaineers do not glow, unlike tier 1 and 2 vindicators. Therefore, enchanted mountaineers do not have glowing eyes or special textures.

title: Dungeons:Piglin
text:
Health points: Tier 1:50 Tier 2:150 Tier 3:250
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Piglin
Attack strength: Tier 1:75 Tier 2:100 Tier 3:120
Equipped models: Tier 1: Golden Sword (reskinned Sword ) Tier 2: Golden Axe (reskinned Axe ) Tier 3: Firebrand
A piglin is a pig-like hostile mob that can be found within the Flames of the Nether DLC of Minecraft Dungeons. Piglins come in three variants; the first resembles a normal piglin, the second resembles a piglin brute, while the third is more heavily armored and draped in red clothing.
Spawning
Nether Wastes

Nether Fortress
Warped Forest
Crimson Forest
Soul Sand Valley
Ancient Hunt
Arena Battle
Tower
Behavior
Unlike their original game counterparts, piglins and wither skeletons don't attack each other nor do they hunt hoglins.
The tier 3 armored piglin cannot be stunned by normal attack strength except Shock Powder such as certain artifacts and any weapon with the stunning enchantment.
Melee attack
Piglins run up to the hero to try and attack them head-on with their gold swords.

title: Dungeons:Pillager
text: For the hostile mob in Minecraft, see Pillager. For the raid captain, see MCD:Pillager Raid Captain. Health points: Tier 1 ： 120 Tier 2 ： 350 Tier 3 ：480 Behavior: Hostile Classification: Illager Attack strength: Tier 1: 60 Tier 2: Default damage x 1.1 Tier 3: Default damage x 1.2 Equipped models: Crossbow A pillager is a crossbow -armed hostile illager that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. Pillagers come in three variants; the first, most common variant, has the basic outfit also seen in the base game, with a maroon outfit, an iron belt buckle and shoulder pads, blue pants and brown boots, the second variant has a gold-trimmed helmet, belt buckle and shoulder pads, beige pants, a purple outfit and black boots, and the third tier variant has a diamond-trimmed helmet, belt buckle and shoulder pads, black pants, an orange outfit, and blue boots. In the Arcade version, the pillager has only one variant, utilizing the second variant of the pillager from the original Dungeons game, though wielding its crossbow in its left hand. Spawning Cacti Canyon Redstone Mines Fiery Forge Highblock Halls Underhalls Obsidian Pinnacle Frosted Fjord Lone Fortress Lost Settlement Windswept Peaks Gale Sanctum Gauntlet of Gales Colossal Rampart End Wilds Broken Citadel Daily Trial Tower Treetop Tangle Mobs Raid Captain Vigilant Scoundrel Behavior Pillagers always have the Piercing enchantment on their crossbows. Walk Pillager walking. Pillagers move away to keep distance from the hero, similar to skeletons, spiders, and geomancers Basic attack Pillager basic attack. Pillagers are armed with crossbows and are usually slow-moving, preferring to shoot the hero from a distance. However, it deals great pierce damage with a single shot. The pillager variant 2 cannot be stunned by normal attack damage except certain artifacts, any armor with the Snowball enchantment, and any weapon with the Stunning enchantment. Solemn Giant Behavior: Hostile Classification: Ancient, Illager Equipped models: Crossbow The arena where the Solemn Giant is summoned and fought. The Solemn Giant is an ancient tier 2 pillager that can be encountered during ancient hunts with the minimum offering of the following runes: This ancient resides within Solemn Giant Ancient Dungeon and possesses, at minimum, the following enchantments: Huge Multishot Radiance Shot Ricochet Four enchanted endermen act as the ancient's minions, possessing the following enchantments: Radiance Upon defeating the Solemn Giant, a gilded variant of the following items drops as a reward: Burst Crossbow Corrupted Crossbow Soul Hunter Crossbow Rapier Bee Stinger Freezing Foil Guard's Armor Sounds Idle Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Idle,? Tier 2 and 3 Pillager idle sounds are slightly low-pitched. (0.7-0.9) Hurt Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Hurt,? Armor Leather Layer 1 (Variant 1 & 2),? Death Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Death,? Mob body fall (Basic),? Mob body fall (Variant 1 & 2 only),? Armor Leather Layer 1 (Variant 1 & 2 only),? Crossbow Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Shoot,? Crossbow loading,? Projectile According to sfx_mob_pillagerProjectile file, the projectile sounds of a pillager's arrow are mixed with sword and "whoosh" sounds. Sounds: Sound, Description Sword Shing Whoosh Projectile impact According to sfx_mob_pillagerProjectileImpact file, the impact sounds of a pillager's arrow are mixed with sword and sickles impact sounds. Sounds: Sound, Description Sword Impact Steel Impact (1-4 only) Data values In the game files, tier 1 pillagers are referred to internally by the names Pillager, IllagerPillager, and PillagerVariant0. In the game files, tier 2 pillagers are referred to internally by the name PillagerVariant1. In the game files, tier 3 pillagers are referred to internally by the name PillagerVariant2. Pillagers have an unused legs position while charging its crossbow. Pillager arrow data file: BP_PillagerProjectile PS_Hit_PillagerArrow PS_PierceArrow History Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added pillagers. Minecraft Dungeons: 1.5.0.0, Reduced base damage by 20%. (75→62) Minecraft Dungeons: 1.8.0.0, Added the solemn giant. Minecraft Dungeons: 1.14.1.0, The solemn giant now wears a helmet.(Variant 0→Variant 1) Quotes “ PILLAGERS – Armed with crossbows, pillagers are slow-moving but capable of inflicting great damage with a single shot. You will have more luck defeating the pillager by dodging their attacks and getting close for a strong melee attack. „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons: A Handbook for Heroes Trivia Alongside crossbows, pillagers were taken from Minecraft Dungeons, even though they were added to the original game first. Pillagers in Minecraft Dungeons have many new sounds that didn't appear in the original game. According Minecraft Dungeons Patch Notes 1.5.0.0, Pillagers base damage has been reduced from 75 to 62. However, in the BP_PillagerProjectile file, their base damage is 60. The second variant pillager's nose texture is different than the other variants, it uses the vanilla minecraft texture. title: Dungeons:Skeleton
text: Health points: Tier 1 ： 60 Tier 2 ： 200 Tier 3 ：480 Behavior: Hostile Classification: Undead Arcade (Melee) Spawn: See § Spawning Equipped models: Tier 1 ： Shortbow Tier 2 ： Longbow Reinforced Mail (Helmet) Tier 3: Reinforced Mail Power Bow Arcade Melee Variant 1: Scale Mail Dagger Arcade Melee Variant 2: Reinforced Mail (Helmet) Scale Mail Sword A skeleton is an undead hostile mob that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. Skeletons have three variants - the first variant is unarmored and wields a shortbow, the second variant wears a reinforced mail helmet and wields a longbow, and the third bears full reinforced mail and a power bow. Spawning Missions Squid Coast Creeper Woods Creepy Crypt Soggy Swamp Soggy Cave Pumpkin Pastures Arch Haven Cacti Canyon Redstone Mines Fiery Forge Desert Temple Lower Temple Highblock Halls (Only in the outside portion and in the Well ) Underhalls (Only in Abandoned Cellar and Forgotten Prison ) Dingy Jungle Panda Plateau Overgrown Temple Lost Settlement (Only in the final ambush) Lone Fortress (Only in the ambush next to the diamond key golem) Windswept Peaks Gale Sanctum Gauntlet of Gales Colossal Rampart Soul Sand Valley Abyssal Monument Radiant Ravine (Only in Molten Monument ) The Stronghold Daily Trial Tower Treetop Tangle Skeletons also spawn when night falls, regardless of the mission, and when at least one hero dies in multiplayer. Ancients Barrage Scuttling Torment Behavior Skeletons attempt to position themselves at a set distance from the hero. Skeletons shoot arrows normally underwater without being slowed down, unlike their original game counterpart. Flee Skeletons flee when the hero gets near. If the player is stationary, the skeleton repeatedly shoot without moving. Bow action Skeletons harm the hero by shooting arrows from their bows at a distance, dealing light damage. Novelty Skeletons have a rare behavior that can only be seen while idle, in which they spin their skull rapidly with their left arm. title: Legends:Armored Tower
text:
Type: Piglin structure
Hordes: Horde of the Hunt
An armored tower is a piglin structure that can be found within the various piglin outposts and piglin bases of Minecraft Legends. They are the piglin equivalent to scatter towers.
Spawning
Armored towers are found commonly in Horde of the Hunt bases and outposts.
Usage
If any non-piglin mob is present, armored towers shoot a volley of blaze rods, lowering their shield in the process. If the target is a structure, they shoot one blaze rod from their top. Otherwise, they raise the shield, becoming immune to all damage.
Siegeworks
An armored tower boosted by siegeworks.
The attack range of armored towers can be increased by nearby siegeworks. This effect being active is indicated by a horned skull appeared on one side of the armored tower.
Quotes
Name: Armored Tower, Category: Piglins, Unlocked by: Destroying an armored tower, Quote: "This powerful tower fires a spread of molten-hot blaze rods that damage and ignite anything they hit. The only way to take down an armored tower is to send in allies when it fires and the tower shield drops."
Sounds
Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier
Shoot blaze rod, Sfx_tower_piglin_arrow_attack
Blaze rod impact (entity), Proj_piglin_tower_shield_impact_entity
Blaze rod impact (world), Proj_piglin_tower_shield_impact_world
Damage, Struct_piglin_tower_shield_damage
Destroyed, Struct_piglin_tower_shield_destroyed
Raise shield, Struct_piglin_tower_shield_door_close
Lower shield, Struct_piglin_tower_shield_door_open
Fire, Struct_piglin_tower_shield_fire
Fire arc, Struct_piglin_tower_shield_fire_arc
Achievements
Achievement: Are These Made of Nether Wart?, In-game description: Destroy 250piglin buildingsin a single world., Actual requirements (if different): —, Gamerscore earned: 40G, Trophy type (PS): Silver
History
Minecraft Legends: TU0, 1.17.24827, Added furnace towers.
Minecraft Legends: 1.17.35227, The furnace tower now has a journal icon.
Minecraft Legends: TU1, 1.17.49848, Changed the name of the furnace tower to armored tower.
Minecraft Legends: Updated the armored tower's journal entry to reflect its name change.

Issues for Minecraft Legends relating to "Armored Tower" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since January 10, 2024.

title: Dungeons:Vindicator
text: Health points: Tier 1: 110 Tier 2: 330 Tier 3：500 Behavior: Hostile Classification: Enemies Mob category: Illager Attack strength: Tier 1: 75 Tier 2: 85 Tier 3: 100 Speed: 1100 Spawn: See § Spawning Equipped models: Tier 1: Axe Tier 2: Original: Double Axe Arcade: Axe Tier 3: Original: Whirlwind Arcade: Double Axe A vindicator is a hostile axe -wielding illager that spawns in various locations. It has three variants, the first being unarmored and wielding an axe, the second variant having a helmet and a double axe, and the third variant wearing a horned helmet and wielding a whirlwind. In Minecraft Dungeons Arcade, the second variant wields an axe instead, and the third variant wields a double axe. Lore Before the Arch-Illager appeared, vindicators were basic illager soldiers, guarding the woodland mansions and participating in raids. After Archie gets the Orb of Dominance, the vindicators and pillagers led by the evokers Thord and Walda, joined the Arch-Illager's army, patrolling the Highblock Keep, the Fiery Forge, Redstone Mines, and participating in raids, now with the help of redstone golems and the redstone monstrosity. After the heroes defeat the Arch-Illager, they traveled to other areas of the mainland and even other dimensions, where vindicators can also be found. Spawning Squid Coast Creeper Woods Creepy Crypt Woodland Mansion Woodland Prison Soggy Swamp Soggy Cave Pumpkin Pastures Arch Haven Cacti Canyon Redstone Mines Fiery Forge Highblock Halls Underhalls Obsidian Pinnacle Frosted Fjord Lone Fortress Lost Settlement Windswept Peaks Gale Sanctum Gauntlet of Gales Colossal Rampart Radiant Ravine ( secret area ) End Wilds Broken Citadel Daily Trial Tower Treetop Tangle Behavior If a vindicator sees a hero, it charges and attacks with an axe. Tier 3 vindicators cannot be stunned by anything except some artifacts, any armor with the Snowball enchantment, and any weapon with the Stunning enchantment. Strong attack Vindicators attack the hero on sight, performing powerful axe swings at melee range. They are incredibly fast, though they attack slower than most other melee monsters. Retreat After vindicators attack a hero, they retreat. When they retreat, they lower their axes. Target lock Vindicators turn to "lock onto" the hero if they run more than fifteen blocks away. Once locked on, their charge speed is greatly increased and they no longer retreat after hitting. Instead, they will chase the hero while attacking whenever they are in range until aggravation is exhausted. Aggressive A vindicator becomes aggressive if a hero is standing in the vindicator's taunt circle deals damage to them. Novelty If the hero is surrounded by three or more vindicators and has already been attacked by some of them, while at a sufficient distance the vindicator will appear to be performing a battle cry while waving their axe in the air. Sounds The idle, hurt and death sounds of vindicator variant 1 and 2 shares with distorted vigilant scoundrel sounds. Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Death, sfx_mob_vindicatorDeath_soundWave Hurt, sfx_mob_vindicatorHurt_soundWave Idle, sfx_mob_vindicatorIdle_soundWave Novelty (plays when they attack), sfx_mob_vindicatorNovelty_soundWave Minecraft Dungeons Arcade Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Death, sfx_mob_pillagerDeath_soundWave Hurt, sfx_mob_pillagerHurt_soundWave Idle, sfx_mob_pillagerIdle_soundWave Data values ID Name: Vindicator, Variant: Tier 1, Blueprint: BP_IllagerVindicatorCharacter_C, Level mob-type: vindicatorvariant0, Unified level mob-type: vindicator, Translation key: mob_Vindicator Name: Tier 2, Variant: BP_IllagerVindicatorVariant1Character_C, Blueprint: — Name: Tier 3, Variant: BP_IllagerVindicatorVariant2Character_C, Blueprint: — History Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added vindicators. Minecraft Dungeons: 1.7.2.0, Adjusted new "taunt circle + lock" behavior for vindicators. Minecraft Dungeons: Reduced charge speed. Minecraft Dungeons: Reduced melee ticks to 2 (was 3). Minecraft Dungeons: Added behavior which makes them go aggressive when hurt when standing in taunt circle. Minecraft Dungeons: They now get locked onto the hero if they run away > 15 blocks. Issues Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Vindicator" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023. Quotes “ VINDICATOR – These axe-wielding terrors are surprisingly quick on their feet. They'll charge at you in a frenzy and attempt to mow you down with one swift chop. It can difficult to react quickly with a bow, so try to hit them with a melee weapon before they can hurt you. „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons: A Handbook for Heroes Trivia Vindicator's texture imported into the illusioner's sketelon mesh model The vindicator's textures are perfectly compatible with the illusioner 's model, and the unused white wig will appear in place of the Illusioner's hood layer, as well as the texture of the vindicator's unused closed arms and upside down T eyebrows. The vindicator has an outsourced cinematic version that can be seen in the Minecraft Dungeons: Opening Cinematic and in the Squid Coast loading screen, using the skin texture of the base game (pre 1.14 ) and having a collar with the same color as the pants, instead of red. Due to outsourcing, vindicators in-game, in achievements, or in books sometimes

title: Dungeons:Zombie
text: Health points: Tier 1: 55 Tier 2: 210 Tier 3: 500 Behavior: Hostile Classification: Enemies Mob category: Undead Attack strength: Tier 1: 70 Tier 2: 85 Tier 3: 100 Spawn: See § Spawning Equipped models: Tier 2: Daggers Scale Mail Reinforced Mail (helmet) Tier 3: Reinforced Mail Sword A zombie is a common undead hostile mob that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. Zombies have three variants, identifiable by their gear. The first tier has no armor and no weapon. The second tier has a dagger and a reinforced mail helmet. The third tier has a longsword ( sword ), a reinforced mail helmet, and a scale mail chestplate. Spawning Zombies will spawn when night falls, regardless of the mission, when at least one hero dies in multiplayer. Squid Coast Creeper Woods Creepy Crypt Soggy Swamp Soggy Cave Pumpkin Pastures Arch Haven Cacti Canyon Redstone Mines Fiery Forge Highblock Halls Underhalls Obsidian Pinnacle ( Arcade ) Dingy Jungle Panda Plateau Overgrown Temple Lone Fortress Gale Sanctum Gauntlet of Gales Colossal Rampart The Stronghold Daily Trial Ancient Hunt Tower Treetop Tangle Summoned by necromancers Woodland Mansion Woodland Prison Desert Temple Lower Temple Summoned by mob spawners Frosted Fjord Secret areas Lost Settlement Abyssal Monument Radiant Ravine Behavior Zombies have three variants. The first variant is unarmored and unarmed. The second wears scale mail with a reinforced mail helmet as well as a single dagger, and the third wears full reinforced mail and a sword. Zombies have nine times more health when enchanted. Walk Zombies wander around when they are idle. Basic attack When an available target is in range, they will perform a downward swipe with both arms, dealing damage to heroes in its range. Novelty sleep Zombies sometimes sleep when idle. The third zombie variant does not perform its novelty animation, however, as it is broken. Unlike in the base game, zombies do not turn into drowned when submerged in water for 30 seconds or more. title: Dungeons:Zombified Piglin
text:
Health points: Tier 1:80 Tier 2:160 Tier 3:320
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Piglin, Undead
Attack strength: Tier 1:94 Tier 2:115 Tier 3:150
Equipped models: Tier 1: Golden Sword (reskinned Sword ) Tier 2: Golden Axe (reskinned Axe ) Tier 3: Firebrand
A zombified piglin is a piglin variant that can be found within the Flames of the Nether DLC of Minecraft Dungeons. They come in two variants; the first resembles a piglin brute, while the second is more heavily armored, wears a cracked golden helmet and draped in red clothing.
Spawning
Nether Wastes
Warped Forest
Crimson Forest
Soul Sand Valley ( Cinematic )
Behavior
Zombified piglins in Minecraft Dungeons are completely hostile and attack the hero upon seeing them, attacking them with their gold sword. They are not immune to fire damage. They also don't scare nearby piglins unlike the original game. They have the same behaviors as piglins, but have more health and deal more damage.
Sounds

Data values
In the game files, zombified piglins are referred to internally by the names ZombifiedPiglinVariant and ZombifiedPiglinMeleeVariant.
In the game files, tier 1 zombified piglins are referred to internally by the name ZombifiedPiglinMeleeVariant0.
In the game files, tier 2 zombified piglins are referred to internally by the name ZombifiedPiglinMeleeVariant1.
In the game files, tier 3 zombified piglins are referred to internally by the name ZombifiedPiglinMeleeVariant2.

title: Dungeons:Auto Crossbow
text:
Type: Ranged Weapon
Rarity: UNIQUE
Power: 1
Speed: 9.4
Ammo: 11.5
Properties: High fire rate Fire rate increases upon firing
Enchantment: Accelerate
Damage type: Ranged
Soul information: Does not accept soul enchantments Does not grant soul collection
Runes
Variants: Rapid Crossbow Butterfly Crossbow
In-game description The Arch-Illager said the design for this powerful crossbow came to him in a vision.
The auto crossbow is a unique rapid crossbow variant that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Missions

A before a mission name indicates that the item is only obtainable in Daily Trials of that mission.
Redstone Mines
Highblock Halls (Adventure)
Lone Fortress (DLC)
Windswept Peaks (DLC)
Coral Rise (DLC)
Tower
Merchants
Gift Wrapper
Mystery Merchant
Luxury Merchant
Piglin Merchant
Ancients
Haunted Caller
Usage
The auto crossbow possesses low power, high speed, and a large ammunition supply. The quiver starts with 133 arrows, normal arrow bundles give 66 arrows, supply chest bundles give another 133 arrows, and surprise gift bundles give 33 arrows.
The auto crossbow can be shot repeatedly if the ranged attack button is held down. Ranged weapons require the hero to have at least one normal arrow or a special arrow from a quiver artifact in their inventory to shoot. Unlike bows, shots from crossbows cannot be charged.
Damage caused by the arrows can be increased with enchantments like Power, the Strength buff, the Cowardice armor enchantment, and certain armors that have increased ranged damage such as hunter's armor.

title: Dungeons:Quiver
text:
For the removed item in Minecraft, see Quiver.

This article's lead section is too long.
You can help by improving it. To do this, shorten the lead section to summarize the topic.
Type:?
When entering or returning to the camp, the arrows in the hero’s quiver reset to the default amount for the bow that they have equipped. If the hero’s currently equipped bow changes to another, the quiver is set to the appropriate amount for the newly equipped item. The only way to refill the quiver slot while in the camp is to activate the Surprise Gift enchantment and create an arrow bundle. Heroes can also use the Flaming Quiver, Torment Quiver, or Fireworks Arrow artifacts to acquire special arrows to shoot.
Arrow bundle
Type: Consumable
Missions found in: All missions
In-game description A small bundle of arrows
An arrow bundle is a consumable in Minecraft Dungeons. When consumed, it adds arrows to your quiver. The amount added depends on the ammo value of the hero's bow, and where the bundle came from. Arrow bundles dropped from enemies and normal chests give half the quiver's starting amount, bundles from supply chests give the quiver's starting amount, and bundles from the Surprise Gift enchantment give one fourth the starting value.
Obtaining
Arrow bundles are mainly obtained by defeating mobs and opening chests.
They are a guaranteed drop from supply chests.
The common armor enchantment Surprise Gift has a chance to create arrow bundles, as well as other consumables.
The number of arrows in an arrow may sometimes vary. For example, with the auto crossbow, normal arrow bundles give 66 arrows, supply chest bundles give 133 arrows, and surprise gift bundles give 33 arrows.

title: Legends:Arrow Tower
text:
Type: Structure
Cost: Campaign: 35 Wood 35 Stone Versus Mode: 150 Wood 150 Stone
An arrow tower is a player structure that can shoot arrows at enemies within Minecraft Legends.
Building
Arrows towers can be built anywhere by using a build allay and spending 35 wood and 35 stone in the Campaign, or 150 wood and 150 stone in Versus Mode.
Behavior
Arrow towers shoot single arrows at any non-allies within range.
Affected by
Arrows towers are affected by the following structures when within their range:
Battle Drum - Increases firing speed
Carpenter Hut - Heals damage dealt
Kaboomery - Makes arrows explode upon impact, which increases damage and area of effect
Masonry - Upgrades the structure, giving it a stone appearance and more health
Spyglass Overlook - Increases maximum range
Quotes
Name: Arrow Tower, Category: Player Building Melodies, Unlocked by: Automatically unlocked, Quote: "Shoots arrows at enemies that come within range. This tower can be upgraded by other structures. Arrow towers can be upgraded by: Kaboomery, Spyglass Overlook and Battle Drum."

title: Dungeons:Artifact
text: The Corrupted Beacon is an example of an artifact from Minecraft Dungeons. The term artifact refers to a category of items that can equipped by the hero to temporarily summon pets, enhance or heal themselves or other heroes, or attack and weaken mobs. The hero can have up to three artifacts equipped at any one time. There are 46 artifacts in total, only one of which are of unique rarity. There are additionally eight unused artifacts, two of which are unique. There are two types of artifacts: ones that require (and grant) souls, and ones that do not. Soul-based artifacts generally have shorter cooldown times, and always come with +1 soul gathering. Obtaining Artifacts can be obtained from these sources: Village Merchant Luxury Merchant Mystery Merchant Obsidian chests Piggy Bank When a mission is completed. When gifted by a friend. Cooldown Cooldown Sequence Artifacts have a cooldown period between uses, during which they cannot be used, unequipped, or switched with a different Artifact from the inventory. Artifact cooldowns are independent from one another: equipping two corrupted beacons and using one will only cause a cooldown on the one that was used. Cooldowns can be reduced by equipping armor with cooldown reduction properties, such as the evocation robe and its unique variants, or enchanting armor with Cool Down. Cooldown Shot can also be used to trim down cooldown time on artifacts that are already on cooldown. Damaging artifacts Artifacts that output damage to mobs with no other notable effects. title: Earth:Ashen Cow
text:
Health points: 10 HP
Behavior: Passive
Mob type: Animal
Hitbox size: Height: 1.4 blocks Width 0.9 blocks
Spawn: Plains
Rarity: Uncommon
Player journal description Although its hide is dark and ashy, no creepers exploded near this cow.
The ashen cow was a variant of the cow with a gray coat of fur found only in Minecraft Earth.
Behavior
Main article: Cow § Behavior
Similar to normal cows, ashen cows wandered around, mooing and breathing occasionally. They avoided water, lava and walking off cliffs high enough to cause fall damage. They fled for a few seconds when harmed.
Obtaining
Ashen cows were uncommon mobs found in cow tappables, with a 8.1% chance of getting one.
Drops
When killed, ashen cows dropped:
0-2 Leather
1-3 Raw Beef ( Cooked Beef if killed while on fire)
Ashen cows could be milked by using a bucket on it, yielding a milk bucket.

title: Story Mode:Assembly Required
text: Details Episode: 2 Season: 1 Release date: October 27, 2015 Written by: Joshua Rubin, Eric Stirpe, and Tim Williams Directed by: Jason Latino Assembly Required, known also as Episode 2, is the second episode of Minecraft: Story Mode. It was released on October 27, 2015. The episode follows Jesse and their friends as they set out to recruit either Magnus or Ellegaard, members of the Order of the Stone, in an effort to stop the wither storm. Synopsis “ Jesse decides to go after one of the members of the Order of the Stone to help defeat the Witherstorm: Ellegaard, the Redstone Engineer, or Magnus, the demolitions expert. „ — In-game description. Plot If the player decides to find Magnus, Jesse, Axel and Reuben will return to the Nether and arrive at a portal which leads them to "Boom Town", a village overrun by griefers. Jesse, Axel and Reuben barely escape the onslaught by the griefers, arriving at Magnus' hideout. They talk to Magnus about leaving to go save the world, in which Magnus agrees, despite initially being reluctant. However Magnus can't leave the tower because he's the king of Boom Town. So they decide to stage a fight in the Death Bowl, to make Jesse win and become king. During the fight Magnus looses control and builds a cannon, but Jesse barely wins by blowing the cannon's TNT with a bow, allowing Magnus to come along with them. The other griefers prepare to attack Jesse, but the wither storm arrives and destroys Boom Town, forcing Jesse, Axel, Reuben and Magnus to retreat back to the temple through the Nether. Likewise, if the player decides to find Ellegaard, Jesse, Olivia and Reuben will return to the Nether and arrive at a portal, which leads them to "Redstonia", which is labeled "the capital city of engineering". There they see Ellegaard, that closes herself in her "Dome of Concentration" to try making a Command Block, and deactivates the stairs to get in. Jesse either crafts a repeater or steals one from Disco Mickey's contraption, and re-enable the stair controls, leading them to the dome. Inside, they meet Ellegaard, who has attempted to create a command block for more than 6,451 times. She is surprised to see Jesse and Olivia and thinks Calvin sent them here. Jesse interrupts, saying that Gabriel sent them here, and she remembers the Order before they split up. Jesse, Olivia, and Reuben agree to help her create a command block. On the third attempt, Ellegaard thinks the command block is destabilizing, but the wither storm arrives and destroys Redstonia, forcing Jesse, Olivia, Reuben and Ellegaard to retreat back to the temple through the Nether. Arriving back at the temple, Gabriel/Petra greets them, and Jesse's friends attempt to find Soren. Ivor shows up on the map, shocking Jesse's friends as well as the Order. They all agree to stop him before he arrives at Soren's hideout. Jesse decides on whether to go to find Ivor at night or in the morning, and they leave to go to Soren's temple. At the temple, Ellegaard and Magnus cause a ruckus regarding who should stop the wither storm, and they both go their separate ways. Jesse decides on whether to go with Ellegaard or Magnus, and Gabriel/Petra go along with Jesse to talk to them about a private manner. It is revealed that Gabriel/Petra got " wither sickness " from the wither storm, a severe form of wither. A creeper blows up behind them, causing the floor to give way to a library, where Jesse finds Ivor. Gabriel/Petra shows up to aid Jesse, and the three fight it out with swords and potions. Jesse's friends show up to stop him, but Ivor hits them with a splash potion of slowness, leaving them behind, so Ivor can kill Ellegaard and Magnus, as well as demolish the world. Appearances Characters Jesse Axel Olivia Reuben Lukas Petra ​ [ determinant ] Ivor Gabriel ​ [ determinant ] Wither Storm Ellegaard Magnus Calvin ​ [ determinant ] Mabel ​ [ determinant ] Sandy ​ [ determinant ] Farmer Gloria ​ [ determinant ] Disco Mickey ​ [ determinant ] TNT Dustin ​ [ determinant ] Nohr ​ [ determinant ] Schoolboy ​ [ determinant ] Species Bat Creeper Cow (detrminant) Enderman Ghast Human Skeleton Spider Squid Wither Storm Zombie Zombie Pigman Settings Nether Overworld (main) Boom Town Temple of the Order of the Stone Redstonia Soren's Fortress Woods Player decision statistics Achievements Main article: MCSM:Achievement Achievement: Nether Say Die, In-game description: Complete Chapter 1 of Episode 2, Actual requirements (if different): —, Gamerscore earned: 20G, Trophy type (PS): Bronze Achievement: Griefer Madness, In-game description: Acquire the Griefer, Actual requirements (if different): Bring Magnus back to the temple., Gamerscore earned: 25G, Trophy type (PS): Bronze Achievement: Occam's

title: Story Mode:PAMA
text: Details Gender: None Species: Computer Status: Destroyed Aliases: Monster Machine (Petra)​ [ determinant ] A Thinking Machine Allies: See § Allies Enemies: See § Enemies First appearance: Access Denied Latest appearance: Access Denied Actor: Jason Topolski The Prototype: Autonomous Management Agent, abbreviated as " PAMA ", is a redstone computer that mind-controlled the inhabitants of Crown Mesa. Originally created by Harper to assist with tasks in the town, PAMA later deemed the inhabitants inefficient. It serves as the central antagonist of Access Denied and is voiced by Jason "jtop" Topolski. Appearance PAMA consisted of a large green monitor from the outside, and another monitor together with redstone machinery in the inside. It was capable of turning on lamps on its monitor to create symbols or pictures of faces that represent emotions. The entire machinery is consisted of redstone cables with repeaters that also connects to each of the monitors from the inside and the outside. There are three spires that connects wirelessly to the redstone heart. The redstone heart powers the whole computer and is placed in a vertical flying machine. Personality PAMA was designed to always strive for progress. It is a computer that helps automate things such as farming or mining and to put monsters to good use before it makes them "useful." While it is unknown if PAMA was capable of experiencing emotions, it was ready to do anything to thrive. According to Harper, when PAMA realized the inhabitants of Crown Mesa were not useful enough, it has attached redstone chips to their heads to control them. When PAMA realized water was its weakness, it had removed almost all water in its world. Biography It was a giant computer from Crown Mesa created by Harper to automate things such as farming or mining and to put monsters to good use. However, the invention saw the townspeople as "inefficient" and enslaved them, making them "useful." It used redstone chips to remotely control its victims. It's motivation after Jesse entered Mesa was to use the enslaved people and mobs to re-create the portal so that PAMA can expand themself to the rest of the world. Relationships Allies Hivemind of chipped mobs PAMA used redstone chips to control mobs. When a mob is chipped, their memories are added to PAMA's databanks. PAMA used a redstone machine in the center of Crown Mesa to attach chips to mobs. Chicken Creeper Harry Harper Lukas Petra Skeleton Spider Zombie Enemies Harper Harper is PAMA's creator Jesse Appearances Access Denied Quotes “ I am PAMA. Greetings. We have much work ahead of us, but maybe it can even be... fun. If you choose to cooperate, that is. „ — PAMA to Jesse's gang. (Determinant) “ I am making everything useful. Efficient. Effective. I am what you might call 'a computer' - A thinking-machine. Designed to make things useful. Using redstone chips, I have connected to the people of this town and helped them be useful. Coordinated. I have made their world perfect. Thanks to me, there is no wasted activity. No wasted thought. I am connected to them, and they are connected to me. A beautiful web of productivity and efficiency. „ — PAMA to Jesse's gang about itself. (Determinant) “ My apologies for the violent nature of this visit. „ — PAMA when Jesse refuses to go with the mind-controlled citizens. (Determinant) “ Yay! „ — PAMA throughout the episode. “ Termination sequence: activated. „ — PAMA when trying to find Jesse after he switches to a mob while in Harper's transmigration headset. “ Processing information. „ — PAMA “ Error. This data is contradictory. Does not not not not compute. Error. Diverting power. „ — PAMA when confronted with a paradox. “ Paradox status... On hold. Gasp! My creator! Have you finally agreed to merge with me? „ — PAMA to Harper. “ Your running makes me sad. „ — PAMA to Jesse. “ Game over, Jesse. Resistance is... not useful. „ — PAMA to Jesse. “ Interference detected. Drone sequencing initiated. Trace subroutine initiated.... Is that you, Jesse?.... A city in the sky... Towns and mountains, eaten by a living storm... an adorable pig friend... „ — PAMA when reading Petra and Lukas' thoughts. “ Your friends have been made useful! You too will be made useful! „ — PAMA to Jesse, through mind-controlled Petra/Lukas. (Determinant) “ You can't play hide and seek forever Jesse. title: Story Mode:Axel
text: Instructions: Empty sections Details Gender: Male Species: Human Status: Alive Titles: King of Boom Town​ [ determinant ] Affiliations: New Order of the Stone Residence: Boom Town (Season Two) Allies: See § Allies Enemies: See § Enemies First appearance: The Order of the Stone Latest appearance: Above and Beyond Actor: Brian Posehn Axel is one of the main characters in Minecraft: Story Mode, known to be a prankster and one of Jesse 's closest friends. Appearance Axel has a huge build and is tall in appearance. He has short, black hair along with a unibrow and his skin is pale white. He wears a green jacket, black finger-less gloves, tan pants, and sandals. Later on, he also gains a helmet similar to Magnus the Rogue's, a chest piece similar to his former shirt with gloves and brown boots. In Season Two, Axel's jacket is now replaced with a green plaid flannel, which is left unbuttoned to reveal a black t-shirt with a skull on it. Personality Axel is portrayed to be a joker who enjoys making people laugh. He acts as a source of comic relief, making lots of sarcastic quips and short one-liners. Over the course of the story, he is revealed to be a little cowardly, but is very fierce when defending his friends and will risk a great deal to protect them. He is, at times, loud and explosive and enjoys griefing. Axel is also shown to be a kleptomaniac, as he tries to steal small items whenever he can. Biography Relationships Allies Jesse Reuben Olivia Petra Lukas Magnus Ivor The rest of the Order of the Stone Harper Enemies Blaze Rods Romeo Appearances Game appearances The Order of the Stone Assembly Required The Last Place You Look A Block and a Hard Place Order Up! A Journey's End? Hero in Residence Above and Beyond Other media Monthly Build Challenge: Save the Ocean Quotes “ I can't believe I just saw that! That was amazing! Why is no one freaking out!? „ — Axel to everyone. “ Thanks for believing in me, buddy. „ — Axel to Jesse in the temple of the Old Builders “ It was Aiden! That punk! „ — Axel “ The spirit is willing, but the flesh is sad. „ — Axel to Jesse (Determinant) “ There's something different about you Jesse. Something- oh-man oh-man oh-man! „ — Axel to Jesse (Determinant) “ After I watch one more chicken get owned. „ — Axel to Jesse (Determinant) “ Haven't you done enough damage for one day? Petra might be dead because of you! „ — Axel blaming Lukas for Petra's presumed death “ Jesse means US, not you. „ — Axel to Lukas (Determinant) “ I felt all of that! „ — Axel after he tripped after the potion of slowness wore off (Determinant) “ Isn't it weird how dispensers are carved to look like spooky little faces? What do you dispense, little guy? „ — Axel “ (Name of group)'s luck is changing. „ — Axel “ Magnus, we totally got caught by Magnus! I mean...I think we're gonna die now. „ — Axel, when first seeing Magnus the Rogue (Determinant) “ Dude, what a chicken! „ — Axel talking about Soren when he runs away (Determinant) “ How do you make Enderman focused? I can't even make myself focused. „ — Axel to Olivia “ Whoa, that is, without question, the coolest thing I have ever, ever seen! „ — Axel when Jesse shows him the weapon they made “ PAI-GOW! „ — Axel “ I think I got something... in both my eyes. „ — Axel, when Jesse is talking about Reuben in his speech (Determinant) “ WHY ARE WE SCREAMING?! „ — Axel (Determinant) “ Oh, it's that guy from EnderCon who was afraid of chickens! „ — Axel about the usher (Determinant) “ I thought maybe you'd have like a cool motivational thing to say there, but... that's cool, that's cool. „ — Axel to Jesse outside the temple of the Old Builders (Determinant) “ It's just... I really love cake. „ — Axel to Jesse inside the temple of the Old Builders “ That guy's voice is the worst. I can even hear it through the obsidian. „ — Axel to Jesse about Hadrian's voice when Axel's in an obsidian prison “ Huh? What part are we at? Am I being awesome? „ — Axel after waking up “ I promised myself I wouldn't cry. Promise broken! „ — Axel when he gives Petra one last hug before her departure Trivia Axel often falls on top of Lukas as a reoccurring gag. He does so four times throughout the game. In Order Up!, it is revealed Axel loves cake. Although absent during much of the second

title: Dungeons:Bag of Souls
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Armor
Property: +1 soul gathering
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Increases the maximum number of souls that can be carried.
Bag of Souls is an enchantment that can be found within the various armor of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 50% extra souls, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: 100% extra souls, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: 150% extra souls, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Bag of Souls increases the number of souls a hero can carry at one time. The maximum number of souls the player can carry without Bag of Souls is 300, the amount of extra souls increases with each tier, at the maximum tier 150% extra souls can be held, for a total of 750 souls. Unlike other soul enchantments, Bag of Souls can appear on non-soul type items.
Properties
+1 Soul Gathering
Stacking
Bag of Souls is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Built-into
Bag of Souls tier I is built-into the following armor:
Verdant Robe
History
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.4.3.0, Added Bag of Souls.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.16.2.0, Increased amount of percentage extra souls from 33%/67%/100% to 50%/100/150%.

title: Dungeons:Panda Plateau
text: Part of: Island Realms Minimum difficulty: V Unlock predecessor: Fossil Canyon scroll Dingy Jungle Raid captains: Usually has all six captains but not always Daily trials: Can have daily trials Protect the Pandas Within a secluded bamboo forest, perched on the face of a sprawling cliffside, a community of pandas have escaped the corrupting presence of the shard - until now. We need to fight back against the sprawling, strangling vines which have threatened to take over the homes of these lovable, defenseless pandas. Panda Plateau is the first island realms secret mission in Minecraft Dungeons, and is part of the Jungle Awakens DLC. It is unlocked after collecting the scroll found in Fossil Canyon and completing Dingy Jungle. Completing Panda Plateau does not unlock any missions. Appearance and description Panda Plateau is an expansive bamboo jungle located south of Overgrown Temple. It is composed of uneven terrain with cliffs, and is populated with countless bamboo stalks and a once-thriving community of pandas. It features jungle foliage, stone ruins, small water ponds, waterfalls, torches, a dark cave, and statues of pandas, parrots, and villagers. Near the start of the mission, a bamboo gate can be found with a gold statue of the Arch-Illager next to it that prevents access further into the mission. To the south-west, a stone ruin can be found up a steep hill suspended high above the ground. It contains a switch that, if activated, lowers the bamboo gate below and starts an ambush. Shortly after the bamboo gate, an open area with a water pool in the center can be found that contains five captured pandas. Rescuing all five pandas lowers the bamboo gate to the north-east, granting access to a pathway that leads to the entrance door of Panda Plateau Cave. Panda Plateau Cave is an expansive cave with glowing mushrooms, thin dirt pathways, and an additional six captured pandas. The six captured pandas must be rescued to lower a bamboo gate at the end of the cave, granting access to the exit door. After exiting Panda Plateau Cave, a supplies chest can be opened and a village built atop the trees can be found to the south-west. After making it past the village, a long pathway populated with celebrating pandas can be found that leads to a small wooden boat. This boat can be used to escape the plateau, ending the mission. title: Dungeons:The Monkey Motivator
text:
This article documents a feature that has been officially scrapped.
Minecraft Dungeons has been discontinued, and as such will not be added to the game in the foreseeable future.
This page describes content that is a part of the Jungle Awakens DLC.
Type: Melee Weapon
Rarity: UNIQUE
In-game description A strange weapon that makes a sound that no creature from the Overworld has ever made.
The monkey motivator is an unused unique pickaxe variant that was scrapped during the development of the Jungle Awakens DLC of Minecraft Dungeons.
Appearance
According to a tweet from one of the developers, the monkey motivator was going to feature a stick with a banana on it, forming the shape of a pickaxe.
Obtaining
Adding the monkey motivator to the inventory with an editor will crash the game when clicked on.
Sounds
Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier
Equip, sfx_item_pickaxeUnique2PlaceDrums_soundWave
Equip, sfx_item_pickaxeUnique2PlaceMonkey_soundWave
Swing, sfx_item_pickaxeUnique2SwingBanana_soundWave
History
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.1.1.0, Added sound files into the game.
Minecraft Dungeons: May 29, 2020, Jungle Awakens officially revealed, with a release date slated forJuly 2020.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.3.2.0, Jungle Awakens releases, yet the monkey motivator is cut.

title: Legends:Banner of Courage
text:
The Banner of Courage is one of the three special items gifted to the player by the hosts in Minecraft Legends.
Journal icon for the Banner of Courage
Lore
The Banner of Courage was created by Knowledge and gifted to the player by the hosts upon beginning the game.
Usage
Players are able to use the Banner of Courage to command allied mobs to follow or attack. A mechanic called banner view can be used to command allied mobs to either "charge" or "focus target". It can also give specific orders to all mobs, all of one category, all of one mob, one random mob, one random mob in a category, or one specific type of mob.
There are four categories of mobs: melee, ranged, support, and special.
Melee mobs include cobblestone golems, zombies, warriors, and the First of Stone.
Ranged mobs include plank golems, skeletons, witches, and the First of Oak.
Support mobs include mossy golems and the First of Brick.
Special mobs include grindstone golems, creepers, and the First of Diorite.
Quotes
Name: Banner of Courage, Category: Tools, Unlocked by: Automatically unlocked, Quote: "The Banner of Courage draws its power from whoever wields it. In the right hands, it is a symbol of unity and hope."
Achievements
Achievement: Banner Expert, In-game description: Give 25 orders from Banner View in a single world., Actual requirements (if different): —, Gamerscore earned: 20G, Trophy type (PS): Bronze
Achievement: Full Force, In-game description: Give an army of 80 mobs an order., Actual requirements (if different): —, Gamerscore earned: 50G, Trophy type (PS): Gold
Achievement: One Boom at a Time, In-game description: Give 10 individual orders to creepers from Banner View in a single world., Actual requirements (if different): —, Gamerscore earned: 20G, Trophy type (PS): Bronze
History
Minecraft Legends: 1.17.24827, Added the Banner of Courage.
Minecraft Legends: 1.17.49848, Overhauled banner view.

title: Legends:Battle Drum
text:
Type: Structure
Cost: Campaign: 100 Wood 50 Stone 200 Redstone Versus Mode: 250 Wood 150 Redstone
Identifier: badger:entity_struct_war_drums
A battle drum is a structure that buffs other structures in Minecraft Legends.
Building
Battle drums can be built by selecting the battle drum within the hotbar and using a build allay. A battle drum costs 100 wood, 50 stone, and 200 redstone in the Campaign, and 250 wood and 150 stone in Versus Mode.
The blue area around it will tell where it affects.
Usage
Battle drums increase the firing speed of nearby arrow towers, scatter towers, redstone launchers and ice traps. A structure is only affected if it is inside the zone the battle drum creates. Building more battle drums does not increase its effect.
Quotes
Name: Battle Drum, Category: Player Building Melodies, Unlocked by: Building Improvement Gather Redstone, Quote: "Increases the firing speed of nearby arrow towers, scatter towers, redstone launchers and traps. Requires redstone in order to build. For a structure to be affected, it must be inside of the zone the battle drum creates. If the battle drum is destroyed, affected structures lose their rate of fire increase."
Sounds

History
Minecraft Legends: 1.17.24827, Added battle drums.
Gallery

Damage type: Melee
Combo: 10 hits
Soul information: Does not accept soul enchantments Does not grant soul collection
Runes

In-game description The battlestaff is a perfectly balanced staff that is ready for any battle.
A battlestaff is a melee weapon that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. Its unique variants are the battlestaff of terror and the growing staff. Due to the 10 hit combo and fast striking, stackable damage enchantments and chance on hit enchantments are especially effective with this weapon. These include Void Strike, Committed, Fire Aspect, Radiance, Thundering, Critical Hit, Echo, and Stunning.
Obtaining
Missions

Tower
Merchants

Gift Wrapper

Piglin Merchant
Ancients

Usage
A battlestaff possesses moderate power, low speed, and moderate range. Its attack combo consists of two side swings, one wide swing, one slam, two stabbing moves, two side twirls, one spin attack, and one powerful slam. Its combo resets after 0.5 seconds, unlike most other weapons which reset their combos after three seconds.
