title: Story Mode:Beacontown
text: Details Type: Settlement Leader: Jesse ​ [ formerly (determinant) ] Radar ​ [ determinant ] Affiliations: Champion City (neighbor) First appearance: A Block and a Hard Place (precursor) Hero in Residence (formal) Latest appearance: Above and Beyond Beacontown is a large town founded by the New Order of the Stone and led by the group's leader, Jesse. It is the primary setting of Minecraft: Story Mode - Season Two. History Beacontown was first revealed at the end of A Block and a Hard Place as a small village with a week's worth of construction. At the village, Jesse's gang was established as the New Order of the Stone by the Old Order of the Stone in what can be seen as the first Founding Day celebration and the first activation of the Rainbow Beacon. Around 6 to 8 months later during Order Up!, the village had grown into a small town with roads, houses, and buildings. Ivor is shown to have settled into town, but his home gathered much attention due to it being deemed "unsafe". The player can choose whether Ivor's home should be left alone or if it should be torn down. The Order Hall is also revealed, with an inner treasure room housing items from the adventures of the New Order of the Stone. In A Journey's End?, following the gang's journey through the portal network, Olivia states that there were many improvements made to town while they were gone. 5 to 7 years later by the time of Hero in Residence, Beacontown had been formally named and had seen various improvements be made to it along with greater expansions. Some inhabitants of other worlds such as Nell, Stacy Plays, and Stampy Cat are shown to now reside in Beacontown. It is also shown that a legendary adventurer named Jack had settled into town and owned a map shop within "Bad Luck Alley", an alleyway district where-in its inhabitants seem to live off the grid and outside the rules of Beacontown. Beneath the town exists a mine with various tunnels that ultimately lead to the Heckmouth 's cavern. Following an adventure to Doom Sea, Jesse would present the structure block to the townspeople and unveil either the Tower of Builder or the Wall of the Builder. As the celebrations for Founding Day began, the Admin would appear in Beacontown having followed Jesse from the Sea Temple. At the beginning of Giant Consequences, Beacontown was heavily damaged as a result of Jesse's battle against Romeo. As repairs were made, Romeo would reappear within the town and soon trapped the Overworld in an eternal, snowy night which would last until Jesse found and destroyed the giant clock. During Jailhouse Block, "Jesse" seemingly returned home with "newly found powers", this unknowingly being the Admin to the townspeople, and as the real Jesse was imprisoned in the Sunshine Institute, Romeo would soon begin modifying Beacontown to his own "twisted" liking. At the end of Below the Bedrock, Jesse and the gang return from the Underneath, arriving at the temple of the Order of the Stone. As the gang notice a firework going off in the distance, they head inside the temple and overlook the new Twisted Beacontown in horror, making Petra comment on whether she was losing it or not. In Above and Beyond, Beacontown saw various changed be made to it, now being under the heavy control of Romeo. The town's citizens are shown to be fearful of either Jesses' presence. Jack's map shop is revealed to have been ransacked by either looters, the Admin's guards, or by Romeo himself. Beacontown also saw the hosting of an event titled "JesseCon" and the creation of a floating tower which primarily housed the portal to the Terminal Space on its roof. Following the defeat of the Admin, Beacontown would begin a restoration process under the "Beautification Project". The much that would be restored leading up to the finale was all of Beacontown's lava being replaced with water, determinantly excluding the lava on Ivor's pad, and the floating tower having trees planted on it and being accessible from the ground level via the use of scaffolding. Location Beacontown is located in the Overworld, being vertically opposite to Champion City, and east of the temple of the Order of the Stone. Beacontown is located far enough from Redstonia and Boom Town that it’s inconvenient to frequently visit, but near enough for occasional visits to not be impossible. A continuity issue regarding Beacontown's location is seen through Hero in Residence and Below the Bedrock; at first, Beacontown is located to the north-west of the world, but due to its revealed proximity to the temple of the Order of the Stone, it should be located east of it and close to the center of the world.

title: Legends:Brilliant Beetle
text:
Details
Type: Mount
Identifier: badger:animal_beetle
Brilliant beetles are ridable mounts within Minecraft Legends.
Spawning
Campaign
In the Campaign, brilliant beetles spawn in their home in jungles. After the hero unlocks one in their home, they can spawn in villages.
Versus
In Versus Mode, brilliant beetles spawn naturally in jungles regardless of if they have been unlocked.
Behavior
Brilliant beetles when not mounted will wander around, trying to avoid hazards and they run away from piglins.
Usage
While mounted by the hero, all movement controls are given to the player. Brilliant beetles have the unique ability to climb over terrain and structures such as walls, and glide slightly like the big beak mount. Unlike other mounts, brilliant beetles move slower on land, but are able to quickly cross the water.
Skins
As a mount, brilliant beetles have various skins that can be equipped to change its appearance. They can be either purchased from the marketplace or obtained as free rewards by completing lost legends. The following is a list of brilliant beetle skins:

title: Story Mode:Below the Bedrock
text: Details Episode: 4 Season: 2 Release date: November 7, 2017 Written by: Nicole Martinez, Meredith Ainsworth, and Doug Lieblich Directed by: Daniel Rosales Below the Bedrock, also known as Episode 4, is the fourth episode of Telltale Games ' Minecraft: Story Mode - Season Two. It was released on November 7, 2017. Jesse's group discover a forgotten world beneath bedrock, where they uncover secrets about the Admin’s past and search for a way to stop his rule. Summary “ Our heroes take a journey to a long-forgotten land where nothing is quite what it seems and danger lurks around every corner. Will Jesse's new alliances and friendships withstand the tests they'll face? „ — In-game description. Plot In Below the Bedrock, the six (Jesse, Xara, Petra, Radar, Jack and Nurm/Lluna) make their way down a tower. Suddenly, a giant enderman appears and begins dismantling the tower, but the group barely escapes and make their way toward the Oasis, a city that supposedly has an automated farm. At the Oasis, it is revealed that the city is in ruins. Xara gets angry over a destroyed bed, but eventually gets over it, and tells Jesse that the portal is the only way home, and that the secret weapon is in Fred's house at Fred's Keep. The group (Jesse, Petra, Radar, Jack and Nurm/Lluna) then go on a minecart track toward Fred's Keep. On the way to Fred's Keep, the giant enderman takes out a portion of the track, causing the group to separate. Jesse and Jack are hanging on the edge of a cliff, and are cornered by people who "sniff out for outsiders" (one of whom is named PorkChop). They offer their assistance in exchange for their swords. Should the player give them their swords, PorkChop and company will assist Jesse and Jack onto solid ground before leaving. Likewise, should the player refuse the deal, Jack will fall toward certain death, and Jesse is able to climb up safely. Nonetheless, the group (Jesse, Petra, Radar, Nurm/Lluna and/or Jack) make it to Fred's Keep. At Fred's Keep, the group meet with Binta, who informs the group that they can't enter Fred's house unless they win a trivia contest, in which Jesse accepts. Jesse is able to talk with other people at Fred's Keep in order to obtain more information about Fred, such as his favorite tool, or his pet chicken's name. Eventually, if Jesse wins the contest against the defending champion, Kent, he is able to search the house freely for the weapon. Likewise, if Jesse loses the contest, he sneaks into the house to find the weapon. It turns out, however, that the "weapon" is actually Fred's personal diary. Nonetheless, Jesse convinces that the people of Fred's Keep will have a much better life above the bedrock. Binta makes preparations while the group (Jesse, Petra, Radar, Nurm/Lluna and/or Jack) head toward Romeoburg. On the way to Romeoburg, the minecarts hit a few ditches, and Petra, Radar and Lluna/Nurm and/or Jack are hit with potions of slowness from the guard that Romeo (disguised as Jesse) summoned. After an altercation, it is revealed that the guard is Ivor, and Ivor reveals that Romeo gave him the Order's amulet in order to find "the fraud" (the real Jesse). Jesse sets up Ivor with a cover story of the player's choosing to fool Romeo, and in exchange, Ivor allows Jesse to use the amulet. Ivor then disappears in a puff of smoke, and the group continue toward Romeoburg. At Romeoburg, the group are confronted by members of Romeo's supposed "army", which turns out to be just a record player. Having caught them in their act, the two (Soup and Cal) let them in and let them take on Romeo's Death Challenge, which involves a bunch of hostile mobs infused with lava. The group are able to survive the challenge, and make it toward a cabin. Inside the cabin, Petra talks to Jesse abut not being able to talk to each other, but the two reconcile and put aside their differences. The two then solve a puzzle to find Romeo's "weapon", which is known as "#Potato451", and make it back toward the Oasis. Back at the Oasis, Jesse sets off the firework to signal the people at Fred's Keep, and gives Fred's "weapon" to Xara. Suddenly, a giant enderman appears and begins demolishing the portal, but it is quickly rebuilt by Jesse. The player can then choose to leave Radar behind as a distraction for the enderman or bring Radar with him, and the group goes through the portal. Back in the Overworld, the group (Jesse, Petra, Jack, Nurm/Lluna and Radar/Binta, Cam and Kent) show up at the Order's old temple. Jesse notices a firework going off, and sees that Beacontown is under Romeo's control. In

title: Dungeons:Beta Update April 3
text:
Game: Minecraft Dungeons
Release date: April 3, 2020
Type: Private beta
Beta Update April 3 ​ [ more information needed ] is a version of Minecraft Dungeons released on April 3, 2020. It was only available to users approved for the game's private beta, which concluded on April 24, 2020.
Changes
Gameplay
Difficulty
Increased the number of mobs that spawn in the Pumpkin Pastures.
Mobs now get staggered from damage slightly less often.
Slightly increased the difficulty of Default V and Default VI.
Attacking mobs from a distance now alerts nearby unaware mobs.
Increased the starting arrow count and number of arrows picked up at supply chests, but lowered number of arrows dropped by mobs.
Fixes
1 issue fixed
MCD-7 – Game randomly freezes during levels.

title: Legends:Big Beak
text:
Details
Type: Mount
Identifier: badger:animal_bird
A big beak is a ridable mount that can be found within the various regions of Minecraft Legends.
Spawning
Campaign
In the Campaign, big beaks spawn in their home in jagged peaks. After the hero unlocks one in their home, they can spawn in villages
Versus
In Versus Mode, big beaks can spawn naturally in jagged peaks regardless of if they have been unlocked.
Behavior
Big beaks idle around their home and can sometimes wander around but never strays too far from their home. They will often avoid hazards and run away from piglins.
Usage
While mounted by the hero, all movement controls are given to the player. Big beaks can glide over long distances. They can be paired with bouncecaps to jump high and traverse over tall distances.
Skins

title: Story Mode:Giant Ghast
text:
Details
Gameplay appearances: Combat Quick time events Cutscene
Attack strength: 1/2 heart ( )
Found in: Sunshine Institute Burrows
Free roam interactable: No
First appearance: Jailhouse Block
Latest appearance: Jailhouse Block
The giant ghast is a three-headed dark ghast that resides outside the Sunshine Institute. It is a boss mob that Jesse killed near the end of Jailhouse Block.
Behavior
Unlike the ghast, the giant ghast is able to fire multiple volleys of exploding fireballs similar to a blaze, fire curveball fireballs, and groups of fireballs. Unlike regular ghasts, it can survive multiple fireball attacks. It is also able to call ghasts to aid it.
Additionally, it would attack other ghasts if they hit it, causing the others to attack it too.
Biography
When Jesse comes up on top of the burrows, the giant ghast appears and calls other ghasts with it. Upon learning that the ghast has a weak spot on its head, Jesse jumped on the other ghast's heads and thrusted a sword at the weak spot, killing the ghast along with the other ghasts.

title: Legends:Blast Tower
text:
Type: Power Tower
Cost: Campaign: 125 Stone 100 Redstone 1 Blast Tower Core Versus Mode: 600 Stone 400 Redstone
Improvement needed: Build Power Towers
A blast tower is a structure that fires high knockback projectiles at enemies in Minecraft Legends.
Lore
Knowledge was responsible for the construction of power towers during the Overworld's creation to assist the Hosts. He built blast towers in forests to act as lightning rods that would direct lightning bolts towards them, preventing forest fires. However, little did he know about the use of all the electricity trapped in these towers, until the Hero discovered one and the Hosts notice another tower electrically shocking three piglin runts who tried to attack the tower.
Spawning
Campaign
Blast towers can be found naturally already built in meadow and forest biomes, though they won’t work or be able to be used until collected.
Building
Campaign
Blast towers can be built for 125 stone, 100 redstone, and one blast tower core using a build allay. To obtain blast tower cores, a blast tower must be found within the overworld and 100 gold and 25 redstone must be spent upon interacting with it. To do this, the collect power towers improvement must have been previously built at an improvement hub.
Versus Mode
Blast towers can be built by players for 600 stone and 400 redstone using a build allay. To do this, the build power towers improvement must have been previously built at an improvement hub.
Usage
Blast towers attack nearby enemies by firing electrically charged projectiles that knock enemies back on impact, sending them flying. The projectiles also deal significant damage within the blast radius. It cannot be affected by other structures except a carpenter hut.
Quotes
Journal
Name: Blast Tower, Category: Overworld, Unlocked by: Campaign:Collecting a blast towerVersus Mode:Building Improvement Gather Redstone, Quote: "The blast tower zaps piglins within its range, stopping them in their tracks and dealing significant damage. To collect these mysterious towers, you must build a special improvement. Knowledge built the Blast Tower a long time ago to combat forest fires but never found a use for all that energy... until now."
Player Building Melodies
Name: Blast Tower, Category: Player Building Melodies, Unlocked by: Campaign:Collecting a blast towerVersus Mode:Building Improvement Gather Redstone, Quote: "Fires projectiles that will knock back enemies on impact. Cannot be buffed by other structures. Ancient host tower. Attacks nearby enemies and sends them flying. Lost permanently if destroyed."
Other
“ Damages and knocks back piglins in an area. A Host relic created as a lightning rod to protect the Overworld from fires. „ — Blast tower map legend

title: Legends:Blast Tower Core
text:
Primary usage: Building blast towers
A blast tower core is a resource used to build blast towers that can be collected from existing blast towers.
Obtaining
In the Campaign, one blast tower core is obtained by collecting naturally generated blast towers or destroying player-built blast towers. Collecting blast towers requires 100 gold and 25 redstone to be spent, and for the collect power towers improvement to have been built at an improvement hub. A player-built blast tower can be destroyed by interacting with it. In both interactions, a gather allay is required. If a blast tower is destroyed by something other than a gather allay, such as a piglin, a blast tower core is not obtained.
In Versus Mode, blast tower cores are unobtainable.
Usage
Blast tower cores are used to create blast towers in the Campaign.
Name: Blast Tower, Cost (Campaign): 125Stone100Redstone1Blast Tower Core, Cost (Versus): 600Stone400Redstone
Quotes
Name: Blast Tower Core, Category: Resources, Unlocked by: Collecting a blast tower core, Quote: "Created by the hosts, this core contains the essence of the Blast Tower, enabling its unique properties. Upon collecting a tower found in the world or deconstructing one you've placed, you will receive its core. The more you collected, the more you can build. If a tower is destroyed, the core will be lost."
History
Minecraft Legends: 1.17.24827, Added blast tower cores.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Legends relating to "Blast Tower Core" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since January 10, 2024.
Gallery

This page describes content that is a part of the Echoing Void DLC.
Health points: 450
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Enemies
Mob category: Ender, Enderling
Attack strength: 30
Spawn: Missions End Wilds Broken Citadel Ancient Hunt Tower Summoned by Watcher of the End
A blastling is a hostile enderling found within the Echoing Void DLC. It attacks by shooting explosive purple goo from its hands. It is the only enderling that does not have an ancient variant.
Lore
There were many different subspecies of endermen that resided within the End, and it did not take the Heroes long to meet them. These subspecies were collectively known as enderlings. Blastlings were accounted as being the toughest enderlings, assailing any intruder to the End by shooting purple gobs at them from their fists. Despite this, however, the Heroes prevailed against them, making their way to the Broken Citadel, where they put a final end to the Orb of Dominance once and for all.
Blastlings have very thick arms and shiny purple fists. The purple flames lighting up their head is said to emit from their neck.
Spawning
Missions
End Wilds
Broken Citadel
Ancient Hunt
Tower
Summoned by
Watcher of the End
Behavior
Blast attack
Blastlings attack the hero by rapidly shooting purple goo from their large hands.
Teleport
If a hero gets too close to a blastling, then they will teleport away. This also applies when the blasting takes damage, in which they teleport upon being hit.
Stats
Health
Blastlings have 450 health points in the code. On Default I, all mobs have 9.6% health and 12% damage of what is set in the code.

title: Legends:Blaze Rod Tower
text:
Instructions: Add more detailed information.
Type: Piglin structure
Hordes: Horde of the Bastion Horde of the Hunt Horde of the Spore
A blaze rod tower is a piglin -built structure within Minecraft Legends. It is the piglin equivalent to arrow towers.
Spawning
Blaze rod towers spawn commonly in piglin bases and piglin outposts of all hordes. In piglin bases they are present in both the outskirts and center.
Behavior
Blaze rod towers frequently shoot singular blaze rods at any nearby non-piglin mob, setting them on fire.
Siegeworks
A blaze rod tower boosted by siegeworks.
The attack range of blaze rod towers can be increased by nearby siegeworks. This effect being active is indicated by a horned skull appeared on one side of the blaze rod tower.
Quotes
Name: Blaze Rod Tower, Category: Piglins, Unlocked by: Destroying a blaze rod tower, Quote: "The blaze rod tower is a medium-range heavy offense structure that must be dealt with from a safe distance unless you don't mind being a tiny bit on fire"
“ BLAZE ROD TOWER - After piglins themselves, blaze rod towers are the biggest threat to your survival. Stray within range of one and it will fire frequent blaze rods your way with devastating accuracy. They should be one of your first targets in every base.
TAKEDOWN TIP - Gather any skeleton allies you have and command them to open fire. In larger bases, destroy them from a safe distance using the power of a redstone launcher. „ — Minecraft Legends: A Hero's Guide to Saving the Overworld

title: Legends:Blaze Runt
text: Details Type: Piglin unit Category: Piglin Ranged Health: 10.5 Hitbox size: Height: 1.8 Blocks Width: 1.1 Blocks Voice actor: Dino Andrade Identifier: badger:piglin_grunter A blaze runt is a ranged piglin unit common to all piglin hordes in Minecraft Legends. They are better trained in combat than mace runts and wield blaze rods as weapons. Appearance Horde of the Bastion The blaze runts of the Horde of the Bastion are small piglins with white eyes that turn red at night and spicy mix-colored skin that is covered in sores. They are clothed in taupe-colored skirts. Each of their ears are decorated by an earring made of gold. They wield a blaze rod in their right hand. Horde of the Hunt The blaze runts of the Horde of the Hunt are small piglins with white eyes that turn red at night and strikemaster-colored skin that is covered in sores. They are clothed in jon-colored skirts decorated by two buttons made of bone. Each of their ears are decorated by an earring made of bone. They wield a blaze rod in their right hand. Horde of the Spore The blaze runts of the Horde of the Spore are small piglins with white eyes that turn red at night and silk-colored skin that is covered in sores. They are clothed in napa-colored bandages and wine berry-colored skirts. Their left ears are decorated by an earring made of what is presumably netherite. They are covered in warped fungi. They wield a blaze rod in their right hand. Spawning Blaze runts spawn in piglin bases and piglin outposts of every piglin horde. They are spawned by Nether Portals, piglin launchers, and piglin pits. Additionally, they spawn in groups during invasions and in every biome, with the exception of the Fatelands. Drops Blaze runts, on death, drop: 1-3 Lapis 1 Piglin Key Behavior Blaze runts idle around in the world until finding an enemy mob, enemy buildings or attacked by mobs. While idling they can be seen picking their ears with their blaze rods. During nighttime their eyes become red and will return to white once its daytime. Blaze runts throw blaze rods at their enemies to damage and set them on fire. If a blaze runt is on water, it will swim. If a blaze runt that participated in a village raid is stopped, they will run away from the village. Attacks Blaze runts have one attack type, a ranged attack in which they shoot a blaze rod which can set non-piglin mobs on fire which deal damage overtime. Name: Ranged attack, ID: grunter_ranged_attack, Damage: 1.75, Cooldown: 1 sec, Min range: 0, Max range: 15, Damage type(s): FireBlazerod Resistances and weaknesses Blaze runts can be dealt more or less damage depending on the type of damage inflicted. Damage type: Player, Multiplier: 1.3x Damage type: Poison, Multiplier: 0.6x Damage type: Slam, Multiplier: 0.45x Damage type: Poison, Multiplier: 0.25x Quotes Name: Blaze Runt, Category: Piglins, Unlocked by: Defeating a blaze runt, Quote: "Blaze Runts are better trained than mace Runts. They wield blaze rods that give them a greater range of attack. They would never win an archery contest against the skeletons, though." title: Dungeons:Blaze Spawner
text:
Behavior: Hostile
A blaze spawner is a hostile mob and a mob spawner variant within the Flames of the Nether DLC that summons blazes.
Spawning
Nether Wastes

Behavior
Blaze spawners behave similar to the spawners in the main game, but light doesn't affect the spawn rate. It produces a crackling sound while idling. Their health ranks among the highest of any regular mob. Blaze spawners cannot spawn with enchantments, however they can be enchanted by enchanters.
Summon
Blaze spawners summon blazes in huge waves whenever the hero is within a certain distance from it.

title: Dungeons:Broken Citadel
text: Part of: Other Dimensions Boss: Vengeful Heart of Ender Minimum difficulty: IV Unlock predecessor: End Wilds Raid captains: Can have captains but never all six Daily trials: Can have daily trials Time to End This! You've reached the very heart of the Void and found the last bastion of the enemy's power. It's time to end the tale of the Orb of Dominance and put a stop to this chaos. Broken Citadel is the sixth other dimensions mission in Minecraft Dungeons, is part of the Echoing Void DLC, and is the final mission of the DLC story. It is unlocked after the completion of End Wilds, and completing Broken Citadel does not unlock any missions. Broken Citadel is counted as a boss mission for Apocalypse Plus progression, due to it containing the Vengeful Heart of Ender. title: Story Mode:Blocco
text:
Details
Gender: Male
Species: Dog
Status: Alive
Residence: Fred's Keep
First appearance: Below the Bedrock
Latest appearance: Below the Bedrock
Blocco is a puppy and minor character in Minecraft: Story Mode Season Two. He first appeared in Below the Bedrock, and determinately gives a Fred fact by indirectly causing Willy to accidentally reveal the name of Fred's former pet chicken: Waffles.
Personality
Blocco is shown to be playful and loves to play fetch with people, like Jesse.
Biography

Interactions
Bone
Pick up
(Jesse picks up the bone.)
Jesse: You are... you wow. You are really cute.
Blocco
Play fetch with
(Jesse throws the bone and Blocco fetches it.)
Jesse: Oh, man. You are a serious fetch artist.
Fake out
Jesse: Go get it!
(Jesse fakes a throw, sending Blocco after nothing. He returns with a whimper.)
Pet
(Jesse pets Blocco)
Jesse: Who's a good dog? You're a good dog!
(Replaced by tug of war interaction after interacting.)
Play tug of war with
(Jesse and Blocco play tug of war with the bone.)
(Replaced by pet interaction after interacting.)
(After interacting with Willy.)
Send after chicken (replaces pet and tug of war interactions)
Jesse: Hey there little guy. I've got a game for you. Okay, you see that chicken? He loves playing chase. Go get him!
(Blocco runs toward Waffles and chases him.)
Willy: Dog! What are you-- ah, no, Waffles! Waffles come back! Come back!
(Waffles flies to a roof.)
Jesse: Waffles, huh?
Willy: Fine, you got me. It's Waffles, all right? Chicken could never make a decision... poor fella.
(While Blocco is in Waffle's enclosure)
Pick up
(Jesse waves the bone and Blocco playfully comes back. Blocco becomes interactable again.)
Appearances
Below the Bedrock
Items
Bone
Trivia
Blocco can hold items in it's mouth similar to how a fox can. This is not seen in vanilla Minecraft wolves.
Blocco was a reference to Juan Vaca's - the lead designer for Below the Bedrock - dog named "Rocco."
Gallery
Juan Vaca′s dog, Rocco, the inspiration for Blocco.
Blocco as he appears in the game.
Blocco sitting.

title: Dungeons:Blue Nethershroom
text:
Behavior: Hostile
The blue nethershroom, known in the files as the poison fungus, is a poisonous non-alive mob similar to the warped fungus in the original game albeit smaller and different colored, found in the Other Dimensions in Minecraft Dungeons.
Lore
Blue nethershrooms are fungi that naturally generate in the Warped Forest. Fungus throwers view them as the best delicacy to be found there, though they also weaponize it for its explosive toxicity against non-piglins such as heroes.
Spawning
Warped Forest
Behavior
If an entity walks over them, they explode into blue poison clouds. They also are used by fungus throwers and zombified fungus throwers due to the similar blue poison cloud as their main attack.
Sounds
Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier
Fungus Explosion,?
Poison Fungus Squeeze,?
History
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.8.0.0, Added blue nethershrooms.
Trivia
Despite being an fungus, they are still classified as mobs in the game files.
Gallery
Textures
Blue nethershroom texture file.

title: Dungeons:Tropical Slime
text:
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Aquatic
A tropical slime is an aquatic slime variant that can be found within the Hidden Depths DLC of Minecraft Dungeons.
Appearance
Tropical slimes are composed of water with two tropical fish swimming within it. Their eyes and mouth are dark blue and their body is brighter blue in color, while their outer layer is a lighter blue.
Spawning
Coral Rise
Abyssal Monument
Radiant Ravine
Ancient Hunt
Behavior
Tropical slimes can split when defeated like slimes do. They have a unique ability where they grant the hero oxygen upon defeat. Medium tropical slimes don’t split when defeated, meaning that small tropical slimes can only exclusively spawn naturally in the mission. If a tropical slime is defeated on land, it will still play the sound and animation for oxygen refilling yet it will have no effect.
Basic attack
Tropical slimes hop toward and ram the hero.
Strong attack
Sounds
New

title: Story Mode:Blueprint
text:
Instructions: Missing information.
Details
Status: Unknown
Creator: Soren
Obtained during: The Last Place You Look
Found in: Soren's Laboratory
First appearance: The Last Place You Look
Latest appearance: The Last Place You Look
A blueprint is a technical drawing that outlines the design, specifications, and construction details of an object or structure. Soren possesses a blueprint, which he intended to use for studying endermen.
History

Instructions: Rewrite it to be appropriate to a history section.
The use of a blueprint in Minecraft Story Mode is to copy the pattern on it, specifically, clay blocks on a grid located outside Soren's laboratory.
Obtaining
Episodes
The Last Place You Look — obtainable during a free-roam segment in Soren's laboratory.
Appearances
The Last Place You Look
Trivia
The blueprint was originally created by Soren to give to the endermen. He hoped that it would teach the endermen how to build.
The blueprint's pattern slightly resembles the Mojang logo, intentional or not.
When Jesse finds the blueprint, they will put it in their inventory, however, after Jesse is concussed by Soren, the blueprint will no longer be in their inventory.
It is possible that Soren took the blueprint back after concussing Jesse.

title: Story Mode:Bob
text:
Details
Gender: Male
Species: Human
Status: Alive
Residence: Beacontown
First appearance: Hero in Residence
Latest appearance: Giant Consequences
Bob is a minor character and the creator of slime-diving in Minecraft: Story Mode Season 2. He first appeared in Hero in Residence.
Appearance

Personality
Bob appears to be a joyful person as seen when he cheers Jesse when they use the slime-diving platform of their choice (determinant).
Biography

Appearances
Hero in Residence
Giant Consequences
Data values
Bob is referred to as BEACONPERSON3 in the game files.​ [ more information needed ]
Trivia
It was confirmed by Eric Stirpe that Bob's appearance is randomized, sometimes having a Caucasian skin-tone with blond hair, and another version with a brown skin-tone and black hair. This means that Bob is the only named character in the game that can have more than one skin tone regardless of the players choices.

Behavior
Main article: Rabbit § Behavior
Similar to ordinary rabbits, bold striped rabbits had the same AI behavior, wandering aimlessly, avoiding lava and cliffs that are enough to cause fall damage.

A bone spider was a type of spider that was covered in a bone texture found only in Minecraft Earth.
Behavior
Bone spiders were hostile toward players, melon golems, and furnace golems. Their melee attack dealt 3 HP. Additionally, they shot bone shards as a ranged attack.
Bone shard
Bone spiders shot bone shards at players. The player took 2 HP – 6 HP of projectile damage upon being hit.
Obtaining
Bone spiders were rare mobs that were only obtainable from epic and rare | adventure chests. They were also obtainable from the " Level 25 " buildplate.
Drops
When killed, bone spiders dropped:
0–2 string with a 35% ( 7 ⁄ 20 ) chance
0–2 bone with a 40% ( 8 ⁄ 20 ) chance
1–2 bone meal with a 25% ( 5 ⁄ 20 ) chance

title: Dungeons:Bonebow
text:
Type: Ranged Weapon
Rarity: UNIQUE
Power: 4.2
Speed: 3.7
Ammo: 6.6
Properties: Arrows grow size
Enchantment: Growing
Damage type: Ranged
Soul information: Does not accept soul enchantments Does not grant soul collection
Runes

In-game description The Bonebow is the pride of Pumpkin Pastures, crafted within the walls of their humble village.
The bonebow is a unique bow variant that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Missions
A before a mission name indicates that the item is only obtainable in Daily Trials of that mission.
Creeper Woods
Creepy Crypt
Desert Temple
Dingy Jungle (DLC)
Gale Sanctum (DLC)
Tower
Treetop Tangle
Merchants
Gift Wrapper
Mystery Merchant
Luxury Merchant
Piglin Merchant
Ancients
Barrage
Usage
The bonebow possesses low power, low speed, and a medium ammunition supply. The quiver starts with 80 arrows, normal arrow bundles give 40 arrows, supply chest bundles give another 80 arrows, and Surprise Gift bundles give 20 arrows.
Bows can be shot immediately for the minimum damage or charged for maximum damage if the ranged attack button is held down. Ranged weapons require the hero to have at least one normal arrow or a special arrow from a quiver artifact in their inventory to shoot. Heroes can also cancel shots by using the melee attack button and releasing the ranged attack button while the melee button is still held.
Damage caused by the arrows can be increased with enchantments, the Strength buff, and certain armors that have ranged damage increases, such as hunter's armor.

title: Dungeons:Bonus Shot
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Ranged weapon
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Firing a shot also fires a second shot at a nearby enemy. The second shot has reduced damage.
Bonus Shot is a ranged weapon enchantment in Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 10% damage per shot, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: 17% damage per shot, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: 24% damage per shot, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Bonus Shot fires a second shot at a nearby enemy when shooting a ranged weapon. The second shot always deals less damage than the primary shot. The amount of damage the second arrow deals increases with each tier, at the maximum tier the second shot deal 24% of the damage the primary shot would do.
If only one mob is in proximity to the hero, Bonus Shot is not activated, making it useless against any boss that doesn't summon additional mobs.
Stacking
Bonus Shot is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Built-into
Bonus Shot tier I is built-into the following weapons:
Butterfly Crossbow
Haunted Bow
Twin Bow
Mob
Enchanted mobs with the enchantment will fire an additional shot when shooting.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Bonus Shot.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Bonus Shot" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.

title: Story Mode:Boom Town
text: Details Type: Settlement Leader: Magnus ​ [ formerly (determinant) ] Axel First appearance: Assembly Required Latest appearance: Assembly Required Boom Town is a town full of griefers and ruined buildings. It was created by Magnus the Rogue. It is determinantly traveled to in Assembly Required to recruit Magnus. However, this also caused the town to be destroyed by the wither storm. History If Jesse chooses to go to Boom Town in Assembly Required, they, along with Reuben and Axel, fall to the ground after exiting the floating Nether portal. While navigating Boom Town, they inadvertently trigger numerous traps. Using the amulet to locate Magnus, they encounter griefers who steal it. After a chase, they successfully retrieve the amulet and proceed to Magnus' tower. Jesse convinces Magnus to join their effort to stop the wither storm. As part of their plan, Jesse and Magnus stage a match in the Death Bowl, which results in Jesse being declared the new King of Boom Town. However, shortly after, the wither storm arrives, destroying the town and absorbing its inhabitants. If Jesse chooses to go to Redstonia instead, Axel travels to Boom Town alone to recruit Magnus. Upon succeeding, Axel is declared the King of Boom Town. In Hero in Residence, Boom Town is hosting its own Founding Day celebration, where Axel serves as a judge for a TNT Rally. If Magnus died, they annually honor him with explosions. If he did not, he let Axel design the fireworks finale in the year Season Two took place. Location Boom Town is located in a desert biome somewhere to the west in the overworld, as shown on the Order's tracking device's map. It can be easily traveled to by using the nether minecart system. Architecture Boom Town is set in a desert biome with many ruined buildings, due to the many griefers. The buildings are mainly made of bricks, stone bricks, oak planks and fences, oak logs, windows, and acacia stairs. A nether portal serves as one of the Os in Boom Town's title "Boom Town" with the rest of the letters made of white or red wool. Culture Boom Town is characterized by its anarchic and competitive culture, with a strong emphasis on explosions and destruction as central elements of daily life. The residents exhibit a high tolerance for risk-taking, often engaging in activities involving TNT and other volatile materials. Explosions occur frequently and are considered a defining feature of the town's identity. Administration Boom Town operates without a formal system of governance, relying instead on an anarchic structure where individuals act independently. Despite this, Magnus the Rogue is recognized as the "King of Boom Town," a title reflecting his status as the town's most respected and influential figure. His title can be claimed by anyone who defeats him in a challenge known as the Death Bowl, a competition that embodies the town's values of chaos and destruction. Magnus frequently hides within Boom Town, and locating him is a prerequisite for issuing a challenge. If Jesse visits Boom Town during the events of Assembly Required, they defeat Magnus and become the new king. Alternatively, if Jesse chooses to visit Redstonia, Axel claims the title after defeating Magnus. In Season Two, if Magnus has died, Axel is presumed to have taken over as the new leader of Boom Town. Points of interest Magnus' Tower Main article: Magnus' Tower The towering structure where Magnus resides, keeping himself hidden from all the griefers in Boom Town. Death Bowl Main article: Death Bowl An event in Boom Town where challengers face off against the reigning King of Boom Town in a battle for the title. Interactables Axel Talk to Axel: Well, here you have it, Jesse. Boom Town in all its glory! What do you think? It’s like I’ve finally come home. These are my people. And listen to that... They speak my language! We should keep moving. Jesse: We need to keep moving. They probably saw us come in here. Axel: Yeah, Boom Town's not exactly made for loitering. It's pretty exciting! Jesse: Wow... Boom Town sure does keep you on your toes. Exciting stuff, huh? Axel: Yeah! Yeah! Now you’re getting it! Axel, this is nuts! Jesse: Augh! Axel! This is place is absolutely crazy! Axel: You're being too small-minded, Jesse! This is awesome! Jesse: Axel, you’d think a poisonous potato was awesome if it had TNT strapped to it. ... Axel: Don’t worry, Jesse. You’ve got me and I totally know how to roll with these guys. Banner Look at (Looks at the banner depicting the amulet and pulls out the amulet itself.) Axel: Dude, it’s freakin’ glowing! Dude, I think you’re onto something there... I know what this means. Jesse: This glow... I think I know what it means! It's done this before. Jesse:

title: Earth:Boost
text:
Boosts menu icon
Boosts scan icon
Boosts in Minecraft Earth
A boost in Minecraft Earth was an enhancement to a player ability. A boost's duration and intensity depended on its level. A player was able to spend rubies in the store to purchase boosts.
Effects
Boosts were available as levels I, II, and III. The level of the boost determined its duration, intensity (if applicable), and cost.
Level I boosts lasted 10 minutes. One level I boost was able to be purchased for 2 rubies.
Level II boosts lasted 15 minutes. Five level II boosts were able to be purchased for 19 rubies (3.8 rubies per boost)
Level III boosts lasted 30 minutes. Three level III boosts were able to be purchased for 25 rubies (8.3 rubies per boost)
Boost effects and their intensities were as follows:
Boost name: Adventure XP Boost, Description: Increased XP fromadventures, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: +50%, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: +75%, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: +100%
Boost name: Attack Boost, Description: Increased attack damage, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: +25%, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: +50%, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: +100%
Boost name: Keeper Boost, Description: Kept the hotbar contents and XP in the event of death, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: -, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: -, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: -
Boost name: Mob XP Boost, Description: Increased XP from collecting mobs from themap, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: +50%, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: +75%, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: +100%
Boost name: Defense Boost, Description: Took less damage when attacked, as if wearing armor, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: +25%, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: +50%, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: +100%
Boost name: Food Boost, Description: Healed more from eating food, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: +50%, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: +150%, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: +300%
Boost name: Hoarding Boost, Description: Preserved backpack contents and XP in the event of death, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: -, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: -, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: -
Boost name: Health Boost, Description: Temporarily increased maximum health, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: +10%, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: +20%, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: +30%
Boost name: Mining Boost, Description: Increased mining speed for a short time, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: +25%, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: +50%, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: +100%
Boost name: Radius Boost, Description: Increased the interaction radius on the map, Level I10 minutes1 for 2 rubies: +35m, Level II15 minutes5 for 19 rubies: +53m, Level III30 minutes3 for 25 rubies: +70m
There was also another set of unused boost icons with experience orbs on them:
Boost minis
Boost minis artwork
Boost minis picture
Boosts could also be obtained by scanning the undersides of collectible figurines called boost minis. There were 24 boost minis to collect, with each one providing a different type of level 1 boost. Each boost mini had an 18 hour cooldown period before it could be used again.
Attacking Alex(Attack Boost): Enraged Golem(Defense Boost), Attacking Steve 1(Attack Boost): Fishing Polar Bear(Food Boost), Attacking Steve 2(Attack Boost): Future Chicken Jockey(Mob XP Boost), Crafting Steve(Crafting Boost): Healing Witch(Health Boost), Crafting Villager(Crafting Boost): Hoarding Skeleton(Keeper Boost), Defending Alex(Defense Boost): Mining Creeper(Mining Boost)
Attacking Alex(Attack Boost): Pigging Out Pig(Health Boost), Attacking Steve 1(Attack Boost): Poison Enderman(Attack Boost), Attacking Steve 2(Attack Boost): Regenerating Mooshroom(Health Boost), Crafting Steve(Crafting Boost): Repairing Villager(Crafting Boost), Crafting Villager(Crafting Boost): Seeking Dolphin(Radius Boost), Defending Alex(Defense Boost): Seeking Wolf(Radius Boost)
Attacking Alex(Attack Boost): Slowed Creeper(Adventure XP Boost), Attacking Steve 1(Attack Boost): Smelting Blaze(Smelting Boost), Attacking Steve 2(Attack Boost): Snacking Rabbit 1(Health Boost), Crafting Steve(Crafting Boost): Snacking Rabbit 2(Health Boost), Crafting Villager(Crafting Boost): Spawning Chicken(Mob XP Boost), Defending Alex(Defense Boost): Undying Evoker(Hoarding Boost)
Placeholder boost mini icons are present in the files of earlier versions:

title: Earth:Bouldering Zombie
text:
Health points: 20 HP × 10
Behavior: Hostile
Mob type: Undead, Monster
Attack strength: 3 HP
Hitbox size: Height: 1.9 blocks Width: 0.6 blocks
Spawn: Plains
Rarity: Rare
Player journal description What is scarier than death itself? Death that can climb!
The bouldering zombie was a gray-skinned variant of the zombie capable of climbing up blocks, found only in Minecraft Earth.
Behavior
Bouldering zombies mostly acted like normal zombies in terms of damage and behavior, attacking players, mobs of me, furnace golems, and melon golems by walking up to and attacking them. However, they were also capable of scaling up blocks like a spider. This behavior was triggered when they saw a player above blocks, where they then proceeded to climb the blocks and then attack the player.
Obtaining
Bouldering zombies were rare mob drops that were only obtainable from rare and epic adventure chests. They were also obtainable as a reward in Season 15.
Drops
When killed, the Bouldering Zombie dropped:
0-2 Rotten Flesh
Potato (2.5% chance of dropping)
Carrot (2.5% chance of dropping)
Iron Ingot (2.5% chance of dropping)

History
Minecraft Earth: 0.30.0, Added bouldering zombies.
Trivia
The biome category of the journal revealed that bouldering zombies spawned in plains.
It, along with the lobber zombie, were the first and only zombie variants exclusive to Minecraft Earth.
Unlike other zombie variants in different games, they were given unique and slightly altered models, with their arms having different widths and lengths, and unique animations for throwing and climbing.
The bouldering zombie was originally leaked on the official Minecraft website as the "Climbing Zombie", and was said to be added alongside the viler witch, but was later removed from the article where this was mentioned.
Bouldering is the sport of climbing small rock formations or artificial rock walls without ropes or harnesses.

title: Legends:Bouncecaps
text:
Type: Boost
Biomes: Jagged Peaks Jungle Swamp
Bouncecaps are a type of boost that increase jump height in Minecraft Legends.
Spawning
Bouncecaps can be found on the floors and treetops of jungles and swamps, as well as on the side of mountains in jagged peaks.
Usage
When traveling over bouncecaps, an effect is granted to the player that makes their jump height much higher than before. All fall damage is negated while the player is under the bouncecaps' effect. However, the effect is immediately removed when coming in contact with poison vines.
Quotes
Name: Bouncecaps, Category: Overworld, Unlocked by: Interacting with bouncecaps, Quote: "Bouncecaps let you jump higher, allowing you to reach the treetops and scale mountains easier. Just make sure their effect doesn't wear down by the time you land."
“ BOUNCECAPS – These brightly colored mushrooms give off spores that are lighter than air. Touch one and you’ll be able to jump to extreme heights and won’t take any fall damage – perfect for maneuvering on mountains! „ — Minecraft Legends:
A Hero's Guide to Saving the Overworld

title: Earth:Build Challenges
text: Results of The Sunset Build Challenge. Build Challenges were the events that took place once every few weeks and consisted in the creation of buildings on a specific theme by players in Minecraft Earth. Description Over the entire history of the game, 27 build challenges were held in the Minecraft Earth Discord server, where players built structures on various themes. Guidelines All build challenges had their own general rules. First of all, the building had to be constructed by the player who represented it on the Minecraft Earth server channel. Also, the building must be built in Minecraft Earth, and nowhere else. Secondly, the building had to be constructed from scratch and for this particular challenge. Thirdly, images or videos of the building had to be sent to the channel of the respective challenge by the deadline. Finally, images or videos could be used for Minecraft Earth social media posts. List of Build Challenges 8 Bit Statue Challenge “ Boy, we have a lot of stone! Bet you do too! Let’s get creative! And make it retro too! Gather all your stone and show everyone what you’ve got! Build your best stone sculpture in the 8-bit style! Channel your inner Michaelangelo and create a stone statue, only this time in 8-bit! „ — 8 Bit Status Challenge description The challenge was held from November 7, 2019 to November 21, 2019. There is no information about this challenge on minecraft.net. There is also no definite winner and no selected buildings. Below are links to YouTube videos and images of some of the buildings. Doowi — The Statue Of Liberty. WhiteHoodHacker — Diorite Danger. Pokeforcegames's building. 15 minute Speed Challenge “ In celebration of Build plate Sharing, I thought we could have a challenge for all share in! In a few moments you will be provided with a Shared Plate, and a build subject. Everyone will have 15 minutes to build and Take screen shots or video recordings of these temporary creations and submit to the Judges. May your plates be steady and tapping fingers ready! „ — 15 minute Speed Challenge description The challenge was held in three rounds, namely from April 17, 2020 to April 18, 2020, from April 18, 2020 to April 19, 2020, and June 06, 2020. There is no information about this challenge on minecraft.net. Since it was a speed challenge, it had its own unique rules: All participates may only use the shared buildplate and hotbar provided. Since shared plates cannot save, player must complete in one go! Picture or video submissions must be sent by the allotted deadline. The winners were: The first - Frahst - Pink Tulip Lover. The second - NinjaK. The third - WhiteHoodHacker, who built the building called "RC car and remote:p". The buildiing by Frahst - Pink Tulip Lover. The buildiing by NinjaK. WhiteHoodHacker — RC car and remote:p. 15 minute Speed Challenge Season 2 “ Its been a while but we're back So get your creative brains ready for another speed build challenge „ — 15 minute Speed Challenge Season 2 description The challenge was held in two rounds, namely November 14, 2020, and November 21, 2020. There is no information about this challenge on minecraft.net. There is also no definite winner and no selected buildings. Below are images of some of the buildings. Round 1 The buildiing by Shiro. The buildiing by Thanks MCE for great memories. Round 2 The buildiing by NinjaK. The buildiing by JakubK027. Blocktastic mobs Build Challenge “ Cows with flowers! Snowmen with melon heads! Minecraft Earth is full of unique mobs, but what makes up the blocky DNA of our quirky friends? Imagination of course! Design your own unique mob that showcases your creativity and then build it! Use any and all blocks and items in your inventory to bring your creation to life. Is your mob big or small? Does it reside in a large city or roam the forest? Fantastic abilities? You decide! „ — Blocktastic mobs Build Challenge description The challenge was held from April 28, 2020 to May 11, 2020. WhiteHoodHacker's building called "Pastry Panda" became famous. It was featured on the cover of a minecraft.net article. WhiteHoodHacker — Pastry Panda. Ditto. Chicken Coop Challenge “ Do you have a Cluckshroom or two? And how about all those regular chickens!? Let's give them a home. But not just any home. Show us your fanciest chicken coop for all your fancy (and not so fancy) chickens! „ — Chicken Coop Challenge description This is the second build challenge in the history of Minecraft Earth. The challenge was held from October 10, 2019 to October 24, 2019. There is no information about this challenge on minecraft.net. There is also no definite winner and no selected buildings. Below are links to YouTube

title: Earth:Buildplate
text: The buildplates icon. Buildplates in Minecraft Earth. A buildplate was an area in Minecraft Earth in which the player was able to build structures. It was the creative mode component of the game, although it also offered a survival mode. [ better source needed ] Obtaining A new player had an 8×8 buildplate available by default. The player selected a surface in the real world on which to place the buildplate, and once placed, the player saw the buildplate superimposed on the real-world camera view. An open space and a flat surface were required for buildplates to be placed. Players were also able to interact with the items or mobs in their buildplate. The player was able to physically move around the buildplate to view it from different angles and to build structures. As the player advanced in level, new buildplates became available at different sizes, up to 32×32. A unique new buildplate was unlocked upon reaching levels 1, 5, 10, 15, 20 and 25. The player was also able to spend rubies to purchase buildplates in the store. List of buildplates Level Level 1 Level 5 Level 10 Level 15 Level 20 Level 25 Store Chill Spot Cluck House Dark and Shroomy Highland Grazing Lava In The Jungle A Bit Fancy Colourful Cottage Highland House Jungle Treehouse Midnight bayou Not A Sandcastle Roadside Inn Sand Sanctuary Secluded Mine Sleepy Village Tiny Waterfall Ahoy Toadstool Terrace Witch's Mansion Heart Of The Jungle Giant Jungle Gritty Grandeur Starting Small Wide Woods Scale Default scale (creative) By default, when the buildplate was set on a real-world surface, it appeared sized as a square about 58 centimeters (23 inches) on each side for an 8×8 buildplate, resulting in a scale of about 1:14. A 32×32 buildplate appeared as about 50 centimeters (20 inches) per side, resulting in a scale of 1:64. Full scale (survival) Before placing the buildplate, the player had the option to place it at full scale. At full scale, the player was in survival mode with only their configured hotbar for items. This was similar to playing an adventure. If the player was playing a shared buildplate, the hotbar provided by the owner was used and cannot be changed. On a full-scale buildplate, the player was able to be burned by lava, and any hostile mobs on the buildplate attacked the player. Multiplayer Players were able to collaborate on a buildplate. A player who placed a buildplate was able to select "Invite Friend", which allowed another player to scan a QR code to join. title: Dungeons:Burst Bowstring
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Ranged weapon (excluding crossbows)
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Shoots nearby mobs when you roll, costing one arrow per roll. Projectiles deal 40% of a charged shot's damage.
Burst Bowstring is an enchantment that can be found within the various ranged weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 1 target, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: 2 targets, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: 3 targets, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Burst Bowstring shoots up to three nearby mobs when the hero rolls, costing one arrow per roll. The arrows fired are affected by enchantments and artifacts that change the type of arrow fired. It can only be applied to bows, not crossbows.
It is considered a rolling trigger enchantment, which means it is activated by the light feather artifact.
Rolling does not shoot any arrows when the hero is carrying TNT.
Stacking
Burst Bowstring is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.4.3.0, Added Burst Bowstring.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Burst Bowstring" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.
Gallery
Burst Bowstring's enchantment icon.
The shine pattern for Burst Bowstring's enchantment icon.

title: Dungeons:Busy Bee
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Melee Weapon
In-game description Chance to summon a bee after defeating a mob, with up to 3 bees joining your side.
Busy Bee is an enchantment that can be found within the various melee weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Busy Bee has a chance to appear in any enchantment slot on a melee weapon. Enchantments on items can be swapped out for Busy Bee by rerolling them at the enchantsmith.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 20% chance to trigger, Cost: 1 enchantment point, Cost (Gilded): 2 enchantment points
Tier: II, Effect: 30% chance to trigger, Cost: 2 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 3 enchantment points
Tier: III, Effect: 40% chance to trigger, Cost: 3 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 4 enchantment points
Built-into
Busy bee tier I is built into the following weapon:
Bee Stinger
Mobs
Mobs can never be enchanted with Busy Bee.
Stacking
Busy Bee is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Usage
Any melee weapon enchanted with busy bee has a chance to summon a bee after defeating a mob. The chance of this happening increases with each tier, at the maximum tier there is a 40% chance. The bee spawns at the point where the weapon deals damage, similar to gravity
Achievements
Achievement: A, B, Bee, Bee, Bee, Bee, Bee, In-game description: Summon 15 Bees at once., Actual requirements (if different): —, Gamerscore earned: 50G, Trophy type (PS): Bronze
History
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.4.3.0, Added Busy Bee.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Busy Bee" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.

The buttercup was included with the moobloom in the Minecraft Live 2020 mob vote. Neither are currently planned to be added to Minecraft.

In-game description This crossbow moves faster than wings in flight and strikes down enemies before they see the bolt.
The butterfly crossbow is a unique rapid crossbow variant that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Missions

A before a mission name indicates that the item is only obtainable in Daily Trials of that mission.

Usage
The butterfly crossbow possesses very low power, high speed, and a large ammo supply. The quiver starts with 133 arrows, normal arrow bundles give 66 arrows, supply chest bundles give another 133 arrows, and Surprise Gift bundles give 33 arrows.
Crossbows can be shot repeatedly if the ranged attack button is held down. Ranged weapons require the hero to have at least one normal arrow or a special arrow from a quiver artifact in their inventory to shoot. Unlike bows, shots from crossbows cannot be charged.
Damage caused by the arrows can be increased with enchantments, the Strength buff, the cowardice armor enchantment, and certain armor have ranged damage increases, such as hunter's armor.

title: Dungeons:Cacti Canyon
text: Part of: Mainland Boss (arcade): Giant Cave Spider Minimum difficulty: II Unlock predecessor: Creeper Woods Raid captains: Can very rarely have all six captains Daily trials: Can have daily trials Lost in a Sea of Sand Those who wish to reach the desert have to traverse the winding paths and trails of Cacti Canyon. A complex network of tunnels, chasms, and caves have led even the most level-headed adventurers astray. Those who wish to survive this sandy labyrinth must rely on their wits. Cacti Canyon is the seventh mainland mission in Minecraft Dungeons. It is unlocked after the completion of Creeper Woods, and completing Cacti Canyon unlocks Desert Temple. It contains the captured luxury merchant. Appearance and description “ CACTI CANYON – The Arch-Illager seeks to summon armies of the Undead using a power that rests deep within an ancient desert temple. Finding the temple, however, is an adventure in its own right. The entrance lies hidden somewhere in this canyon - a sprawling maze of paths filled with malevolent mobs and lost secrets. „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons: A Handbook for Heroes Cacti Canyon is an expansive, dusty landscape made up of a jagged canyon and a desert. The canyon portion features uneven terrain and a mix of grass and coarse dirt, while the desert portion is composed of flatter terrain and a mix of sand and coarse dirt. Despite the steep terrain and dry conditions, cows and sheep can still be found roaming here. In the desert portion of this mission, husks wander around and fossils made of quartz are encountered as the hero approaches the temple in the distance. Halfway through the mission, a blue locked gateway must be unlocked using a diamond key golem guarded by an ambush found to the south-west. Near the diamond key golem, a hidden button can be activated to reveal the entrance door of Cacti Canyon Rune Room. Upon unlocking the gateway to the north-east using the diamond key golem, a gold key golem can be found that must be brought back to the yellow locked gateway at the center of the area. Near the end of the mission, an ambush must be defeated to reach the distant temple. The mission ends upon opening the entrance of the temple. title: Dungeons:Camp
text: Part of: Mainland Unlock predecessor: Squid Coast Unused in-game story A place for rest and reflection. Camp is a location in Minecraft Dungeons, and is the main hub area of the game. It is unlocked after the completion of Squid Coast. Appearance and description “ YOUR CAMP – You were too late to save the Villagers at Squid Coast, but all is not lost if you have anything to do with it. Your next step is to set up camp just outside the village – this is where you will return after completing each mission. Take some time to look around, for there is more here than you might expect… „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons: A Handbook for Heroes Camp is set atop a mountainous area next to a forest, and is located north-west of the burning village in Squid Coast. The mission select map can be found here, which is used to access the major locations of the game. Camp contains various shops operated by camp merchants, including the blacksmith, luxury merchant, mystery merchant, village merchant, gift wrapper, enchantsmith, and piglin merchant. The shops operated by villagers only appear after rescuing their merchants from various mainland missions, and the piglin merchant only appears after completing Ancient Hunt at least once. Camp contains The Tower, Home, Great Hall, and Nether Room. Various emerald chests spawn around Camp after the completion of each mainland mission, with each chest containing fifty emeralds. title: Legends:Campaign
text:
“ CAMPAIGN – Your journey starts just after the piglin invasion has begun. Their attacks will show up on your map and you must race to defeat their hordes. Be warned — they won't give up. Their nightly invasions will grow more ferocious and their bases will expand. Your determination alone won't be enough, so you'll have to upgrade your abilities and call on mobs to join the fight to save the Overworld. „ — Minecraft Legends:
A Hero's Guide to Saving the Overworld
The Campaign is one of the Play Modes. It follows the storyline of Minecraft Legends.
Gameplay
In Campaign, players are in the main storyline of the game. In this mode, you are tasked to stop the piglin invasion and save the overworld with the help of player units that can be spawned by the legendary lute and controlled by the banner of courage. The campaign is won by defeating the Great Hog after destroying all piglin bases.
Custom Game
Main article: Minecraft Legends:Custom Game
Custom Game is the mode created when Custom Game settings are used which can be used to customize the campaign based on the player's preferences and can be used to greatly help out the player or make the game harder. Various settings can only be enabled when playing Campaign.

title: Dungeons:The Rise of the Arch-Illager
text:
Author(s): Matt Forbeck
Audio read by: Dan Bittner
Cover artist: M.S. Corley
Country: United States
Language: English
Genre(s): Fiction
Set in: Minecraft Dungeons
Publisher: Del Rey Books
Release date: July 7, 2020 (North America) July 9, 2020 (UK)
Media type: Print, Digital
Pages: 240
ISBN: 978-0-399-180811-1
OCLC: 1124523530
Minecraft Dungeons: The Rise of the Arch-Illager is a Minecraft Dungeons novel. It is authored by Matt Forbeck and was published by Del Rey Books on July 7, 2020 in North America and July 9, 2020 in the United Kingdom. An audiobook was also released on July 7, 2020, with narration provided by Dan Bittner.
The novel expands upon the opening cinematic in the game, acting as a prequel.
Synopsis
Brave heroes have banded together to take a perilous journey through the war-riven overworld to defeat the Arch-Illager and his formidable army. But how did that army come to be? And just where did the Arch-Illager come from?
The terrible truth behind the Arch-Illager is that he never asked for ultimate power. Known as Archie, this little Illager is bullied by his fellow Illagers and mistrusted by fearful Villagers. Archie only ever wanted a place to call home but he finds himself shunned by all. As he wanders through deep forests and up craggy mountains, he stumbles upon a dark cavern—with a sinister secret waiting inside. Archie discovers an object which whispers to him promises of power: the Orb of Dominance. With it, Archie realizes he can wield incredible magic and reshape a world that turned its back on him. All he needs to do is exactly what it tells him.
After all, it’s called the Orb of Dominance for a reason, right? But is it named for the way Archie uses it—or is it using him?
Plot
Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Dungeons: The Rise of the Arch-Illager that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk!

title: Dungeons:Card
text: Add series 2 and 3 variants of series 1 card images (their colors and artwork are slightly different) Images of card list from Series 4 & 5 Needed A promotional banner listing all 118 Series 3 cards for the Minecraft Dungeons Arcade game. A card is a part of a game mechanic in Minecraft Dungeons Arcade, the arcade adaptation of Minecraft Dungeons. Each time a player starts or continues a game, a card is dispensed beneath their control panel, which can have its barcode scanned on the machine's scanners to equip a player with specific weapons, armor, pets and heroes. There are a total of 144 cards to collect: 60 base cards in the first series, an additional 38 in the second series, an additional 20 in the third series, an additional 25 in the fourth series, and an additional 1 card in the fifth series. Each card also has a foil version, although they have no extra effects on gameplay. All series 3 cards are hero cards which are always distinguished by gold foil accents and contain items and cosmetics for the melee, range, armor, skin, and pet slots. There is a 10% chance for a hero card to be dispensed when the machine dispenses a card. title: Legends:Carpenter Hut
text:
Type: Structure
Cost: Campaign: 150 Wood 150 Stone Versus Mode: 250 Wood 125 Stone
A carpenter hut is a structure that heals other structures within Minecraft Legends.
Obtaining
Campaign
Carpenter huts can be built by selecting the carpenter hut within the hotbar and using a build allay. Carpenter huts cost 150 wood and 150 stone each.
Versus
Carpenter huts cost 250 wood and 125 stone to build.
Usage

Instructions: add more information about the usage of carpenter huts and more specific info about improvements
When built, carpenter huts create a large zone around them. This is indicated by blue particles surrounding the targeted structure and a reduction in the cracked texture. Any other buildings in this zone will slowly be repaired if they are damaged. If a damaged building is in the zone of two carpenter huts, its healing rate will not be increased. However, if an expert carpentry improvement is built, all carpenter huts will have their repair speed increased.
Quotes
Name: Carpenter Hut, Category: Player Building Melodies, Unlocked by: From the start, Quote: "Repairs nearby structures within its zone. Healing effects cannot stack with additional carpenter huts but can allow you to heal structures in a wider area. The carpenter hut affects most structures within the zone it creates. These structures will slowly repair any damage taken over time. If a building is destroyed, the carpenter hut cannot repair it."

title: Story Mode:Cassie Rose
text:
Note: Missing information
Details
Gender: Female
Species: Human
Status: Alive
Aliases: White Pumpkin
Residence: Spooky Mansion
Allies: See § Allies
Enemies: See § Enemies
First appearance: A Portal to Mystery
Latest appearance: Hero in Residence
Actor: Ashly Burch Roger Jackson (White Pumpkin)
Cassie Rose​ is a character in Minecraft: Story Mode and the hidden main antagonist in A Portal to Mystery. She has a pet cat named Winslow. She is voiced by Ashly Burch. She also has a persona, the serial murderer known only as the White Pumpkin.
Appearance

Cassie Rose first appears in the creepy house in the episode " A Portal to Mystery ". Cassie Rose appears to wear a black shirt and blue shorts. She has red hair, green eyes, and wears a blue cap along with black glasses. Next to her is her cat named Winslow. During the murder of Captain Sparklez, she shows herself as a girl who is ready to leave this house just to not participate in it.
Personality
Cassie Rose is portrayed to be a grumpy, suspicious individual who seems to be very frightened as well. She also cares very deeply for her cat Winslow.
She also appears to be a somewhat friendly, yet fearful, individual who is good friends with the YouTubers. Her aggressive nature is also hinted at, as shown when she suggests throwing Lukas outside to be eaten by zombies, much to the YouTubers' shock.
White Pumpkin
"Story Mode:The White Pumpkin" redirects here. For the block, see Story Mode:White Pumpkin.
Her persona, the White Pumpkin killer, is shown to lean more into aggressive tendencies by being willing to kill to get what she wants. When the White Pumpkin is revealed to be Cassie Rose, she states that she was trapped in the mansion’s world for a very long time. Her time in their world likely caused her to be in her current mental state.
Biography

Cassie Rose was not originally from the Spooky Mansion's Overworld. Before the events of A Portal to Mystery, she was kicked by the Old Builders into a portal that leads to an Overworld with two moons.
In Season Two, she had written a letter to Jesse that said, "Look out!" with the sender's initials as "W. P." Jesse will only be notified about the letter by Radar if they stayed in Beacontown in Above and Beyond.
Relationships
Allies
Winslow

Enemies

Everyone in the Spooky Mansion

Trivia
Cassie Rose got her name from Cassie Rose Stirpe, the sister of Eric Stirpe, a Telltale Games staff member.
Cassie Stirpe's pet dog is also called Winslow.
It was confirmed that Cassie Rose's last name is not "Rose."
The White Pumpkin's appearance is a reference to the character Ghostface, an identity that primary antagonists take in the Scream franchise. The White Pumpkin is also voiced by Roger L. Jackson, who voiced the Ghostface identity.
In a pre-release version of Access Denied, leftover textures for a tuxedoed White Pumpkin can be found. This design was reused in a portrait in the living room.
Within Eric Stirpe 's headcanon, Cassie Rose had the cover story of being a television star from an obscure channel.

title: Dungeons:Corrupted Cauldron
text:
Health points: 7000
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Enemies
Mob category: Boss
Attack strength: Poison cloud: 75 Sorrow (death): 1000 base damage to hostile mob
Spawn: Soggy Swamp ( boss ) Daily Trial Tower ( boss )
The Corrupted Cauldron, or simply The Cauldron, is the first boss of the game. It is encountered at the end of Soggy Swamp. The boss attacks by generating a ring fire around itself and by summoning enemies such as the conjured slime.
Spawning
Soggy Swamp ( boss )
Daily Trial
Tower ( boss )
Drops
Main article: MCD:Drops § Soggy Swamp
Armor: Evocation RobeEmber RobeVerdant RobeMystery Armor, Artifacts: Fishing RodHarvesterLightning RodTotem of Regeneration, Melee weapons: DaggersFangs of FrostMoon DaggersSheer DaggersDouble AxeCursed AxeWhirlwindGlaiveGrave BaneVenom GlaiveSoul KnifeEternal KnifeTruthseeker, Ranged weapons: Hunting BowAncient BowHunter's PromiseMaster's BowScatter CrossbowLightning Harp CrossbowHarp Crossbow
Behavior
The Corrupted Cauldron has three behaviors. It does not move during its battle, even if struck by a Gravity -enchanted weapon.
Basic attack
The Corrupted Cauldron creates a ring of purple fire on the mossy cobblestone blocks around it, which deals heavy damage to anything that steps in it. Used occasionally.
Intense spin
The Corrupted Cauldron, after a stirring animation, conjures a variety of dangerous mobs to join the battle.
Strong attack
The Corrupted Cauldron sucks up the corpses of any minion that is not a conjured slime. Once the corpse of a mob is inside of the Corrupted Cauldron, the cauldron consumes it, regenerating 1% of its total health. It is somewhat similar to the leeching enchantment.
Minions
These minions are summoned by the Corrupted Cauldron. Minions are related with their own summoner.
The Corrupted Cauldron can only absorb its own minions' corpses except conjured slimes. For example, in the tower, the Corrupted Cauldron does not suck up drowned corpses (summoned by drowned necromancer ). Minions within 20 blocks are killed by the Corrupted Cauldron if it is defeated.

The Cauldron produces magical purple fire that deals damage to any who touches it and also conjures up a variety of dangerous mobs to join the fray. Most disturbingly, it can consume dead mobs to regenerate its health! Defeat minor mobs before turning a ranged weapon on the Cauldron itself. „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons:

title: Dungeons:Conjured Slime
text:
Health points: 150 Life time:10 seconds
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Enemies
Attack strength: 60
Spawn: Summoned by the Corrupted Cauldron in the following: Soggy Swamp Daily Trial Tower
A conjured slime, internally referred to as pink slime, is a variant of slime that is summoned by the Corrupted Cauldron. Unlike other slimes, it remains stationary and attacks the player with projectiles.
Spawning
Conjured slimes are summoned by the Corrupted Cauldron during its boss battle. The Corrupted Cauldron spawns in the following:
Soggy Swamp
Daily Trial
Tower
Behavior
Conjured slimes are immobile, preferring to stay in one spot and attack heroes from a distance. They do not have a jump behavior, unlike normal slimes.
Medium attack
Conjured slimes attack heroes on-sight by shooting small purple projectiles that deal moderate damage upon being hit.
Conjured slime's projectiles only deal 20% damage to the Corrupted Cauldron.
Sounds
Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier

title: Dungeons:Cool Down
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Armor
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Reduces the cooldown time between uses of your artifacts.
Cool Down is an enchantment that can be found within the various armor of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: -18% reduced cooldown, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: -29% reduced cooldown, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: -38% reduced cooldown, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
The cool down enchantment decreases the cooldown time on artifacts. The duration decreases more with each tier, at the maximum tier artifact cooldowns are reduced by 38%.
Stacking
Cool Down is stackable, meaning it can appear in multiple enchantment slots on an item. If Cool Down appears in the gilded slot of an item, it never appears in the other three enchantment slots, although by rerolling the item's enchantments using the enchantsmith, Cool Down has the chance to appear even if it appears in the gilded slot.
When stacked it has a diminishing effect, meaning each extra Cool Down is less effective than the last.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Cool Down.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.8.0.0, Now stacks less effectively.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.17.0.0, Now able to be stacked four times by rerolling with theenchantsmith.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Cool Down" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.
Trivia
The theoretical maximum cooldown would be 91.1% (Gilded Evocation Robe with built in Cool Down 3 and Cool Down 3 in all three enchantment slots), but actually it would be 84.1%.

title: Dungeons:Cellar
text:
This topic is named based on the topic's music track.
Not to be confused with MCD:Abandoned Cellar,  MCD:Forge Cellar, or  MCD:Nether Cellar.
Part of: Obsidian Pinnacle
Spawn chance: 100%
Multiple per layout: No
Raid captains: Can have captains
Cellar is a required rune room sub dungeon within the mission Obsidian Pinnacle. It contains the H rune. The tile that contains the rune can spawn only after defeating the Arch-Illager on Default difficulty and pressing the button at the back of Great Hall in Camp. It is the only required sub dungeon that can be entirely avoided using an unintended exploit.
Appearance and description
The hidden rune room found within Cellar.
Cellar is located in an entrance door found around halfway through Obsidian Pinnacle. It highly resembles Highblock Halls, and contains furniture like paintings, tables, chairs, pianos, bookshelves, banners, and chandeliers. It contains a supplies chest near its exit door, and it also contains the rune room for Obsidian Pinnacle. The area containing the rune is accessed by interacting with a green book that is jutting out of one of the bookshelves next to an emerald urn. The book that opens this area appears after pressing the button at the back of Great Hall in Camp. Unlike all other rune rooms, the one found in Cellar has a unique appearance, and also contains a fancy chest and emerald urn. Upon activating the button in the center of the room, the H rune can be collected.
Objectives location
Main article: MCD:Objective
Cellar is found during the following Obsidian Pinnacle objective: " EYE OF THE STORM: PURSUE THE ARCH-ILLAGER"
