title: Dungeons:Chain Reaction
text:
Rarity: Powerful
Applicable to: Ranged weapon
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Has a chance to fire five arrows in all directions on impact.
Chain Reaction is an enchantment that can be found within the various ranged weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 10% chance to trigger, Cost: 2 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: 20% chance to trigger, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: 30% chance to trigger, Cost: 4 Enchantment Points
Usage
Any ranged weapon enchanted with Chain Reaction has a chance to shoot five arrows in multiple directions when shot at a mob. The chance of it activating increases by 10% each tier, at the highest tier it has a 30% chance of triggering.
Stacking
Chain Reaction is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Built-into
Chain Reaction tier I is built into the following weapon:
Firebolt Thrower
Mob
Enchanted mobs with the enchantment's projectiles will split into 5 more projectiles on impact.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Chain Reaction.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Chain Reaction" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.
Quotes
“ CHAIN REACTION – Every arrow that leaves your bow has the chance to spawn five extra arrows on impact, creating a crazy arrow tsunami. It's perfect for dealing with a scattering crowd of mobs. „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons:
A Handbook for Heroes

title: Earth:Challenges
text: The challenges menu icon. Challenges in Minecraft Earth. Challenges were tasks given to the player in Minecraft Earth, for the purpose of guiding the player into features of the game while earning experience at the same time. Challenges were analogous to achievements in Bedrock Edition or advancements in Java Edition. Challenge types There were three kinds of challenges offered in the game: Daily challenges Tappable challenges Journal challenges Each challenge involved individual tasks, each of which earned experience. Completion of a challenge earned the player additional experience points. In total, there were 348 challenges in the game. Daily challenges Daily challenges consisted mostly of tasks that encouraged real-world exploration to collect items from tappables, and placing a number of specific kinds of blocks on a buildplate. New challenges appeared each day. This kind had their own challenges, as well as rarity ratings. title: Dungeons:Chicken Jockey Tower
text:
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Undead
A chicken jockey tower is a rare hostile mob that can be found within various missions of Minecraft Dungeons.
Appearance
Chicken jockey towers are composed of a chicken jockey with six baby zombies balanced upon each other, with five of them sitting, and the top one balancing standing upright.
Spawning
Creeper Woods
Soggy Swamp ( Summoned by the corrupted cauldron )
Pumpkin Pastures
Arch Haven
Cacti Canyon
Chicken jockey towers can also spawn when night falls, regardless of the mission, when at least one hero dies in multiplayer, or the Night mode modifier is active.
Behavior
The behavior of a chicken jockey tower is similar to a normal chicken jockey but is somewhat slower in speed due to the weight of the baby zombies. When the chicken gets hit, this causes the baby zombies to scatter and attack the hero at once.

title: Earth:Chill Spot
text:
Cost: 19 (75 before 0.33.0 )
Size: 8×8
Rarity: Common
Mobs: 3 Salmon
“ Winter ice cannot deter the most resourceful survivalists. „ — Shop description
Chill Spot was a buildplate in Minecraft Earth that could be obtained at the store.
Appearance
Chill Spot was a small buildplate measuring 8×8. The buildplate contained a lot of ice and snow, as well as a frozen lake with a place for fishing. There were 3 salmon swimming in the lake.
Composition
The Chill Spot buildplate consisted of:
34 Water
90 Dirt
51 Top Snow
13 Grass Block s
23 Sandstone
36 Stone
42 Sand
86 Packed Ice
9 Ice
2 Spruce Trapdoor s
1 Spruce Fence
1 Spruce Wood Stairs
1 Torch
Obtaining
The buildplate could be obtained by buying it in a store for 19 (75) rubies. Rubies could be obtained in various ways, for example, by collecting tappables or buying them for real money.
Notes

title: Dungeons:Chilling
text:
Rarity: Powerful
Applicable to: Armor
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Emits a blast every two seconds that reduces the movement and attack speed of nearby enemies for one second.
Chilling is an armor enchantment in Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier III Chilling effect
Tier: I, Effect: -20% reduced speed, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: II, Effect: -40% reduced speed, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: -60% reduced speed, Cost: 4 Enchantment Points
Usage
Chilling emits a blast every two seconds that reduces the movement and the attack speed of all nearby enemies for one second, it also gives mobs a blue glow and snow particle effect for the duration of the debuff. The reduction amount increases with each tier, at the maximum tier mob speed is reduced by 60%.
Stacking
Chilling is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Built-into
Chilling tier I is built-into the following armor:
Frost Armor
Mob
Enchanted mobs with this enchantment will emit a chilling aura that slows the hero’s movement and attack speed every two seconds.
Sounds
Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier
Mob Frozen,?
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Chilling.

title: Dungeons:Daily Trial
text: New modifiers added in previous updates and note their addition in history. Modifiers frequently present in the Seasonal Trials Unused modifiers if present (Do not include Raid Captain modifiers) Update altered drop table. Info in Drops More Images if applicable It has been suggested that this page be split into MCD:Daily Trial and MCD:Seasonal Trial. [ discuss ] If this split affects many pages, or may potentially be controversial, do not split until a consensus has been reached. A Daily Trial is a daily challenge in Minecraft Dungeons that alters the game mechanics to create a new environment for heroes to overcome and obtain exclusive equipment. They consist of existing locations with a set of positive and negative modifiers. The modifier set of a Daily Trial. On PC, F5 hide/show the modifier list. Daily trials rotate every day at midnight in local time. An active internet connection and, for Nintendo Switch users, a connected Microsoft account is required to fetch new trials every day upon logging into the game. Trials do not refresh until the game is reopened. Accessible from the Mission Selection Map as green icons classified into I, II and III, two daily trials are available on the Mainland map per day and one per owned DLC on the Island Realms and Other Dimensions maps. Daily Trials can be played in both Online and Offline Games, alone or with friends. They randomly appear in all levels except for Squid Coast, Obsidian Pinnacle,???, The Stronghold, Gauntlet of Gales, and Treetop Tangle. Each hero can complete a Daily Trial once per day per difficulty (default, adventure, or apocalypse), regardless of threat level. Joining another hero that has not completed the trial or returning to camp before finishing the trial allows that trial to be played again. Daily Trials drop and reward upon completion weapons, armor, and artifacts of higher power levels ranges than what would be available in regular gameplay and are weighted to higher rarities. The "early" increase in power can ease the initial difficulty spike when transitioning between default, adventure, and apocalypse difficulties. In "apocalypse plus" threat levels, trials can prove particularity difficult with certain modifier sets combined with the vast abundance of enchanted mobs. A log page of Daily Trials since Sep 8 can be found at Daily Trial/Archive. Modifiers Modifiers are positive or negative effects added to Daily Trials to increase the challenge of missions. title: Dungeons:Christina
text:
This article is missing information about: Source of gender and design context

Obtaining: Automatically obtained

Christina is a hero in Minecraft Dungeons. She is intended to represent Xbox, for the Cross-platform Play Trailer for Minecraft Dungeons. [ citation needed ]
Appearance
Christina is a warm, light skinned human with front long blonde hair, blue eyes, and visible eyelashes. Her hair droops over a green headband, with the hair below it being noticeably faded. Her clothes consist of a green poncho on top of a muddy tunic resembling leaves or camouflage, with a large belt buckle, small green gloves, brown leggings, and green boots.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.6.0.0, Added Christina.

"Clam Cat" redirects here. For other uses, see Cat (disambiguation).
The clam Easter egg is a clam prop that can rarely spawn on a sidepath in Coral Rise. It is found next to a single emerald urn on an isolated stone column not marked on the map in the area near the end of the mission with the stone columns. The layout that contains the clam Easter egg always feature a shipwreck at the bottom of the ravine.

title: Legends:Clanger
text:
Details
Type: Piglin unit
Health: 110
Hitbox size: Height: 2.1 Block Width: 1.5 Blocks
Identifier: badger:disruptor
A clanger, also referred to in the journal as a disruptor, is a piglin type that attacks by slamming two cymbals together within Minecraft Legends.
Appearance
Horde of the Bastion
The clangers of the Horde of the Bastion are tall piglins with white eyes that turn red at night and spicy mix-colored skin that is covered in sores. Their foreheads are covered with taupe-colored masks and wear a fin made out of presumably netherite. In place of their ears on each side of the head are two tusks connected by presumably netherite. Their noses are pierced by a gold nose ring. They wield a gold cymbal on each hand.
Horde of the Hunt
The clangers of the Horde of the Hunt are tall piglins with white eyes that turn red at night and strikemaster-colored skin that is covered in sores. Their foreheads are covered taupe-colored masks and wear a fin made out of presumably netherite. In place of their ears on each side of the head are two tusks connected by presumably netherite. Their noses are pierced by a bone nose ring. They wield a bone cymbal on each hand.
Horde of the Spore
The clangers of the Horde of the Spore are tall piglins with white eyes that turn red at night and spicy silk-colored skin that is covered in sores. Their foreheads are covered taupe-colored masks and wear a fin made out of bandages and warped fungi and are clothed in napa-colored bandages and wine berry-colored skirts. In place of their ears on each side of the head are two tusks connected by bandages with warped fungi. Their noses are pierced by a bandaged nose ring with warped fungi. They wield a bandaged cymbal with warped fungi on each hand.
Spawning
Clangers can spawn in medium tier or higher piglin bases of every piglin horde and are spawned by piglin pits and Nether Portals. They can also spawn in groups with other piglins in every biome except for the Fatelands.
Drops
Clangers on death drop:
1-3 Lapis
1 Piglin Key
Behavior
Clangers are hostile to all non-piglin mobs and the player and will attempt to attack them. When clangers are not attempting to attack, they will idle and wander aimlessly in the piglin base they are located in.
Attacks
Cymbal Stun
Clangers have one type of attack, in which they slam their two cymbals together and create a large blast of sound. This attack deals very low damage, but stuns mobs caught in its radius.
Name: Stun, ID: bard_stun, Damage: 0.1, Cooldown: 6.75 secs, Min range: 0, Max range: 2.5, Damage type(s): Melee
Resistances and weaknesses
Clangers can be dealt more or less damage depending on the type of damage inflicted.
Damage type: Explosion, Multiplier: 0.4x
Damage type: Ranged, Multiplier: 1.25x
Damage type: Player, Multiplier: 1.2x
Damage type: Crush, Multiplier: 4x
Damage type: Dart, Multiplier: 0.5x
Damage type: Melee, Multiplier: 0.75x
Damage type: Sniper, Multiplier: 0.3x
Damage type: Chop, Multiplier: 0.4x
Damage type: Slam, Multiplier: 0.5x
Quotes
Name: Clanger, Category: Piglins, Unlocked by: Defeating a clanger, Quote: "These piglins stand out from the horde with their tall stature and their enthusiasm for playing their crude instruments. The powerful sounds they emit at close range are strong enough to stun nearby units for a long duration. Disruptors don't deal any damage on their own, so they're dependent on the rest of their piglin buddies to do the clean up work. Disruptors can be countered by direct player attacks or by attacking them being their range."
Sounds

Cloudwatcher is a hero in Minecraft Dungeons unlocked as part of the Cloudy Climb premium rewards track at the Adventure Hub.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: October 16, 2021, More heroes are announced to be coming soon to the game, with ten brand new heroes.
Minecraft Dungeons: November 26, 2021, A video was released in a Twitter post, revealing that the blue armor hero is called the cloudwatcher.

Minecraft Dungeons: 1.12.0.0, Added the Cloudwatcher.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Cloudwatcher" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.

The cloudwatcher is also called the Cloud Watcher.
Mojang based the Cloudwatcher off of drifting clouds in the sky.

title: Earth:Cluck House
text:
Cost: 25 (100 before 0.33.0 )
Size: 8×8
Rarity: Uncommon
Mobs: 1 Cluckshroom
“ Decorate this dugout and study fungus in earthy comfort. „ — Shop description
Cluck House was a buildplate in Minecraft Earth that could be obtained at the store.
Appearance
Cluck House was a small buildplate measuring 8×8. The buildplate contained a dugout with a cluckshroom in it. Birches and oaks grew on the dugout. The dugout also had a basement where mushrooms grew.
Composition
The Cluck House buildplate consisted of:
26 Birch Wood Planks
12 Stripped Birch Log s
16 Oak Wood Planks
207 Dirt
2 Oak Pressure Plate s
9 Oak Trapdoor s
58 Grass Block s
11 Oak Fence s
114 Oak Leaves
1 Torch
1 Birch Trapdoor
108 Birch Leaves
16 Birch Log s
29 Oak Log s
1 Spruce Wood Slab
2 Flower Pot s
5 Oak Wood Slab s
2 Birch Door s
1 Poppy
46 Gravel
108 Stone
3 Brown Mushroom s
2 Red Mushroom s
1 Water
4 Coarse Dirt
2 Birch Wood Stairs
Obtaining
The buildplate could be obtained by buying it in a store for 25 (100) rubies. Rubies could be obtained in various ways, for example, by collecting tappables or buying them for real money.
Notes

title: Legends:Cobblestone Golem
text:
Details
Type: Player unit
Category: Melee

Spawning

A cobblestone golem is a basic player unit that performs melee attacks in Minecraft Legends.
Spawning
Cobblestone golems can be spawned by spending the following resources in their respective spawner:
1 stone, 1 lapis, and 1 flame of creation in the Campaign
15 stone, 2 lapis and 1 Flame of creation in Versus Mode
The First of Diorite also has a chance of spawning cobblestone golems.
Behavior
Cobblestone golems use melee attacks against enemy units and structures. If the target is a structure, they rapidly punch it and deal more damage. Once the Nether portal they were targeting was destroyed, they will cheer and raise their arms up to the air as a celebration.
Attacks
Cobblestone golems have two attack types, melee and ground pound.
Name: Unknown, ID: stone_approach, Min range: 0, Max range: 15
Name: Melee, ID: melee_attack, Damage: 2, Cooldown: 0.25 secs, Min range: 0, Max range: 1, Damage type(s): CrushSmash
Name: Ground pound, ID: ground_pound_attack, Damage: 5, Cooldown: 0 secs, Min range: 0, Max range: 1, Damage type(s): CrushSmash
Resistances and weaknesses
Cobblestone golems can be dealt more or less damage depending on the type of damage inflicted.
Damage type: Hazard, Multiplier: 0.5x
Damage type: Blunt, Multiplier: 10x
Damage type: Stomp, Multiplier: 0.42x
Damage type: Grenade, Multiplier: 2x

title: Legends:Cobblestone Golem Spawner
text:
Type: Spawner
Cost: Campaign: 25 Wood 25 Stone Versus Mode: 35 Stone 15 Lapis
Build time: 4 seconds
Identifier: badger:spawner_stone_golem
A cobblestone golem spawner is a type of spawner that spawns cobblestone golems in Minecraft Legends.
Building
Cobblestone golem spawners can be built with 25 wood and 25 stone in the Campaign, or 35 stone and 15 lapis in Versus Mode. No improvements are needed to gather the needed resources or build cobblestone golem spawners, meaning they can be built almost immediately after spawning into a world.
Like all other player-built structures, cobblestone golem spawners can be destroyed by interacting with them, no resources are refunded when one is destroyed.
Usage
Cobblestone golems can be spawned at cobblestone golem spawners by interacting with one and spending 1 stone and 1 lapis in the Campaign, or 15 stone and 2 lapis in Versus Mode.
Cobblestone golem spawners can also be used to refund and recall any allied units.
Quotes
Name: Cobblestone Golem Spawner, Category: Combat Melodies, Unlocked by: Automatically unlocked, Quote: "Spawns cobblestone golemsStrong vs structuresResistent to fire and knockbackWeak vs ranged unitsInteract with the structure to spawn them, each mob requires the following resource costs:- 1 stone- 1 lapis- 1 Flame of Creation"
History

Minecraft Legends: TU1, 1.17.49848, Mobs can now be refunded at cobblestone golem spawners.

title: Dungeons:Colossal Rampart
text: Part of: Island Realms Minimum difficulty: V Unlock predecessor: Ravine Ruins scroll Windswept Peaks Raid captains: Always has six captains Daily trials: Can have daily trials Formidable Fortifications A formidable wall twists along the base of these mighty peaks, stretching endlessly in every direction. These impressive defenses, abandoned long ago, have now been claimed by Illagers who seek to control the entire mountain range. Drive the Illagers from their newfound fortifications while you still can! Colossal Rampart is the third island realms secret mission in Minecraft Dungeons, and is part of the Howling Peaks DLC. It is unlocked after collecting the scroll found in Ravine Ruins and completing Windswept Peaks. Completing Colossal Rampart does not unlock any missions. Appearance and description Colossal Rampart is a massive battlement built around the base of a snowy mountain. It is composed of thin walkways atop dark stone brick walls, and is suspended over foggy chasms and cliffs. It features small abandoned homes and towers, banners, dangerously strong winds, torches, swinging coals, windmills, a tall outpost, ambush pillars that release wind bursts, fields of wheat and rivers, siege gears, hay bales, and a massive garrison. At the start of the mission, a thin bridge with strong winds must be crossed to reach an area with an ambush and three gates. Defeating the ambush lowers the gates, granting access to a clifface area with an outpost at the top of the cliff. Upon reaching the outpost by climbing the cliffs and wooden stairs, a wind ambush pillar must be defeated to grant access to the entrance door of Ominous Outpost. Ominous Outpost is a multi-level tower featuring three colored gates that prevent access to a gold key golem. The different floors of the outpost are color-coded, with the first being red, the second being yellow, and the third being blue. Completing the puzzles and ambushes on the red and yellow floors will grant access to buttons that lower the first and second gates next to the key golem. The final blue floor, called Frontier, is always an outdoor area with a wind pipe puzzle, and completing said puzzle extinguishes an open flame at the center of the room, granting access to a button that lowers the third and final gate next to the key golem. By using the gold key golem on the yellow locked gateway up the stairs to the north-west, the exit door of Ominous Outpost can be accessed, leading to a second outdoor portion of the mission. Upon navigating across the battlements and bridges to the south-east, which can occasionally have a blue locked gateway and diamond key golem, another ambush can be reached. This ambush ends with a fight with an illusioner and, if defeated, grants access to an open area with wheat fields and two siege gears. Upon deactivating the two siege gears, the entrance door of the Garrison can be reached by navigating up the battlements to the north-east. The Garrison is an expansive fortress featuring bookcases, furniture, trees, and two locked gateways, one blue and the other yellow. The blue locked gateway must be unlocked first, and is unlocked using the diamond key golem found past the hallway to the north-west. Upon opening the blue locked gateway and navigating to the north-east, an area with a button, gate, and gold key golem can be reached. Using the button to the north-west opens the gate to the south-east, granting access to the gold key golem which must be brought back to the yellow locked gateway to the south-west. A final arena can be entered upon opening the yellow locked gateway, where an ambush ending with a fight with a rampart captain occurs. Defeating the ambush grants access to a small treasure room with emerald urns and a fancy chest. Additionally, the exit door of the fortress can be found to the north-east which, if entered, ends the mission. Unlocking The entrance door of Ravine Ruins, the sub dungeon where Colossal Rampart's scroll is found, can rarely spawn in Windswept Peaks during the following objective: " The Roof of the World: Open The Gate" Locations Main article: MCD:Location NameID: Colossal RampartEndlessRampart, Resource pack ID: endlessrampart, Is required: ✔, Is DLC exclusive: Howling Peaks, Contains scroll: ✖, Contains rune room: ✖, Contains eye of ender: ✖ NameID: Ominous OutpostOminous Outpost Red FloorOminous Outpost Yellow FloorFrontiersub_ramparts_outpost, Resource pack ID: endlessrampart, Is required: ✔, Is DLC exclusive: Howling Peaks, Contains scroll: ✖, Contains rune room: ✖, Contains eye of ender: ✖ NameID: Garrisonsub_rampart_garrison, Resource pack ID: endlessrampart, Is required: ✔, Is DLC exclusive: Howling Peaks, Contains scroll: ✖, Contains rune room: ✖, Contains eye of ender: ✖ NameID: Workshopsub_rampart_workshop, Resource pack ID: endlessrampart, Is required: ✖, Is DLC exclusive: Howling Peaks, Contains scroll: ✖, Contains rune room: ✖, Contains eye of ender: ✖

title: Legends:Journal
text: The journal icon in Minecraft Legends. The journal. The Journal is a tool that can be accessed within Minecraft Legends. It contains information about things from the game. The Songbook is part of the journal that contains songs to command allays to gather and build. List of entries Journal Resources Name: Wood, Unlocked by: Collecting wood, Entry: "Allays use wood to build structures like walls, stairs and towers. It's also necessary to spawn plank golems. It would be silly to explain where it comes from, right?" Name: Stone, Unlocked by: Collecting stone, Entry: "Stone is required to build moderate and advanced structures like stronger village fortifications. You can find stone pillars and deposits in most biomes." Name: Iron, Unlocked by: Collecting iron, Entry: "Iron is a bit rarer than stone but shouldn't be too difficult to find. It is used by the allays to build structures like the protector tower and the masonry as well as some allies." Name: Coal, Unlocked by: Collecting coal, Entry: "Coal is used to spawn creepers and build certain advanced structures like the kaboomery. It's fairly rare, but you can find coal deposits in most biomes." Name: Gold, Unlocked by: Collecting gold, Entry: "Gold is obtained from destroying piglin bases and some of their structures. It is required to build wellhouses. Taking the piglins' gold after defeating them in battle is definitely adding insult to injury." Name: Diamond, Unlocked by: Collecting diamond, Entry: "Diamond can rarely be found in small deposits around the Overworld as well as collected in village chests. It is used by the allays to build freeze traps and the spyglass overlook." Name: Redstone, Unlocked by: Collecting redstone, Entry: "Redstone is a rare resource found in small deposits around the Overworld and collected in village chests. It's required to build the redstone launcher and the battle drums. Don't worry. the allays will do all the redstone engineering for you." Name: Piglin Key, Unlocked by: Collecting a piglin key, Entry: "These keys open piglin chests which have been locked to prevent their valuable contents from being stolen. Though any piglin may be found carrying them, bosses and tougher units, especially those found roaming the world, are the best place to look. Their crude craftsmanship causes them to break after use, so you'll need to find a new one for each chest you open." Name: Lapis, Unlocked by: Collecting lapis, Entry: "Lapis is what feeds the Flames of Creation, allowing you to spawn golems and mobs. It is obtained from roaming piglins and may also be stocked in village chests. Lapis is the main reason why you should give every piglin you see a good thwacking. Not that you needed more reasons." Name: Prismarine, Unlocked by: Collecting primsarine, Entry: "Prismarine can only be obtained from destroying piglin structures and can be used to build upgrade structures at the Well of Fate." Name: Blast Tower Core, Unlocked by: Collecting a blast tower core, Entry: "Created by the hosts, this core contains the essence of the Blast Tower, enabling its unique properties. Upon collecting a tower found in the world or deconstructing one you've placed, you will receive its core. The more you collected, the more you can build. If a tower is destroyed, the core will be lost." Name: Frost Tower Core, Unlocked by: Collecting a frost tower core, Entry: "Created by the hosts, this core contains the essence of the Frost Tower, enabling its unique properties. Upon collecting a tower found in the world or deconstructing one you've placed, you will receive its core. The more you collected, the more you can build. If a tower is destroyed, the core will be lost." Name: Stun Tower Core, Unlocked by: Collecting a stun tower core, Entry: "Created by the hosts, this core contains the essence of the Stun Tower, enabling its unique properties. Upon collecting a tower found in the world or deconstructing one you've placed, you will receive its core. The more you collected, the more you can build. If a tower is destroyed, the core will be lost." Tools Name: Map, Unlocked by: Automatically unlocked, Entry: "Not only does the map show the Overworld and its settlements, but it will also reveal the locations of piglin bases and piglin movements. And you didn't even have to make it yourself!" Name: Legendary Lute, Unlocked by: Automatically unlocked, Entry: "Allays love music. The Lute allows you to play different melodies to ask them to do various tasks, like gathering or building." Name: Banner of Courage, Unlocked by: Automatically unlocked, Entry: "The Banner of Courage draws its power from whoever wields it. In the right hands, it is a symbol of unity and hope." Name: Flames of Creation, Unlocked by: Automatically unlocked, Entry: "The Flames of Creation signify the unity between you and your allies and

title: Story Mode:Ultimate Weapon
text:
While a name exists in the game code, it has not been used officially by Mojang or Telltale Games.
Note: Based on "obj_weaponUltimate_axeUltimate.d3dmesh".
Details
Status: Destroyed
Creator: Jesse
Obtained during: A Block and a Hard Place
First appearance: A Block and a Hard Place
Latest appearance: Hero in Residence
The ultimate weapon, also known as a super weapon, was an enchanted diamond tool with the power of a command block. It was enchanted using Ivor's command enchanting book.
History
After the truth was exposed in Ivor's laboratory, Jesse was given the option to choose which diamond tool they would craft. They would enchant it with the command enchanting book. If Jesse enchanted a diamond hoe, Reuben would laugh at them.
After getting inside the wither storm, Jesse used it to strike the command block three times with each having an increasing challenge. Jesse, however, dropped the weapon accidentally which Reuben retrieves. After the final strike against the command block the block was destroyed together with the weapon.
Properties
An ultimate tool can destroy any item, including the command block. The enchantment applied is specifically for destroying the command block.
Obtaining
Ingredients: Any Diamond Tool +Command Enchanting Book
Ingredients: Any Diamond Tool +Command Enchanting Book
Ingredients: Anvil Recipe
Episodes
A Block and a Hard Place
Appearances
A Block and a Hard Place
Hero in Residence (flashback)
Trivia
The ultimate sword’s grip is shorter than the other swords’, accurately matching Minecraft ’s. The others, based on Minecraft swords, have been subtly modified to have slightly longer grips.
In the Adventure Pass thumbnail, Jesse is shown holding the ultimate sword, even though they use a normal enchanted diamond sword in the Adventure Pass.
In the Netflix adaptation of Minecraft: Story Mode, the appearance of the ultimate pickaxe and hoe does not appear as they do in the original game. Instead, they use their respective vanilla texture, and with the pickaxe having the shape of its ultimate version.
Gallery
Ultimate tool set

title: Story Mode:Command Enchanting Book
text:
While a name exists in the game code, it has not been used officially by Mojang or Telltale Games.

Latest appearance: A Block and a Hard Place
The command enchanting book, or just enchantment book is a book that contains the power of a command block. It was used by Jesse to enchant a diamond tool to destroy the command block powering the wither storm.
History
In A Block and a Hard Place, while the Order of the Stone and Jesse and the others are taking shelter in a cave, Ivor approaches Soren about the wither storm, revealing the existence of an enchanting book he stored in his distant laboratory.
After solving the puzzles in Ivor's laboratory, they retrieve the enchanting book. Jesse can then choose which diamond tool to enchant with it.
Properties
It has the power of a command block. However, it is unknown if it can be used like a command block, and not just for enchanting.
Obtaining
Episodes
A Block and a Hard Place — obtained automatically during the story.
Usage

A Block and a Hard Place

“ An enchanting book. One that contains the power of the Command Block itself. „ — Ivor to Soren and Jesse.
“ How ironic that the best thing the Command Block gave us was the very means to destroy it. „ — Ivor about the command block enchanting book.

title: Dungeons:Committed
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Melee Weapon Ranged Weapon
In-game description Deal increased damage against already wounded enemies.
Committed is a weapon enchantment that can be found within the various melee and ranged weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Committed has a chance to appear in any enchantment slot on a melee or ranged weapon. Enchantments on items can be swapped out for Committed by rerolling them at the enchantsmith.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 0-50% bonus damage, Cost: 1 enchantment point, Cost (Gilded): 2 enchantment points
Tier: II, Effect: 0-75% bonus damage, Cost: 2 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 3 enchantment points
Tier: III, Effect: 0-100% bonus damage, Cost: 3 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 4 enchantment points
Built-into
Committed tier I is built-into the following weapons:
Growing Staff
Resolute Tempest Knife
Truthseeker
Mobs
Mobs can only be enchanted with Committed if the "100% of melee mobs have the Committed enchantment" modifier is active.
Stacking
Committed is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Usage
Committed makes the hero's weapon deal more damage against mobs depending on how much health they've already lost. For example, if the mob has lost 50% of health and the weapon has committed 3, the next attack will deal 50% more damage. If the mob has full health, it will deal 0% extra damage. The bonus damage increases with each tier, at the maximum tier the bonus damage is 0-100%.
Mobs
When a mob is enchanted with it, Committed functions identically to how it does for the hero.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Committed.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.10.2.0, Now compatible with ranged weapons.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Committed" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.

title: Dungeons:Consumable
text:
A consumable is a type of helpful item that is immediately "consumed" upon interacting with it. This includes food, potions, and other items like arrows and TNT. These items do not go into the hero’s inventory and are lost after completing a dungeon. These items can be randomly dropped by enemies or found in chests.
List of consumables
Food
Main article: Food
Food gives the hero regeneration for a set duration. The effects can be stacked, but the duration does not.
Apple
Bread

Cooked Salmon
Dried Kelp ( unused )
Melon
Pork
Rabbit's Foot ( unused )
Sweet Berries
Tropical Fish
Potions
Main article: Potion See also: Health Potion — the utility bar potion that replaced the consumable alternative.
Potions give the hero its respective effect for a set duration. The different effects can stack, but similar effects do not.

A Handbook for Heroes

title: Dungeons:Coral Rise
text: Part of: Island Realms Minimum difficulty: II Unlock predecessor: Squid Coast Raid captains: Usually has all six captains but not always Daily trials: Can have daily trials Danger in the Corals Home to colorful corals and schools of playful tropical fish, Coral Rise is full of life and breathtaking sights. It would be easy to be lulled into a false sense of security by the serene beauty, but the Orb's corruption brews beneath the sandy surface. Hordes of Drowned and Sunken Skeletons lurk in the depths and wait for the currents to bring them fresh foes. Coral Rise is the ninth island realms mission in Minecraft Dungeons, and is part of the Hidden Depths DLC. It is unlocked after the completion of Squid Coast, and completing Coral Rise unlocks Abyssal Monument. It has a rare chance to generate the Twilight Cavern optional sub dungeon that contains a scroll which, if collected, unlocks the Radiant Ravine secret mission. Appearance and description Coral Rise is an expansive underwater coral reef located on the seafloor north-east of Abyssal Monument. It is composed of sandy pathways dotted with dirt and rock structures, and tall columns suspended over ravines. It features bubble columns, kelp and seagrass, coral, ocean ruins, starfish and clams, shipwrecks, and an ocean monument at its end. A quarter a way through the mission, an area with a split path of two elevated stone bridges near a cave opening can rarely generate, which contains the entrance door of Twilight Cavern, an optional sub dungeon containing the scroll that unlocks Radiant Ravine. Halfway through the mission, a gate must be lowered by pressing two buttons found in the surrounding ocean ruin. Shortly after, an ambush that ends with a drowned necromancer must be defeated to grant access further into the mission. Upon activating said ambush, a conduit will slowly detach from the pedastal until it drops on the ground to be collected. Collecting the conduit is not required to complete Coral Rise, but it provides increased movement speed and infinite underwater breathing when held, at the cost of taking up the ranged weapon slot. Near the end of the mission, another ambush must be defeated within a shipwreck to grant access to the entrance door of The Abyss. Upon entering The Abyss, the shadow of the Ancient Guardian will appear and move towards a stone structure to the north-west. Upon getting close to the structure, its seal will open and grant access to a button that, if pressed, will start one final ambush that ends with an elder guardian. Upon defeating the ambush, the prismarine gate to the north-east will open, granting access to the exit door of the mission. When the layout containing the clam cat Easter egg has spawned, a shipwreck can be seen at the bottom of the ravine north-west of the sidepath. To access the sidepath, the hero must roll across the crevasse to the isolated stone column with seaweed growing on it, and then navigate around to the north-west to reach a small opening that leads to the clam cat. A single emerald urn can be found next to the clam Easter egg, and the clam cat cannot be interacted with. title: Dungeons:Corrupted Beacon
text:
Type: Artifact
Rarity: COMMON / RARE
Properties: X artifact damage per second 2.5 Seconds cooldown +1 Soul Gathering Requires souls (20 per second)
Runes
In-game flavor text Fires a high-powered beam that continuously damages mobs.
In-game description The Corrupted Beacon holds immense power within. It waits for the moment to unleash its wrath.
A corrupted beacon is an artifact that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. Its unique variant is the corrupted pumpkin, making it the only artifact to have a unique variant. It functions similarly to the eye of the guardian from the Hidden Depths DLC.
Lore
While it isn't "clear how this beacon got corrupted", it may have "something to do with the Arch-Illager and the Orb of Dominance ". In Minecraft Legends, the corrupted beacon makes an appearance in the Night Beacon base.
Obtaining
Missions

Cacti Canyon
Redstone Mines
Pumpkin Pastures (Adventure)
Treetop Tangle (Adventure)
Gale Sanctum (DLC)
End Wilds (Apocalypse, DLC)
Merchants
Village Merchant
Gift Wrapper
Mystery Merchant
Luxury Merchant
Usage
The Corrupted Beacon fires a beam that can pierce mobs, dealing 10% of the base value as damage and consumes 2 souls every 0.1 seconds. With the base maximum soul capacity of 300, the beacon can be fired for up to 15 seconds. The beam is toggleable, meaning it can be sustained without the need to continuously hold down its slot button. However, it will enter cooldown after the beam is cancelled.
The beam fired has a massive range (about 40 blocks away) that exceeds the boundaries of the screen, and cannot pass through walls.

The player equips the Corrupted Defender when the following cards are used in Minecraft Dungeons Arcade.

text:
Type: Artifact
Rarity: UNIQUE
Properties: X artifact damage per second 2.5 Seconds cooldown +1 Soul Gathering Requires souls (20 per second) Special event item
Runes
In-game flavor text Fires a high-powered beam that continuously damages mobs.
In-game description The Corrupted Pumpkin glows with supernatural power, illuminating even the darkest nights with its powerful beacon.
The corrupted pumpkin is a unique corrupted beacon variant within Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Events
Spooky Fall (Ended)
Merchants
Gift Wrapper
Usage
With the maximum amount of souls gathered, being 300, it can be fired for 24 seconds in total, using about 12.5 souls per second.
Each tick of damage deals around 10% of the damage per second value.
The beam fired has a massive range (about 40 blocks away) that exceeds the boundaries of the screen.

title: Dungeons:Corrupted Seeds
text:
Type: Artifact
Rarity: COMMON / RARE
Properties: X damage per second Briefly entangles mobs Poisons entangled mobs 20 second cooldown
Damage type: Poison
Runes
In-game flavor text Grow grapple vines, which inflict poison.
In-game description A pouch of poisonous corrupted seeds which grow into spiky grapple vines, entangling and slowly draining the life from its victims.
The corrupted seeds is an artifact that can be found within the Jungle Awakens DLC of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Missions
Overgrown Temple
Panda Plateau
Treetop Tangle
Merchants
Village Merchant
Gift Wrapper
Mystery Merchant
Luxury Merchant
Usage
Upon activation, the corrupted seeds entangles (locks in place) up to eight mobs for a few seconds, while entangled, poison damage is dealt to the mobs every second.
Properties
X damage per second
Briefly entangles mobs
Poisons entangled mobs
20 second cooldown
Entangle vine
Health points: Lifespan:3.0
Behavior: Hostile
An entangle vine is a non-attackable entity that can be summoned by the corrupted seeds, whisperers and the treetop whisperer within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. They share functionally with spider webs.
Behavior
When a hero uses the corrupted seeds, it will summon a small patch of entangle vines which can trap mobs and other heroes for a few seconds before disappearing. Entangle vines are also summoned when a whisperer stretches their arms forward and closes their palms.
Sounds

Data values
In the game files, the corrupted seeds are referred to internally by the names CorruptedSeeds, and the Corrupted-Seeds.
In the game files, entangle vines have multiple BP character JSON files and are referred to internally by the names EntangleVine, grapple, grappleVines, GrappleVineTrap, Vine_Trap, Whisperer_Trap, Whisperer_Trap_A, and AnimBP_EntangleVine, BP_EntangleVineCharacter, BP_EntangleVineGameplayEffect}}, and BP_EntangleVineImmunityEffect}}.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.1.1.0, The internal files for corrupted seeds had been added into the game files.
Minecraft Dungeons: May 29, 2020, Jungle Awakens officially revealed, with a release date slated forJuly 2020. Corrupted seeds will be a part of the Jungle Awakens DLC.
Minecraft Dungeons: Jun 26, 2020, Corrupted seeds showcased alongside the release date for Jungle Awakens.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.3.2.0, Added corrupted seeds and entangle vines.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.16.2.0, Now obtainable from theTreetop Tangle.

title: Dungeons:Cowardice
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Armor
Soul gear only: No
In-game description While you are at full HP, you deal increased ranged and melee damage.
Cowardice is an enchantment that can be found within the various armor of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: +20% increased ranged and melee damage, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: +30% increased ranged and melee damage, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: +40% increased ranged and melee damage, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Cowardice grants the hero an increase in the ranged and melee damage that the hero deals when at full health. When Cowardice is activated it is indicated by a status effect of the same name being displayed on the hero's screen. The additional damage increases with each tier, at the maximum tier the additional damage is +40%.
Stacking
Cowardice is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Mob
Enchanted mobs with the enchantment deal extra damage when at full health.
Scuttling Torment is guaranteed to spawn with cowardice.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Cowardice.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Cowardice" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.

title: Earth:Make Stuff
text: The Make Stuff window. Make Stuff was a feature in Minecraft Earth that lets players craft or smelt items. Functionality Players were able to craft and smelt items in Make Stuff. Players were able to select the buttons "crafting" or "smelting". Crafting and smelting in Make Stuff were only possible via pre-set recipes. Crafting In most ways, crafting worked like in normal Minecraft except for crafting time, indicated by the timer icon, which ranged from 4 seconds ( planks or sticks ) up to 5 minutes ( diamond pickaxe or diamond axe ). The duration for crafting depended on rarity of the item. The player was able to leave the crafting interface to continue playing while the item is crafted. Two additional craft slots were available. Smelting In Minecraft Earth, smelting also had similar mechanics, except for the smelting time and fuel, indicated by the timer and flame icon which had three variants. Upon smelting, the furnace would start heating and a flame icon appeared based on the fuel. The duration of smelting also depended on the rarity of the item. As with normal Minecraft, smelting continued if the player left the smelting interface. Buying slots Prior to 0.23.0, players had the option to buy additional crafting and smelting slots in exchange for rubies. Finishing immediately While an operation was underway, the interface offered to finish immediately at a cost of 1 Ruby per minute remaining. While crafting or smelting was underway, the interface offered the player an option to "finish now"; that is, complete the crafting job immediately by spending rubies. "Finish now" cost 1 ruby per minute of time remaining (rounded up) to craft or smelt. title: Earth:Cream Cow
text:
Health points: 10 HP
Behavior: Passive
Mob type: Animal
Hitbox size: Height: 1.4 blocks Width: 0.9 blocks
Spawn: Plains
Rarity: Uncommon
Player journal description Heavy? Double? Whipped? No matter your favorite type of cream, this cow has you covered.
The cream cow was a creamy-colored variant of the cow exclusively found in Minecraft Earth.
Behavior
Main article: Cow § Behavior
Similar to normal cows, cream cows wandered around, mooing and breathing occasionally. They avoided water, lava, and walking off cliffs high enough to cause fall damage. They fled for a few seconds when harmed.
Obtaining
Cream cows were an uncommon mob drop in cow tappables. They were also obtainable as a reward in Season 17.
Drops
When killed, cream cows dropped:
0-2 Leather
1-3 Raw Beef ( Cooked Beef if killed while on fire)
Cream cows could be milked by using a bucket on it, yielding a milk bucket.

title: Story Mode:Creeder
text:
Details
Gameplay appearances: Cutscene Quick time events
Found in: Sunshine Institute
Free roam interactable: No
First appearance: Jailhouse Block
Latest appearance: Jailhouse Block
Creeders are monsters that appear in Minecraft: Story Mode - Season Two. They are a hybrid of creepers and prison spiders, and they make their only appearance in Jailhouse Block as they are seen chasing the characters.
Behavior
Creeders have the ability to climb and leap like a spider and explode like a creeper. However, their explosions are more powerful than creepers as they are capable of destroying obsidian. They are also seen using their front pair of legs to try and grab Jesse before exploding.

Unlike the other spider variants, the creeders only have six legs rather than eight.
Unlike normal creepers or charged creepers, the creeders are capable of exploding obsidian.

title: Legends:Creeper Clash
text:
Type: Lost Legend
Date: October 2023
Internal ID: lost_legend_07
Reward: Spooky Swathe
Creeper Clash is the seventh Lost Legend of Minecraft Legends. It was released in October 2023.
Objective

Destroy all the 6 piglin bases while only using creepers in a limited amount of time.
Features
Player
The player can only build creeper spawners, cure netherrack and build ramps. Only 1 build and gather allay and 3 flames of creation are given to the player. When killed players respawn in the most recent piglin outpost or base they destroyed.
Nether Portal
Nether portals located in medium piglin bases can regenerate health quickly without the help of patchers. Nether portals located in small piglin bases do not have the ability of regenerating health.
Piglin Outposts
Small piglin outposts generate similar Versus Mode's piglin outpost, with just flat terrain with no pillars or obstacles. Regular piglin outposts are much more unique in comparison. Regular Horde of the Bastion piglin outposts generate in a cross-like style with the ends of the cross having 4 slightly elevated rooms with piglin gates and walls around with a singular piglin structure inside of it. Regular Horde of the Spore piglin outposts generate in a similar way to the Horde of the Bastion regular piglin outpost but now having 5 elevated pillars in a cross-like formation connected by piglin ramps and without walls or gates. Regular Horde of the Hunt piglin outposts on the other hand are similar to small piglin outposts with it generating on flat terrain with no pillars, gates or walls.
Outpost: Antechamber, Piglin Structures: Piglin WallNether SpreaderGold Extractor, Resource Rewards: 5 Minutes
Outpost: Small Bastion Outpost, Piglin Structures: Piglin PitBlaze Rod Tower, Resource Rewards: 2 Minutes2 Flames of Creation
Outpost: Small Spore Outpost, Piglin Structures: Piglin PitBlaze Rod TowerSpore Tower, Resource Rewards: 2 Minutes2 Flames of Creation
Outpost: Small Hunt Outpost, Piglin Structures: Piglin PitBlaze Rod TowerNether Spreader, Resource Rewards: 2 Minutes2 Flames of Creation
Outpost: Regular Bastion Outpost, Piglin Structures: Piglin PitBlaze Rod TowerPatcherArmored TowerPiglin WallPiglin GatePiglin Launcher, Resource Rewards: 3 Minutes5 Flames of Creation
Outpost: Regular Spore Outpost, Piglin Structures: Piglin PitBlaze Rod TowerSpike TowerPatcher, Resource Rewards: 3 Minutes5 Flames of Creation
Outpost: Regular Hunt Outpost, Piglin Structures: Piglin PitBlaze Rod TowerNether SpreaderPatcherArmored TowerPiglin Launcher, Resource Rewards: 3 Minutes5 Flames of Creation
World Generation
The Overworld only consists of two biomes which are the forest and swamp.The forest only has birch trees, dark oak trees and autumn trees. Speed wheat along with redthorn generate more often. Cats are the only passive mobs to spawn. The moon is yellow with a black creeper face.
Quotes
“ In this legend, you must destroy the piglins' portal bases and outposts before time runs out. And you only have creepers to help you make it happen. Move fast and blow things up to earn the Spooky Swathe hero skin. „ — In-game description

title: Dungeons:Creepy Crypt
text: Part of: Mainland Minimum difficulty: II Unlock predecessor: Creeper Woods Crypt scroll Creeper Woods Raid captains: Usually has all six captains but not always Daily trials: Can have daily trials The Forsaken Undercroft A sprawling crypt lies deep below the forest floor where the Illagers believe they can continue their wicked work undisturbed. Will you stop them or will you get lost in the dark? Creepy Crypt is the first mainland secret mission in Minecraft Dungeons. It is unlocked after collecting the scroll found in Creeper Woods Crypt and completing Creeper Woods. Completing Creepy Crypt does not unlock any missions. Appearance and description “ CREEPY CRYPT – Located in Creeper Woods, the crypt is not for the faint of heart - the dark corridors are filled with terrifying tombs and crawling with mobs. It is also laden with loot - there are switches that reveal hidden areas and bonus chests that appear as rewards for defeating mob hordes. Your mission is to find the lost tome and make it out in one piece. Beware of crumbling walkways as you explore. „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons: A Handbook for Heroes Creepy Crypt is a labyrinth of dark corridors filled with tombs, chasms, and crumbling walkways. Parts of the crypt have had their ceilings collapse, letting in skylight from the forest above. It features torches, candles, cobwebs, villager statues, skeleton remains of royal guards, glowing mushrooms, skeleton skulls, and chandeliers. The starting area of the mission is in Crypt Entrance, where heroes must proceed into the crypt entrance to the north-west. Halfway through the mission, a lost tome must be collected from an elevated area called the Tomb of the One, accessed after defeating an ambush of undead mobs. After collecting the tome, all gates throughout the mission will be opened, which includes the gate blocking the exit of the crypt, as well as the gate from the start of the mission in Crypt Entrance. Near the end of the mission, the exit door of the main crypt can be found, leading to Crypt Exit. To the north, an exit door can be found across a wooden bridge suspended over lava, which when entered ends the mission. Unlocking The entrance door of Creeper Woods Crypt, the sub dungeon where Creepy Crypt's scroll is found, always spawns in Creeper Woods during the following objective: " THE CARAVAN: FIND THE title: Legends:Creeper Homestead
text:
Type: Homestead
Biomes: Badlands
The creeper homestead is an ally structure in Minecraft Legends, and serves as the homestead for creepers.
Description
Creeper homesteads are composed of multiple shallow pools of bright green acid which creepers seem to bathe in harmlessly, with a bulbous rock formation in their center, and multiple fencepost-like rocks that are similar in appearance to cut sandstone blocks scattered around. The rock formations have stripes in multiple hues of yellow and orange, and taper off into a few short twisting spires at the top. Blocks touching the acid have their sides stained a deep green.
The fencepost-like rocks are shown to spew smoke within cutscenes, but this behavior isn't seen in-game.
The green acid damages the player on contact.
Spawning
The creeper homestead will spawn in one of the badlands biomes in the Overworld after destroying at least one Nether portal after the Night Beacon.
Mechanics
Upon visiting it for the first time, the creeper homestead is invaded by the Horde of the Bastion, pillars are damaged, and creepers are locked inside cages. Defeating the requisite number of piglins and destroying the base spawning there will free the homestead, rendering creepers spawnable. Afterward, piglins have a chance of targeting the homestead and, if their attack was successful, they rebuild the base, making creeper spawners impossible to build.
Quotes
“ Home to the reclusive creepers, this collection of stone and sulphur buildings is nestled deep in the vast barrens of the badlands. „ — Map legend
Sounds

title: Legends:Creeper Spawner
text:
Type: Spawner
Cost: Campaign: 25 Wood 25 Stone 5 Coal Versus Mode: 55 Coal 45 Lapis
Build time: 4 seconds
Identifier: badger:creeper_spawner
A creeper spawner is a type of spawner that can be used to spawn creepers in Minecraft Legends.
Building
Creeper spawners can be built with 25 wood, 25 stone, and 5 coal in the Campaign, or 55 coal and 45 lapis in Versus Mode.
Like all other player-built structures, creeper spawners can be destroyed by interacting with them, no resources are refunded when one is destroyed.
Usage
Creepers can be spawned at creeper spawners by interacting with one and spending 2 coal and 2 lapis in the Campaign, or 12 coal and 10 lapis in Versus Mode.
Creeper spawners can also be used to refund and recall any allied units.
Quotes
Name: Creeper Spawner, Category: Combat Melodies, Unlocked by: Campaign:Saving the Creeper HomesteadVersus Mode:Building Improvement Gather Coal, Quote: Spawns creepers. Strong vs structures, mace runts and lava launchers. Weak vs warboars, portal guards and pigmadillos.Interact with the structure to spawn them, each mob requires the following resource costs:- 2 coal- 2 lapis- 1 Flame of Creation

title: Dungeons:Creeper Woods
text: Part of: Mainland Boss (arcade): Enderman Minimum difficulty: I Unlock predecessor: Squid Coast Raid captains: Always has six captains Daily trials: Can have daily trials A Journey Begins... The Arch-Illager has come to power using the Orb of Dominance, turning the Overworld upside down. Power is collecting in strange places, the undead are stronger and refuse to burn in the sunlight, and Illagers are raiding the countryside! The Illagers capture the defenseless Villagers and move them east, toward their mysterious base in the Redstone Mines. The first step in their ruthless journey starts by going through Creeper Woods, a mob infested maze of tangled roots and branches. Move quickly if you wish to save the Villagers from a terrible fate! Creeper Woods is the second mainland mission in Minecraft Dungeons. It is unlocked after the completion of Squid Coast, and completing Creeper Woods unlocks Pumpkin Pastures, Soggy Swamp, Redstone Mines, Cacti Canyon, Treetop Tangle, and Gauntlet of Gales. It contains the captured village merchant. It always generates the Creeper Woods Crypt optional sub dungeon that contains a scroll which, if collected, unlocks the Creepy Crypt secret mission. Appearance and description “ CREEPER WOODS – Somewhere in the woods, a caravan is transporting Villager prisoners to labor in far-off lands. Your first quest is to find the caravan in this mob-infested maze and stop the Illagers, or there's no telling what dreadful fate will befall our Villager friends. „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons: A Handbook for Heroes Creeper Woods is a wooded area set at night, and contains monoliths, hills, narrow bridges, rope-bridge walkways, and rivers. It is foggy, dim, and eerily illuminated by yellow fungi and pop flowers, with creepers walking about the forest. It contains two supply chests, one in The Inn before the spider cave, and one at the Illager caravan. Piles of emerald urns can be found in Creeper Room, an optional sub dungeon found early on in the mission. A fancy chest can also be found inside Creeper Room upon surviving the ambush of creepers. Giant stone mob heads can be found in the lake at the start of the mission, with the enderman one that can spawn only in the Echoing Void DLC indicating that Deepwood Brook can be found to the north-east of the lake. Near the end of the mission, an illager caravan can be found that contains five captured villagers, as well as rudimentary jails made by the Illagers. The area is patrolled by vindicators to deter villagers from escaping, and these illagers attack the hero on sight. To the south-west in the caravan area, a tomb can be found with a button that, if activated, reveals the entrance door of Creeper Woods Rune Room. When a villager is freed it bows in thanks and vanishes, and once all five villagers are released a graveyard can be accessed beyond the gate to the north-west. Further north, a wooden bridge suspended over lava must be crossed to reach a stone brick temple which, if entered, ends the mission. Creeper Pit is an optional sub dungeon within the secret mission Panda Plateau.
Part of: Panda Plateau
Spawn chance: Uncommon
Multiple per layout: No
Raid captains: Never has captains
Creeper Hole is an optional sub dungeon within the secret mission Panda Plateau. It is accessed from Creeper Pit.
Appearance and description
Creeper Pit is located in a sidepath area in Panda Plateau Cave within Panda Plateau. It is a short hallway-like cave featuring glowing mushrooms, jungle foliage, and a large stone creeper head on the wall at the end of the hallway. Within the mouth of the creeper head statue, the entrance door of Creeper Hole can be found. Creeper Hole is a large cave with a descending pathway with a large cubic creeper head statue at the bottom. It contains a single ambush which, if defeated, will reward a single fancy chest and lower the gate that blocked the exit door.
Objectives location
Main article: MCD:Objective
Creeper Pit and Creeper Hole are found during the following Panda Plateau objective: " INTO THE DARK: RESCUE THE PANDAS X/6"

title: Dungeons:Creeping Winter
text: For the free content released alongside this DLC, see MCD:1.4.3.0. This page describes content that is a part of the Creeping Winter DLC. “ A creeping winter spreads across the land, consuming everything it touches – and at the center of the storm is the Wretched Wraith. The only chance to stop the biting frost is for a hero to face new missions and relentless enemies. Hunt down the source of corruption and discover new armour, weapons, and artifacts! „ — In-game pre-purchase description The cover of Creeping Winter. Creeping Winter is the second paid DLC for Minecraft Dungeons. It was released on September 8, 2020. It is the second and final DLC of the Hero Pass and the second DLC set in the island realms. It includes two missions, Frosted Fjord and Lone Fortress, a secret mission, Lost Settlement, and a boss, the Wretched Wraith, found in Lone Fortress. Appearance The map where Creeping Winter takes place, located in the island realms. Creeping Winter is set in an unnamed snowy island. The island contains Frosted Fjord on a river that flows across the majority of the island. The island also contains Lone Fortress and villages dotted around the land. To the north is a large iceberg with Lost Settlement on top. Many spruce trees grow throughout the island. Within certain parts of the river viking-like shipwrecks can be seen in the background. The island is also littered with iron mines. Additions The following is included in Creeping Winter: Armor Added Snow Armor Frost Armor Artifacts Added Ice Wand Weapons Snow Bow Winter's Touch Consumables Cooked Salmon Sweet Brew Effects Blind Freezing Resistance Heroes Frosty Winter Warrior Missions Frosted Fjord Lone Fortress Lost Settlement ( Secret Mission ) Mechanics Added Icy water entrapping heroes within breakable ice. title: Story Mode:Crown Mesa
text: Instructions: Fill empty interactables. Details Type: Settlement Biome: Mesa Leader: PAMA ​ [ formerly ] Population: 1,063 First appearance: Access Denied Latest appearance: Access Denied Crown Mesa is a location in Minecraft: Story Mode that appears in Access Denied. It is located in a Mesa biome. History The town was initially founded by its citizens. Harper developed PAMA with the intent of aiding the townspeople and utilizing monsters for constructive purposes. Over time, however, PAMA concluded that the citizens were inefficient and began implanting chips to make them "useful". Harper successfully evaded being chipped. During the events of the game, Jesse and their companions arrive in Crown Mesa, a town that initially appears deserted. They soon encounter chipped citizens and later meet Harper in one of the houses. Harper is visibly surprised to see unchipped individuals. Shortly after, Jesse, Ivor, Lukas, and Petra are ambushed and captured by the chipped citizens and brought before PAMA. Lukas and Petra are forcibly chipped, leaving Jesse and Ivor to distract PAMA with either a riddle, a logic puzzle, or a self-contradictory statement. Harper intervenes, enabling Jesse and Ivor to escape. Jesse then acquires the transmigrational headset, allowing them to take control of a chipped zombie in Crown Mesa. Using the headset, Jesse jumps control between a skeleton and another zombie while attempting to save Harper from being chipped. Despite their efforts, Harper is ultimately chipped, and the zombie Jesse controls is destroyed by other mobs. After accessing Harper's memories, PAMA begins constructing their Overworld's portal. Jesse, Ivor, and Lukas/Petra return to Crown Mesa to infiltrate PAMA. After successfully defeating PAMA, the chipped citizens are freed from its control. The citizens recognize Jesse and the others as their saviors but express anger toward Harper upon learning she created PAMA. At this point, Jesse can choose Harper's fate, either assuring the citizens that Harper will assist in rebuilding the town or leaving them to decide. With the situation resolved, Jesse, Harper, Ivor, Lukas, and Petra head to the portal to continue their journey through the portal network. Location Crown Mesa is a small village located on the surface of a badlands in a different Overworld. Architecture Crown Mesa appears to consist of a number of small houses built on top of a large plateau on an elevation. The house exteriors are made out of smooth white sandstone stairs on the floors, hardened clay for walls and oak planks for roofs. Culture The town's culture is heavily influenced by the presence of PAMA who governs all aspects of daily life. PAMA enforces strict order and efficiency. The residents of Crown Mesa are forcibly assimilated into PAMA's network through the use of redstone chips implanted in their heads, turning them into "mind-controlled citizens." This creates a uniform and highly regulated environment where individual expression and free will are absent. After PAMA's defeat, Crown Mesa begins to recover its individuality and autonomy. Administration Under PAMA's control, Crown Mesa functioned as an authoritarian society with the computer serving as the sole authority. PAMA dictated all decisions, from resource allocation to the behavior of its citizens, leaving no room for human governance or dissent. Following the events of Access Denied, PAMA is destroyed, and the surviving residents are freed from its control. While the post-PAMA leadership structure is not explicitly detailed, it can be assumed that the town shifts toward a more traditional, self-governed community as its residents regain their autonomy. Points of interest PAMA's internal space Main article: MCSM:PAMA's Internal Space Chipping redstone machine A machine, consisting of a sticky piston and a dispenser, is used to implant chips into mobs. Interactables Chickens Look at Transcription missing. See the documentation to learn how to use the template. Talk to Transcription missing. See the documentation to learn how to use the template. Ivor and Lukas Talk to Transcription missing. See the documentation to learn how to use the template. Petra Talk to Transcription missing. See the documentation to learn how to use the template. Sign Look at Transcription missing. See the documentation to learn how to use the template. Well Look at Transcription missing. See the documentation to learn how to use the template. Townsperson Talk to Transcription missing. See the documentation to learn how to use the template. Inhabitants Characters Harper Harry PAMA (destroyed) Species Chicken Computer Creeper Human Skeleton Spider Zombie Visitors Ivor Jesse Lukas Petra Appearances Access Denied Quotes “ This architecture is fascinating. All sand and clay-based. It's awesome. „ — Lukas, noting the architecture of the houses. “ Welcome to Crown Mesa. Population: 1,063. „ — Jesse, reading a sign. Trivia The population count of Crown Mesa was 1,063 before PAMA took over. It is unknown if the number is still the same. Gallery The portal of Crown Mesa's Overworld Jesse in Crown Mesa Jesse

text:
This page describes content that has been removed from the game.
This feature was present in earlier versions of Minecraft Dungeons, but has since been removed.
Type: Armor
Rarity: UNIQUE
Unused in-game description No one knows where this strange armor came from but it seems familiar to you.
The ender armor is a unique guard's armor variant that can be found exclusively within Minecraft Dungeons Arcade. In Minecraft Dungeons, it remains unused under the name curious armor.
Obtaining
Minecraft Dungeons

Usage
Little is known about the ender armor as it never appeared in the full release. It was intended to have a 5% chance to teleport the hero when hit, but this ability does not appear on the item when edited in using a third-party software. It also makes chicken noises for some reason.

5% chance to teleport when hit
25% artifact cooldown
+10 arrows per bundle

title: Legends:Custom Game
text: The options screen when beginning a Custom Game. Custom Game is a sub-mode of the Campaign and Versus Mode that allows customisation of mechanics, terrain, resources, mobs, and more within Minecraft Legends. Gameplay When beginning a Custom Campaign or Custom Versus Mode, the player is presented with a menu that contains a large number of options. Different options will alter other aspects of how the mode is played. The majority of options apply to both the Campaign and Versus Mode, with a few exceptions. Almost all gameplay differences in Custom Game are controlled by how the player alters the options. The only exception to this is that in a Custom Campaign, the prologue is skipped, meaning the Night Beacon is already defeated. List of options Reason: Update to TU2 The following is a list of all options that exist in Custom Game, organized in the same way as seen in-game. In Versus Mode all options are by default the same as they are in the Campaign's fabled difficulty. World Option name: Storied, Description: Fabled, Default: Mythic, Maximum: Legendary Option name: Terrain Option name: World seed, Description: Unique identifier for each world generation. Generated objects like structures are not determined by the world seed., Default: Random, Maximum: N/A Option name: World size, Description: Alters the size of the generated world, Default: 100%, Maximum: 100%, Minimum: 100%, 100%, 500%, 70% Option name: Gravity Option name: Global gravity, Description: Alters the amount of gravity that the player and allmobsexperience; higher values increase fall speed, and lower values decrease it, Default: 100%, Maximum: 100%, Minimum: 100%, 100%, 500%, 10% Option name: Fall damage, Description: Alters the amount of damage the player and all mobs take from falling, Default: 100%, Maximum: 100%, Minimum: 100%, 100%, 500%, 0% Option name: Fall distance, Description: Alters the distance that the player and all mobs must fall before taking fall damage, Default: 100%, Maximum: 100%, Minimum: 100%, 100%, 500%, 10% Option name: Knockback Option name: Player knockback, Description: Alters the amount of knockback the player's sword attack deals, Default: 100%, Maximum: 100%, Minimum: 100%, 100%, 500%, 10% Option name: Player knockback resistance, Description: Alters how resistance the player is to knockback, Default: 100%, Maximum: 100%, Minimum: 100%, 100%, 500%, 10% Option name: Mob knockback resistance, Description: Alters how resistant non-piglin mobs are to knockback, Default: 100%, Maximum: 100%, Minimum: 100%, 100%, 500%, 10% Option that generate, Default: 2-5, Maximum: 2-5, Minimum: 2-5, 2-5, 10, 0 Option name: Stun towers, Description: Changes the amount of stun towers that generate, Default: 2-5, Maximum: 2-5, Minimum: 2-5, title: Story Mode:Deejay
text:
Note: Expand sections
Details
Gender: Female
Species: Human
Occupation: D.J. at EnderCon
Residence: EnderCon's host town
First appearance: The Order of the Stone
Latest appearance: The Order of the Stone
The deejay or the D.J. is a minor character in Minecraft: Story Mode who first appears in The Order of the Stone.
Appearance
The deejay has light skin, hazel eyes, dark brown hair, and purple lips. She wears a black tank top, blue pants with the cuffs rolled and a chain hanging from the waist, gray shoes, red and gray headphones, and colored bracelets.
Personality
The deejay's personality isn't explored, but she seems to appreciate people who enjoy her music.
Biography

Appearances

The Order of the Stone
Trivia
Jesse can choose to do the Guybrush dance at her music, in which case the deejay winks at them.
This dance is a reference to Tales of Monkey Island, another Telltale game.
The deejay glares at Jesse when they ask her about Ivor.
If Jesse walks up to the deejay, and does nothing, the game does not progress and the player is stuck in that area until restarting the game.

title: Earth:Dairy Cow
text:
For the cosmetic pet in Minecraft Dungeons, see Dungeons:Baby Dairy Cow.
Health points: 10 HP
Behavior: Passive
Mob type: Animal
Hitbox size: Height: 1.4 blocks Width: 0.9 blocks
Spawn: Plains
Rarity: Uncommon
Player journal description Black and white is a bold combination that never goes out of style.
The dairy cow was a black and white variant of the cow found only in Minecraft Earth.
Behavior
Main article: Cow § Behavior
Similar to normal cows, dairy cows wandered around, mooing and breathing occasionally. They avoided water, lava, and walking off cliffs high enough to cause fall damage. They fled for a few seconds when harmed.
Obtaining
Dairy cows were an uncommon mob drop found in cow tappables.
Drops
When killed, dairy cows dropped:
0-2 Leather
1-3 Raw Beef ( Cooked Beef if killed while on fire)
Dairy cows could be milked by using a bucket on it, yielding a milk bucket.

title: Dungeons:The Trial of the Nameless
text: While a name exists in the game code, it has not been used officially by Mojang. This page describes content that is a part of the Echoing Void DLC. Not to be confused with MCD:Shrine or MCD:End Shrine. Part of: Desert Temple Spawn chance: 100% Rare Multiple per layout: No Raid captains: Never has captains The Trial of the Nameless is the collective name for a group of two optional sub dungeons within the mission Desert Temple. It consists of Dark Descent and Sinister Shrine. Dark Descent is an optional sub dungeon within the mission Desert Temple, and is one of the six endersent sightings added by the Echoing Void DLC. Part of: Desert Temple Spawn chance: 100% Rare Multiple per layout: No Raid captains: Never has captains Sinister Shrine is an optional sub dungeon within the mission Desert Temple. It is accessed from Dark Descent, and it contains an eye of ender. Appearance and description Dark Descent is located to the north-west in the area shortly before the battle with the Nameless One in Desert Temple. It is a dimly lit tower suspended over lava, with glowing green candles and several endermen. The gate blocking the entrance door of Dark Descent is lowered by activating the button behind the spike traps to the right of the door. Upon descending to the bottom of Dark Descent, two fancy chests and several emerald urns can be found, as well as the entrance door of Sinister Shrine. Sinister Shrine is a dimly lit sequence of areas that feature a massive number of deadly traps, including spike traps, metal blades, and crushers. The starting area of Sinister Shrine is called Erratic Entry, and it contains two emerald urns accessed by activating a button. The area encountered after Erratic Entry is called Crushing Crescent, and it contains a massive number of crushers leading into an area with a glowing pool that has a gold key golem in the center. Upon entering the area with the glowing pool, an ambush will begin. After defeating said ambush, the gold key golem can be used on the yellow locked gateway in the center of Crushing Crescent, allowing access to further areas in Sinister Shrine. An area with a walkway covered in spike traps suspended over lava is found past the locked gate. This area is called Wily Walkway, and features several buttons that must be activated to progress. Upon activating the buttons, an area with a massive number of metal blades, called Laceration Lane, can be entered. After making it through Laceration Lane, the main area of Sinister Shrine is found, containing an arena suspended over lava that contains an endersent. If the eye of ender has not been collected yet, this endersent will be the Reaping Eye instead. Once the endersent has been defeated, the last area of Sinister Shrine, called Elevated Exit, can be accessed. It contains several palm trees and spring platforms that launch the player towards the exit door of Sinister Shrine. Objectives location Main article: MCD:Objective Dark Descent and Sinister Shrine are found during the following Desert Temple objective: " THE NAMELESS KINGDOM: FETCH THE STAFF" Locations Main title: Dungeons:Death Barter
text:
Rarity: Powerful
Applicable to: Armor
Soul gear only: No
In-game description The first amount of emeralds collected are stored and then spent to save the player from death.
Death Barter is an enchantment that can be found within the various armor of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: Cost 150 Emeralds, Cost: 2 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: Cost 100 Emeralds, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: Cost 50 Emeralds, Cost: 4 Enchantment Points
Usage
Any armor enchanted with Death Barter revives the hero from death at the cost of emeralds. The base cost is 150 emeralds, and it decreases by 50 for every extra tier of the enchantment.
When a hero has armor with the Death Barter enchantment equipped, the first 150, 100, or 50 emeralds collected since the mission started, since their last death, or since the last time they were saved from death are saved towards the enchantment instead of going to their wallet. Once the enchantment is fully charged, further emeralds are collected as normal, and the hero is saved from a single death. If the hero would die with the enchantment fully charged, they are instead healed a portion of their health, and the emerald count resets.
Being saved from death is not dying, and it doesn't trigger effects like the Life Boost enchantment.
Stacking
Death Barter is not stackable, meaning it requires its own enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Built-into
Death Barter tier I is built-into the following armor:
Gilded Glory

Decal Trail is a common enchantment exclusively obtainable by the Heart of Ender.

title: Dungeons:Deflect
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Armor
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Grants a small chance to deflect incoming projectiles.
Deflect is an armor enchantment in Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 25% chance to trigger, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: 35% chance to trigger, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: 45% chance to trigger, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Deflect grants the chance to deflect any enemy projectiles fired at the hero, negating any damage it would have done. The chance of it happening increases with each tier, with a 45% chance for Deflect to activate at the maximum tier.
Stacking
Deflect is not stackable, meaning it must have a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Built-into
Deflect tier I is built-into the following armor:
Shulker Armor
Sturdy Shulker Armor
Mobs
The mobs that are classified with the deflect enchantment:
Tower Guard
Shulker
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Deflect.
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.5.0.0, Decreased trigger chance from 20%/40%/60% to 25%/35%/45%.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Deflect" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.
