title: Legends:The Devourer
text: Details Type: Piglin unit Category: Boss Mob Piglin Health: 15000 Voice actor: Erin Rubin Identifier: badger:piglin_rotters_boss The Devourer is a piglin boss and secondary antagonist in Minecraft Legends. They are one of the generals of The Great Hog, being the leader of the Horde of the Spore. Lore Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Legends that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk! Before the events of the game, The Great Hog summoned all their horde generals to their planning map table where the Devourer watches as the Beast raises their arm up to give them a Horde of the Hunt banner then the Great Hog takes it and smashes it to a place in the map. The Beast nods in agreement and sends piglins towards the portal, and the Great Hog then laughs menacingly. After the Hero defeats multiple Piglin Outposts, back in the Nether the Devourer and the Unbreakable watch the Great Hog choke the Beast; then they throw the Beast towards a wall. The Great Hog afterwards calls upon the Seer and the Great Hog points towards a place in the map, the Seer then asks four piglins to bring them a beacon. After the piglins bring the beacon to the Seer, they corrupt it with the Orb of Dominance. The Seer tells the three piglins to place the corrupted beacon on top of the nether portal within the Night Beacon. After the Hero destroys the night beacon, a piglin comes through the nether portal and rushes towards the Great Hog and their generals to tell them the night beacon was destroyed. After hearing it was destroyed the Great Hog gets angry and with their generals proceeds to catch the piglin and presumably kill it. The Devourer and the other generals return to their respective horde bases. During the war the Hero successfully destroys a piglin base with a nether portal in it. We then cut back to the Nether where we see the Devourer in a soup bath eating spores, Overworld food and animals, a piglin approaches the Devourer to tell them that the Hero successfully destroyed a portal but they are too busy eating. A piglin with a purple flower approaches them and they eat the flower along with the piglin carrying it. The Devourer spits out a spore and the piglin from the destroyed Nether portal catches and admires it. Then a group of piglins pile on the piglin trying to steal it. We then cut back to the Devourer eating and laughing. Once again the Hero successfully destroys a piglin outpost with a nether portal in it and a piglin approaches the Devourer, who is still eating in the soup bath. The piglin gets the attention of the Devourer and they roar at them. The piglin tells the Devourer what has happened and the Devourer calls upon three Sporebacks and the Devourer tells one Sporeback to pluck an explosive mushroom from its back and throw it, causing piglins near the mushroom to be flung into the air. The Devourer then tells the same Sporeback to give them one explosive mushroom, the Devourer proceeds to eat this mushroom causing them to be flung into the air a bit and smoke to come out of their mouth and nose. The Devourer then tells the three Sporebacks to go to the Overworld. The Devourer rose from their soup bath and jumped towards a clear field where a group of piglins are and they rallied those piglins and the piglins cheered on. Meanwhile in the Overworld the Hero successfully destroyed the Horde of the Spore's final piglin outpost with the nether portal and an injured piglin went back into the nether with bandages and tried to get the Devourer's attention to tell them that the last nether portal had been destroyed, but the piglin was not successful as the Devourer was still rallying their troops. After the Devourer rallied their piglins they puked on them. Afterwards they charged into the portal the injured piglin was near, entering into it, though one of the Devourer's pink spores fell onto the injured piglin's back, causing the piglin to try to eat it. The Devourer then comes through the portal and into the Overworld though they struggled to get out of the portal for a bit. Once the Devourer was out of the portal they noticed one of their pink spores are missing so they grew it back. The Hero travels to the piglin outpost to defeat the Devourer, after a ferocious and long battle the Hero weakened the Devourer. They start puking everywhere, before all of the spores on their back combust and explode. They then fall over backwards and let out one final burp. The base's portal self-destructs shortly afterward. Appearance

text:
For the normal mob, see MCD:Key Golem. For other uses, see Key.
Health points: 1
Behavior: Passive
Classification: Friendlies

A diamond key golem is a variant of the key golem used to unlock diamond gates. It runs from heroes and retreats to its original location.

Behavior
Diamond key golems remain asleep until a hero comes near, at which point they awaken and attempt to flee. A hero must attack a key golem to collect it, causing it to strap itself onto the hero's back. While being carried, the golem squirms and cries out in fear. If the hero is damaged, the key golem escapes and returns to its original location. Certain mobs are also capable of picking up key golems, and if such a mob is killed, the golem returns to its original location.
Fake diamond key golem
Health points: 1

A fake diamond key golem is a variant that cannot be acquired by heroes and functions as a decoy for the real key.

End Wilds
Behavior
A large number of fake diamond key golems appear in the same map structure. They have the same health as the real diamond key golem but cannot be acquired by heroes when hit. Only one real diamond key golem is hidden among them.

title: Dungeons:Dingy Jungle
text: Part of: Island Realms Boss (arcade): Jungle Abomination Minimum difficulty: II Unlock predecessor: Squid Coast Raid captains: Usually has all six captains but not always Daily trials: Can have daily trials Entangled in Vines We've tracked the Orb's shard to the edge of a dense jungle where dangerous creatures creep out from under the thick canopy. What brave hero would dare cut through the twisting vines to reach the dark power that hides within? Dingy Jungle is the first island realms mission in Minecraft Dungeons, and is part of the Jungle Awakens DLC. It is unlocked after the completion of Squid Coast, and completing Dingy Jungle unlocks Overgrown Temple. It has a rare chance to generate the Fossil Canyon optional sub dungeon that contains a scroll which, if collected, unlocks the Panda Plateau secret mission. Appearance and description Dingy Jungle is a dense, humid jungle covered in ruins and statues. It features constant rainfall, jungle foliage, statues of various mobs, torches, and mobs corrupted by the Orb of Dominance like whisperers and leapleafs. Near the start of the mission, an elevated stone ruin can be found where a switch must be turned to start an ambush that, if cleared, will lower the gate. North-east of this ruin, a large stone brick wall with many switches can be found. Pressing the bottom-middle button will open the gates that prevented access to the bottom-left and bottom-right buttons. Pressing the bottom-left and bottom-right buttons will open the gates on the floors above them, granting access to another set of two buttons. The top-left and top-right buttons if pressed will start ambushes and grant access to the top-middle button, which itself will lower the gate to the right, allowing access further into the mission. Shortly after, an area covered in the roots of a giant tree can be found, where another stone ruin to the south-east can be found surrounded by quick growing vines that contains another ambush that must be cleared. Halfway through the mission, an area with bridges connecting the tops of jungle tree canopies can be found, where a sidepath can rarely generate that contains the entrance door of Fossil Canyon, an optional sub dungeon containing the scroll that unlocks Panda Plateau. Near the end of the mission, two giant statues of mobs can be found before and after the exit door of the mission. The mobs that the two statues depict are the solutions to the rotating cube puzzle next to the exit door of the mission. Solving the puzzle will start one final ambush that, if cleared, will open the door to the temple, a temple which if entered will end the mission. Go Through the Gateway Voiceovers “ The Orb of Dominance may have shattered, but its powerful shards have scattered across the vast Overworld. One of those shards has landed in a lush jungle, where new threats roar to life beneath the dark canopies. The shard, the source of corruption must be destroyed to free this land from the Orb's dark manipulations. „ — Opening voiceover “ The journey has just begun. For the corrupting shard lies deeper still within the heart of the jungle. „ — Ending voiceover Mobs and entities Main article: MCD:Mob Minecraft Dungeons Mob: Default, Difficulty: Adventure, Apocalypse Mob: Non-attackable Mob: Firefly,

title: Dungeons:Dipping Poison
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Ranged weapon
Soul gear only: No
In-game description When you use a health potion, you are given a quiver of Poison Arrows.
Dipping Poison is an enchantment that can be found within the various ranged weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 6 poison arrows, Cost: 1 enchantment point
Tier: II, Effect: 8 poison arrows, Cost: 2 enchantment points
Tier: III, Effect: 12 poison arrows, Cost: 3 enchantment points
Usage
Any ranged weapon enchanted with Dipping Poison grants the hero poison-tipped arrows upon use of a health potion. The number of poison arrows increases with each tier, with the maximum being 12 poison arrows.
Stacking
Dipping Poison is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Sounds

Activate 2, D6_sfx_item_dippingPoison_Activate02
Activate 3, D6_sfx_item_dippingPoison_Activate03
Poison Arrow
Type:?
Poison arrows are a type of ammo that function similarly to regular arrows except after hitting a mob, poison arrows inflict poison on that mob for ten seconds. The poison damage dealt by poison arrows is increased by using the Poison Focus enchantment.
Obtaining
Poison arrows are obtained by using the Dipping Poison enchantment on ranged weapons.

title: Story Mode:Diving Helmet
text:
Details
Owner: Deep Dwellers of Han'bar Crevasse ​ [ formerly ] Jack
First appearance: Hero in Residence
Latest appearance: Hero in Residence
A diving helmet is a rigid head equipment which allows the user to breath underwater. It was used by Jesse and several others when diving underwater in Doom Sea. The helmets they used are enchanted.
History
Jack obtained the helmets from the "Deep Dwellers of Han'bar Crevasse" and may have used them the first time he went to the Sea Temple. When he brings Jesse and Petra with him to an island, he digs them up from a chest in the sand where he had hid them. They use them to dive under the water and enter the temple.
Properties
The helmet grants the wearer the ability to breathe underwater, seemingly indefinitely. The helmet notably has a glint, alluding to some kind of enchantment such as respiration, albeit a much stronger type.
Appearances
Hero in Residence
Trivia
A diver's helmet appears in A Minecraft Movie, among other memorabilia in Steve's Stash.

title: Earth:Muddy Pig
text:
Health points: 10 HP
Behavior: Passive
Mob type: Animal
Hitbox size: Height: 0.9 blocks (excluding flower) Width: 0.9 blocks
Spawn: Plains
Rarity: Epic
Player journal description The Muddy Pig is a mud bath connoisseur. You wouldn't know it at first glance, but they have a great sense of style, too.
The muddy pig was a passive mob that served as a muddy variant of the pig. Muddy pigs were also of epic rarity.
The dried muddy pig was a passive mob that served as a dried variant of the muddy pig. Dried muddy pigs were also an epic rarity.
Behavior
If a pig was in the mud, it became a muddy pig.
Muddy pigs oink and typically roam in small groups avoiding lava, hazards and cliffs high enough to cause falls. A muddy pig flees for some seconds when harmed.
If the muddy pig was in its dry state, it searched for mud. If it found mud, it jumped into the mud and rolled around until it regained its muddy appearance.
Obtaining
Muddy pigs were of epic rarity, found in pig tappables, with a 0.5% chance to get one, and appeared on " Sleepy Village " buildplate.
Drops
When a muddy pig was killed, it had a chance of dropping the following:
1-3 Raw Porkchop ( Cooked Porkchop if killed while on fire)

text:
Health points: 2500
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Aquatic, Undead, Powerful
Attack strength: Trident Storm:200 Steam Missile:50
A drowned necromancer is a powerful necromancer variant that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons.

Drowned necromancers have three attacks that they utilize to fend off heroes.
Basic attack
Drowned necromancers shoot several steam projectiles at heroes, dealing a small amount of damage on impact.
Strong attack
Drowned necromancers summon drowned, trident drowned, and their variants to assist them. They usually summon about two drowned at a time.
Trident storm
Drowned necromancers wave their trident in the water, conjuring tridents to fall from above within 2 seconds. The tridents explode on impact, and fall at random times in a large area around the necromancer.

title: Legends:Dry Savanna
text:
Resources: Iron Stone Wood
Boosts: Regeneration Stone Speed Wheat
Hazards: Redthorn
Structures: Village
Mounts: Regal Tiger
A dry savanna is a flat biome within Minecraft Legends.
Description
Dry savannas are biomes covered with dried grass and populated by decaying trees. Veins of iron and speed wheat can be found in this biome. The First of Brick generates disassembled here on a stone outcrop.
Locations
Village
In the Campaign, villages may generate in one of the dry savanna biomes.
First of Brick
In the Campaign, the First of Brick may be found disassembled in one of the dry savanna biomes.
Mobs
The following mobs can spawn in dry savannas:
Regal Tiger
Quotes
“ An arid landscape of dried grass, stone outcrops and small watering holes decorated with strange coral plants. „ — Map legend
“ DRY SAVANNA – An arid landscape of dried grass and stone outcrops, you'd be forgiven for thinking life would be a struggle in this savanna. Explore here if you need to gather valuable iron ore, but don't leave until you witness its intriguing watering holes and gather any iron you find. „ — Minecraft Legends:
A Hero's Guide to Saving the Overworld
History
Minecraft Legends: 1.17.24827, Added dry savannas.

About the game
Minecraft Dungeons is a dungeon crawler RPG released on May 26, 2020 and developed by Mojang Studios and Double Eleven, and published by Xbox Game Studios where a group of up to four players can travel through randomly generated levels, combat monsters, and confront the main antagonist of the game, the Arch-Illager.
There is also an arcade adaptation of the game known as Minecraft Dungeons Arcade.
On September 28, 2023, Mojang announced that the game would receive no further updates.
A sequel, Minecraft Dungeons II, was announced at Minecraft Live – March 2026, to be released later in the year.
Learn more about Dungeons
Learn more about Dungeons II
Beginner's guide
Featured article
Woodland Mansion is a sub-mission within Ancient Hunt in Minecraft Dungeons. It is composed of hallways and rooms littered with illagers, and it features hunt doors which lead to Gold Room or one of the various ancient dungeons. Atop Roof, charged creepers and lightning rods can be found. This mansion is the one where the Arch-Illager was raised, and is described in The Rise of the Arch-Illager.

title: Dungeons:Concept art
text: There is a category for this topic! See Category:Minecraft Dungeons concept art for a list of pages relating to this topic. An example of Minecraft Dungeons concept art. Concept art is artwork created by concept artists that shows early iterations of features and cut features from Minecraft Dungeons. Various pages of concept art have been shared by the developers of Minecraft Dungeons across various art websites. These pages show early iterations of items and locations, as well as ones that did not make the final cut. Fang Jing concept artwork page 1 Solid climbing gear The solid climbing gear is an armor set featured in concept art for the Howling Peaks DLC. It had three early design iterations, showing different possible color variations. It was implemented in the form of the rugged climbing gear. Emerald heart The emerald heart is an armor set featured in concept art for the Howling Peaks DLC. It had one early design iteration, showing its appearance and emphasizing how an emerald was placed on its chestplate. It was implemented in the form of the emerald gear, the opulent armor, and the gilded glory. Ancient Guardian The Ancient Guardian is a mob featured in concept art for the Hidden Depths DLC. It had one early design iteration, showing a more similar texture to normal guardians with a sepia-toned texture, as well as exposed blood and bones. It was implemented in the form of the Ancient Guardian. Bard's garb The bard's garb is an armor set featured in concept art for the Echoing Void DLC. It had one early design iteration, and featured a luke on the back. It was implemented in the form of the entertainer's garb and the troubadour. Shulker ranger The shulker ranger is an armor set featured in concept art for the Echoing Void DLC. It had one early design iteration, showing an appearance that featured more of its design being made of shulker material. It was implemented in the form of the shulker armor and the sturdy shulker armor. Snareling The snareling is a mob featured in concept art for the Echoing Void DLC. It had many early design iterations, showing a purple-colored design with various designs for its arms and mouth. It was implemented in the form of the snareling. Enderpod The enderpod is a mob featured in concept art for the Echoing Void DLC. It had one early design iteration, showing different possible particles that it would emit. Gruntling The gruntling is a mob featured in concept art for the Echoing Void DLC. It had eleven early design iterations, showing different designs for its body and arms. It was implemented in the form of the watchling. Fang Jing concept artwork page 2 Windspeed bow The windspeed bow is a ranged weapon featured in concept art for the Howling Peaks DLC. It had five early design iterations, showing different possible color variations. It was implemented in the form of the wind bow, the echo of the valley, and the burst gale bow. Trident The trident is a melee weapon featured in concept art for the Hidden Depths DLC. It had eight early design iterations, four made of gold, two made of bones, and two made of steel. It was implemented in the form of the trident consumable, rather than as a melee weapon. Anchor The anchor is a melee weapon featured in concept art for the Hidden Depths DLC. It had twelve early design iterations, four made of steel, two made of gold, one wrapped with seaweed, and four with chains attached to them. It was implemented in the form of the anchor and the encrusted anchor. Flashbang crossbow The flashbang crossbow is a ranged weapon featured in concept art for the Hidden Depths DLC. It had six early design iterations, three depicting a common variant and three depicting a unique variant. Eye of the guardian The eye of the guardian is an artifact featured in concept art for the Hidden Depths DLC. It had four early design iterations, two resembling guardians and two resembling elder guardians. It was implemented in the form of the eye of the guardian. Gem scimitars The gem scimitars are a dual-wielded melee weapon featured in concept art for the Echoing Void DLC. It had two early design iterations. Master backstabber The master backstabber is a knife-like weapon featured in concept art for the Echoing Void DLC. It had eight early design iterations. It was implemented in the form of the backstabber and the swift striker. Fang Jing concept artwork page 3 Void shards The void shards are a level prop featured in concept art for the Echoing Void DLC. They had eleven early design iterations, eight small shards and three larger cubic shards. It was implemented in the form of a level prop in End

title: Dungeons:Creeper
text:
Health points: 100
Behavior: Hostile
Attack strength: Explosion: 300
A creeper is a hostile mob that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. When within melee range, it will quickly run at the player before exploding.
Spawning
Creepers can spawn throughout the following missions:
Creeper Woods
Creepy Crypt
Woodland Mansion
Woodland Prison
Soggy Swamp
Soggy Cave
Pumpkin Pastures
Arch Haven

Spider Cave
Redstone Mines

Desert Temple ( Arcade )
Highblock Halls (Outside)
Underhalls (Cellar)
Dingy Jungle
Panda Plateau
Overgrown Temple
Frozen Fjord ( Arcade )
Gale Sanctum
Gauntlet of Gales
Colossal Rampart
Radiant Ravine ( Secret area )
The Stronghold
Broken Citadel ( Cinematic )
Daily Trial
Ancient Hunt
Tower
Treetop Tangle
Creepers can also spawn during night mode, regardless of the mission, when at least one hero dies in multiplayer.
Behavior
Creepers become passive if an ocelot is nearby. They also run away if they are currently being attacked by an ocelot.
Ignition
When within range of a target, creepers ignite themselves, which also increases their movement speed. After 1.5 seconds, it will explode to deal heavy damage to heroes within the explosion's radius.

History
Minecraft Dungeons: September 8, 2019, The developers experimented with throw-able creepers that would explode a few seconds after landing, but scrapped this idea in favor of TNT and regular creepers.
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added creepers.

“ CREEPER – To stop creepers from blowing you up, take them out with a bow from a distance or hit them quickly before they go boom! „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons:
A Handbook for Heroes

Creepers in Minecraft Dungeons are darker and smoother than their original counterparts.
Creepers in the base game are not capable of moving while ignited like creepers in Minecraft Dungeons can.

title: Dungeons:Enchantment
text: An enchantment is an ability that augments weapons and armor by applying enchantment points. Enchantment points can be obtained by leveling up, and once applied to an item, can only be retrieved by salvaging it, upgrading it with the blacksmith or changing an enchantment with the Enchantsmith. This weapon can be enchanted with Radiance twice. Overview Enchantment slots Weapons and armor each have up to three enchantment slots, with up to three random choices per slot. One enchantment can be selected from the available choices to apply onto the equipment. Each enchantment can be extended up to 3 tiers. The number of available enchantment slots on collected equipment and the type of enchantments on them depends on the difficulty and the hero’s threat level. Unavailable enchantment slots or enchantment choices cannot be unlocked. All enchantments are either stackable or non-stackable. Stackable enchantments be stacked up to three times. Once an enchantment has been applied, the only way to clear it is to use the Blacksmith, which upgrades the item and returns all invested enchantment points, or the Enchantsmith, which returns all invested enchantment points on a particular enchantment. Salvaging the item will also return all invested enchantment points. There are two types of enchantments: common and powerful. Common enchantments require less enchantment points than powerful enchantments. Gilded items have increased Enchantment Point costs. Type: I, Tier: II, Total: III Type: Common, Tier: 1, Total: 2, 3, 6 Type: Powerful, Tier: 2, Total: 3, 4, 9 Type: Gilded +Common, Tier: 2, Total: 3, 4, 9 Type: Gilded +Powerful, Tier: 3, Total: 4, 5, 12 Instantaneous enchantments behave independently when stacked. A weapon with two Chains III, each having a 30% chance to occur, does not give a 60% chance to occur, but instead a combined 51% chance, including a 9% chance that the effect occurs twice. However, a weapon enchanted with two rampaging III, there will be a 10% chance to apply 30 seconds of rampaging, as opposed to 15 seconds individually. Built-in enchantments When dealing with equipment possessing a built-in enchantment, that enchantment is unavailable as a choice to apply onto that particular piece of equipment, even if the built-in enchantment is normally stackable. (However, it will still start with that enchantment) For example, the diamond pickaxe never has Prospector as an option, the firebolt thrower never has Chain Reaction as an option, the nimble turtle armor never has Rush as an option, and so on. The only exception to this is in the Tower, where the uniquesmith can turn a normal item into a unique item while keeping its enchantments. Built-in enchantments are shown as the enchantment icon with an orange border on the equipment's properties list and are visible in-game through the enchantment notification icon and enchantment effects. Also, the Status Effect icon relating to the enchantment appears on the Heads-Up Display upon the use of non-enchanted equipment. Enchantments that are built-in cannot be upgraded, altered, or removed. Soul enchantments Enchantments that involve souls, with the exceptions of Bag of Souls and Soul Focus, occur only on weapons and armor that provide soul gathering naturally, such as the soul knife, soul bow, or soul robe and their respective unique variants. Soul gathering equipment can possess non-soul enchantments the same as regular equipment. Anima Conduit, Enigma Resonator, and Soul Siphon are soul enchantments that occur on both melee and ranged weapons. Soul Speed occurs on armor. Gilded gear Gilded gear in the inventory. For a list of Ancients and the gilded gear they drop, see MCD:Ancient. Gilded gear is obtainable to a maximum power of 263. Gilded gear can be obtained by: Defeating ancient mobs within Ancient Hunt Bartering with the Piglin Merchant using gold. Completing the Tower when at a high enough level. Gilded gear is melee weapons, ranged weapons and armor that possess an extra built-in enchantment at tier I, II, or III. Gilded gear have increased enchantment costs to activate and upgrade the enchantments within the slots. Gilded items appear in the inventory with a gilded gold background and a golden tag adjacent to the rarity. On lower difficulties, the hero may receive a tier I enchantment, but on higher levels the hero can receive up to a tier III enchantment. All weapons and armor currently in the game have gilded variants that can be bought or dropped from a certain ancient mob. Prior to the release of 1.12.0.0, a DLC-exclusive ancient mob like the Swarm drops items exclusive to the DLC it comes from. Gilded gear can be salvaged for gold rather than the usual emeralds with regular gear. Ancient Hunt does not allow gilded gear to be used as an offering. Special event items equipment can be obtained gilded through the Tower. Enchanted mobs Main articles: Enchanted Mobs and Ancient Mobs Enchanted mobs are

The Minecraft Dungeons Launcher was the login and downloader front-end to the Minecraft Dungeons client, that was available for Windows. Its purpose, before being later merged into the Minecraft Launcher on November 27, 2020, with 2.2.74x, was to launch the Game under a Microsoft account as at the time the Minecraft Launcher was incapable of supporting Microsoft accounts. Soon after the merge the Minecraft Dungeons Launcher became incapable of launching Minecraft Dungeons on January 18, 2021.
While the Minecraft Dungeons Launcher is still downloadable, it simply displays a message telling the user to play in the Minecraft Launcher.

title: Dungeons:Mob
text: The specific instructions are: Ancient Mob faces A mooshroom, a blaze, an ocelot, a jungle zombie, an iceologer, and a soul wizard, some of the many mobs in Minecraft Dungeons. A mob (short for " mobile ", " mobile entity ", or " mobile object ") is an AI-driven video game entity. Minecraft Dungeons and its upcoming sequel shares many mobs with the original game, while also featuring several that are exclusive to it. Mobs in Minecraft Dungeons are mainly classified as either Enemies or Friendlies, depending on their behavior toward the player. Minecraft Dungeons A list of every mob in Minecraft Dungeons. title: Dungeons:Weapon
text: For the weapons in Minecraft, see Weapon. The Sickles and its unique variants, Nightmare's Bite and The Last Laugh, are examples of weapons from Minecraft Dungeons. A weapon is an item equippable in the melee or ranged slot used to deal damage to mobs in Minecraft Dungeons. Obtaining Weapons can be obtained by opening chests, defeating mobs, exchanging emeralds with merchants in Camp, and from gifting via the gift wrapper. There are 83 obtainable melee weapons in total, including 56 uniques, seven of which are seasonal event -exclusive. There is one unused melee weapon. There are 72 ranged weapons in total, including 48 uniques, seven of which are seasonal event -exclusive. There is one unused ranged weapon. Power level A weapon's power level affects its stats, with a higher power level weapon having higher damage output than a lower power one of the same type. The power level of a weapon can be increased at the blacksmith at the cost of emeralds and the completion of three missions or secret missions. The maximum power level for weapons received from merchants, ordinary missions, and blacksmith upgrading is 251 for rare and unique rarity items and 250 for common rarity items. The maximum power level for weapons received through daily trial and raid captain reward chests is 256. Raid captain reward chests can surpass the 256 limit and reach a maximum of 263 by collecting all six raid banners in a tier three daily trial. Weapons obtained by completing The Tower also reach a maximum power of 263. Enchanting Main article: MCD:Enchanting Both melee and ranged weapons can be enchanted from the inventory. Weapons can have up to three enchantment sections which contain up to three enchantment slots. An enchantment can be chosen within each enchantment section to provide its properties, and can be further upgraded to a second and third level. Enchantment slots can be rerolled using the enchantsmith by exchanging gold. Weapons are unenchanted when upgraded using the blacksmith or modified using the enchantsmith. Unique weapons often feature built-in tier one enchantments, and gilded weapons feature another built-in enchantment that can reach up to tier three. Gilded weapons can be obtained within Ancient Hunt and from the piglin merchant. Melee weapons "Melee Weapon" redirects here. For melee weapons in Minecraft, see Weapon § Melee weapon. Melee weapons are weapons that can be used to damage nearby entities. Close range combat is the hero's unlimited and reliable source of damage output with no ammo requirement. However, the immediate proximity to mobs positions the hero within melee mobs' attack ranges, risking loss of health and receiving negative status effects such as becoming Frozen. Ranged mobs also take advantage of the time taken for the melee hero to approach them. Long ranged melee weapons such as glaives can help keep a distance between the hero and mobs or received damage can be mitigated and nullified by consistent healing with Radiance on high speed weapons such as the fighter's bindings. If no melee weapons are equipped, the hero's fists are used to inflict 1 damage point to mobs. Combos Combo length is the number of different attack movements in a Melee weapon's attack sequence. The final attack of a combo triggers Shockwave and Swirling enchantments and inflict the maximum damage specified for the weapon in the inventory. A dyed cat was a variant of a cat with dyed purple fur found only in Minecraft Earth. The name was announced with the Series 19 mini figures. It also appeared as a minifigure in a LEGO Minecraft set called The First Adventure.
Trivia
Ledski revealed that the dyed cat’s name in his profile picture has the initials of PB.
Ledski revealed that PB is a grey cat.
It was also revealed by Ledski, one of the Minecraft Earth developers, that the dyed cat, PB, is actually just a grey cat with dyed fur, and its purple dye is actually purple goo.
Ledski revealed that PB stands for Peanut Butter.

title: Dungeons:Dynamo
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Melee Weapon Ranged Weapon
Dynamo is an enchantment that can be found within the various melee and ranged weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Dynamo has a chance to appear in any enchantment slot on a melee or ranged weapon. Enchantments on items can be swapped out for Dynamo by rerolling them at the enchantsmith.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: +X damage, Cost: 1 enchantment point, Cost (Gilded): 2 enchantment points
Tier: II, Effect: +1.25X damage, Cost: 2 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 3 enchantment points
Tier: III, Effect: +1.5X damage, Cost: 3 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 4 enchantment points
Built-into
Dynamo tier I is built-into the following weapons:
Great Axeblade
Corrupted Crossbow
Ancient Bow
Mobs
Mobs can never be enchanted with Dynamo.
Stacking
Dynamo is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Usage
Any melee weapon or ranged weapon enchanted with Dynamo increases the damage of the next attack after rolling. The effect can stack up to twenty times, and the amount of times it has been stacked is shown as a status effect.

About the game
Minecraft Earth was a free-to-play augmented reality mobile game developed by Mojang Studios and published by Xbox Game Studios for iOS, iPadOS, and Android where players could travel to real-life locations to build structures and collect resources. It featured numerous unique gameplay aspects and interactions resembling the main game. The first closed beta in the US released on July 16, 2019, and was released worldwide on December 11, 2019.
It received its last update on January 5, 2021, and was shut down on June 30 later that year as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic, and can no longer be played.
Learn more
Featured article
The moolip was a counterpart of the moobloom, itself a flowery variation of the mooshroom. Instead of buttercups or mushrooms, they are devoted to pink daisies.

title: Dungeons:Echo
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Melee Weapon
In-game description Some of your attacks can be followed up by another attack in quick succession.
Echo is an enchantment that can be found within the various melee weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Echo has a chance to appear in any enchantment slot on a melee weapon. Enchantments on items can be swapped out for Echo by rerolling them at the enchantsmith.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 5 seconds cooldown, Cost: 1 enchantment point, Cost (Gilded): 2 enchantment points
Tier: II, Effect: 4 seconds cooldown, Cost: 2 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 3 enchantment points
Tier: III, Effect: 3 seconds cooldown, Cost: 3 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 4 enchantment points
Built-into
Echo tier I is built-into the following weapons:
Swift Striker
Whispering Spear
Spine-Chill Spear
Mobs
Mobs can only be enchanted with Echo if the "50% of melee mobs have the Echo enchantment" modifier is active.
Stacking
Echo is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Usage
When Echo is applied to an item, an automatic second attack is performed after the hero attacks, followed by a cooldown period. In this cooldown period, the hero can still perform a single attack. After the cooldown period is over, the hero is able to use echo again. The cooldown period shortens by one second for each tier, at the maximum tier the cooldown period is three seconds.
Mobs
When enchanted with this enchantment, mobs repeat their attacks immediately after the initial attack. There is no cooldown for this effect.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Echo.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Echo" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.
Gallery
Shine pattern for Echo's enchantment icon

title: Dungeons:Echoing Void
text: For the free content released alongside this DLC, see MCD:1.10.2.0. Note: Somehow get in touch with the developers to find out the official names of the three End Illagers. This page describes content that is a part of the Echoing Void DLC. “ TO THE END — You’ve followed the story of the Arch-Illager and found the shards from the Orb of Dominance – now it’s time to reach the End. The final chapter of Minecraft Dungeons unfolds in Echoing Void, the newest DLC. Face new enemies, collect gear, and make your way through challenging new missions to end this fight – once and for all. „ — In-game pre-purchase description The cover of Echoing Void. Echoing Void is the sixth and final paid DLC for Minecraft Dungeons, ignoring the Adventure Pass. It was released on July 28, 2021. It is the fourth and final DLC of the Season Pass, the first part set in the mainland, and the second part set in the other dimensions. It concludes the narrative of the Orb of Dominance. It includes three missions, The Stronghold, the End Wilds, and the Broken Citadel; and a boss, the Vengeful Heart of Ender, found in the Broken Citadel. It is the first and only DLC to not include at least one secret mission. Appearance The first map where Echoing Void takes place, located in the mainland. The second map where Echoing Void takes place, located in the other dimensions. Echoing Void is based on the End dimension in the base game. The hero starts out in a stronghold beneath a small island. The island is made up of spruce trees growing on a small hill. On the island is a short path leading from the beach to a small cave entrance. Beneath the cave entrance is the stronghold's portal room, holding nothing but an End Portal, which the player activates with collected Eyes of Ender. The portal leads to the southern area of the Echoing Void map, the End Wilds, which is made up of floating islands with chorus trees. An End City can be seen near the southeast area. Two End gateways can be seen on the eastern side, presumably connecting the southern and the northern areas. In the northern area, Broken Citadel, multiple buildings can be seen on floating islands, which are connected via bridges. Void liquid can be seen pouring from some islands. Near the northwest corner, there is a large circular area—the city’s capital— surrounded by rocks, where the final battle against the Vengeful Heart of Ender takes place. Lore Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Dungeons that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk! After the orb shattered, it started to spread corruption in far off lands. One shard fell into a cave, where 2 endermen took it to The End. Causing corruption across the land, the shard awaited direct vengeance against the heroes. Creatures with 9 eyes appeared. With corruption locking access to The End, the heroes find Eyes of Ender. They travel by flying towards the shard and eventually breach the Citadel’s guards, giving them access to shattering and ending this vengeance. Additions The following is included in this DLC pack: Achievements Main article: MCD:Achievement § Echoing Void Avoidable Burning Up Diver's Dozen End Run Heartbreaker I Need Them All Take the High Road To the End! Treasure Trove Armor Entertainer's Garb The Troubadour Shulker Armor Sturdy Shulker Armor Teleportation Robes Unstable Robes Artifacts Shadow Shifter Tome of Duplication Vexing Chant Void Quiver Unused Icon Map ( Unused ) Consumables Burning Brew Chorus Fruit Elytra Eye of Ender Projectiles Shulker Bullet Snareling Goop Effects Added Thrive Under Pressure Void Touched Voided Enchantments Added Ambush Dipping Poison Levitation Shot Shadow Barb Shadow Blast Shadow Shot Shadow Surge Shared Pain Thrive Under Pressure Void Strike Heroes Added Corrupted Defender Void Voyager Unused End Rogue ( Unused ) Missions Added Missions that are first introduced by the Echoing Void DLC: Gauntlet of Gales (Free) The Stronghold End Wilds Broken Citadel Included Missions that are updated by the Echoing Void DLC: Pumpkin Pastures Highblock Halls Soggy Swamp Desert Temple Cacti Canyon Creeper Woods Mechanics Void blocks and liquid found in End missions that cause the Voided and Void Touched effects respectively. Entities Added Blastling Endermite ( Cosmetic pet ) Endermite Endersent Binding Eye Blight Eye Ravenous Eye Reaping Eye Savage Eye Spiked Eye End illager [ unofficial name ] ( Cinematic ) Guardian Vex ( Summonable ) Imperfect Heart of Ender Rogue ( Unseen ) Shulker Silverfish Scuttling Torment ( Ancient silverfish) Snareling The Swarm ( Ancient snareling) Survivor ( Unseen ) Trader Llama ( Cinematic ) Vengeful Heart of Ender ( Boss ) Voice In The Darkness ( Unseen ) Wandering Trader ( Cinematic ) Watchling

Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Elaine" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.
In other media
While frantically sweeping their cackling broom against a target dummy while wearing some sturdy bubbling cauldron armor, the hero Elaine is admired by the zombified baby pig.
The zombified baby pig, upon deciding to stick with Elaine, attempts to see a connection between the hero’s bravery and strength with the combined match of their phantom cape and phantom bow.
In the midst of a battle, the zombified baby pig marvels at Elaine’s bravery and honor as they cut down foes with their gnarly skull scythe and elegant webbed bow.
Elaine’s wondrous spooky gourdian armor causes the zombified baby pig to come to the realization that it did not matter that it missed the Pet Parade, as it relished proudly having been apart of the Spookier Fall.
Sometime earlier or later, Elaine wears the troubadour while wielding a harp crossbow within the Pumpkin Pastures, the Soggy Swamp, and the Frosted Fjord.

title: Dungeons:Electrified
text:
Find out what patch changed Electrified's description
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Armor
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Rolling zaps two nearby enemies with lightning bolts, dealing damage.
Electrified is an armor enchantment in Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: Deals X damage, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: Deals 2X damage, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: Deals 3X damage, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
The Electrified enchantment creates lightning bolts when the hero rolls. A maximum of two nearby enemies can take damage from these bolts. The damage dealt by the bolts increase with each tier.
The damage dealt by Electrified is increased by the Lightning Focus enchantment.
Stacking
Electrified is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Mobs
Enchanted mobs with the enchantment will emit an electrical burst every 5 seconds, dealing major damage to nearby heroes.
Stats
In-game, values can vary even in the same power level, and may not be exactly as seen below.
Powerlevel: Tier I, Damage dealt: Tier II, Tier III
Powerlevel: 226, Damage dealt: 134685, 269370, 404055
Powerlevel: 263, Damage dealt: 601608, 1203215, 1804823
Sounds
Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier
Electric zap, sfx_env_thunderZap_soundWave
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Electrified.
Minecraft Dungeons:?, Decreased the amount of enemies that can be zapped from three to two.
Minecraft Dungeons: Changed description from "Rolling zaps three nearby enemies with lightning bolts, dealing damage." to "Rolling zaps two nearby enemies with lightning bolts, dealing damage.".
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.9.1.0, Decreased activation delay when used by enchanted mobs from 5 seconds to 4 seconds.
Minecraft Dungeons: Decreased damage dealt when used by enchanted mobs from 150 to 90.
Minecraft Dungeons: The two above changes effectively decreased Electrified's damage per second when used by enchanted mobs from 30 to 22.5.

title: Story Mode:Ellegaard the Redstone Engineer
text:
Note: Empty sections
Details
Gender: Female
Species: Human
Status: Determinant
Aliases: Ellegaard the Redstone Engineer Ellie
Affiliations: Order of the Stone
Allies: See § Allies
First appearance: The Order of the Stone
Latest appearance: A Block and a Hard Place
Actor: Grey DeLisle
Ellegaard the Redstone Engineer, more commonly referred to as Ellegaard or Ellie, is a redstone engineer, a member of the Order of the Stone and a major character in the first season of Minecraft: Story Mode. She is voiced by Grey DeLisle.
Appearance
She wears armor that has a brown tinge to it and a bright red around the collar. In the flashbacks, she can be seen with goggles and a helmet. However, meeting her in person, she doesn't have her headgear and instead has brown hair that curls on the sides of her head. She has red lines around the edge of her sleeve and a golden shape around her torso. She also has light brown shoes and red leggings. When she is not wearing armour, she wears a white shirt under a copper-coloured blazer with dark reddish-brown pants and black shoes.
Personality
Ellegaard can be cold and judgmental, but genuinely cares for her close friends. Even Magnus, who she doesn't like for his reckless and not-at-all smart and calculated behavior. Through the extensive grats of her arrogance and circle-chain, she can automatically belittle people into doubting themselves, Olivia being a prime example; intentionally or not. Though she often has arguments and prejudice perspectives with her rival, Magnus, like all her antagonists, her conflict priorities swap to and from on her list of precedence when the situation turns urgent. In spite of all of her flaws, her personality doesn't only consist of negatives: her smarts and cleverness with redstone mechanics are clearly what steered her in the Order of the Stone as an engineer which she knows well. She keeps a habit of openly notifying when she respects somebody amidst her guarded formality. Despite the path of experience, once she gets a negative impression of someone, it can be hard to change her mind with her stubbornness unless a great deal has been done like Jesse volunteering to set off the formidi-bomb and Magnus' death. Her honesty, though commonly over-truthful, can be openly noticed in the reflexive pieces of her temper.
Biography

Legacy (determinant)

Relationships
Allies

Gabriel

Quotes
“ Necessity is the originator of invention, and that monster is my necessity for inventing, of which I am the originator! „ — Ellegaard the Redstone Engineer to Jesse and Olivia. ​ [ determinant ]
“ Wait...waitwaitwait, how do you know what a Command Block is? Not to seem belittling, but we're talking mega-levelled, ultra-genius stuff here. „ — Ellegaard to Jesse
“ Oh, so you're sticking up for Magnus. That's very, sweet. „ — Ellegaard to Axel
“ You really weren't kidding. „ — Ellegaard to Jesse's gang and Magnus if Jesse chooses to find Magnus with Axel
“...and I, Ellegaard, will make a Command Block, today! „ — Ellegaard to Jesse and Olivia
Trivia
Her favourite food is bread, as reported by Gabriel.
When Ellegaard wears her armor, the top brown part of her arm is 4×4 pixels, but when Jesse wears it it is 5×5.
If Jesse goes to Redstonia and Ellegaard dies, there will be banners from Redstonia present in the Order Hall.
In the game's files, assets exist for Ellegaard's top hat and her appearance after her death.

The enchanted flint and steel (alternatively said as glowing flint and steel or portal key ) is a flint and steel with an unknown enchantment. It can create three-block-high columns of corresponding colored fire and activate Overworld portals.
History

Instructions: All the history of the item from all the episodes where it appeared.
It was created by the Old Builders in the past to activate portals to different dimensions.
The blue flint and steel can not be seen in the Order Hall in Season Two, as it was taken by Ivor in A Journey's End?.
Properties
When struck, it can activate Overworld portals or create unique three-block-high flames matching its color.

Trivia
In Order Up!, Jesse will always find the enchanted flint and steel inside the temple of the Old Builders, regardless of which chest they choose to open.
In Minecraft, it is possible to enchant regular flint and steel with the Unbreaking and Mending enchantments; however, as mentioned by Olivia, the enchantment is neither of those enchantments.

title: Dungeons:Enchanted Sheep
text:
Arcade Season 4 info is needed.
Behavior: Passive (Green, Red: Heroes & Passive mobs; Blue: All mobs) Hostile (Green, Red: Enemies & Passive mobs targeted by a hunting bow and its variants)
Classification: Animal, Pet
An enchanted sheep is a summonable pet spawned using an enchanted grass artifact in Minecraft Dungeons.
Behavior
Basic attack
The poisonous green sheep is capable of directly attacking mobs by kicking them with their front legs, similar to mooshrooms.
Strong attack
A more powerful version of the basic attack.
Effect
The enchanted sheep can appear in only three colors, and behave like most summoned mobs, attacking enemies and staying nearby the hero. Each color corresponds to a certain effect.
Speedy Blue
The speedy blue sheep is passive, being capable of increasing the speed aura of the hero.
Fire Red
The fire red sheep walks around burning mobs in the area surrounding it.
Poisonous Green
The poisonous green sheep is capable of summoning a poison cloud upon hitting mobs.

title: Dungeons:Enchantsmith
text: Health points: Non-attackable Behavior: Passive Classification: Villager, Merchant In-game description Replaces a random enchantment with a new enchantment. The enchantsmith is a camp merchant that can be found captured within the Highblock Halls mission of Minecraft Dungeons. They allow you to reroll enchantments on any given weapon or armor for the price of an increasing amount of gold. Spawning Highblock Halls Camp Interaction An item with the custom tag. The enchantsmith can be found captured in the outside area of Highblock Halls, next to the golden key gate. After being rescued by the hero, the enchantsmith can be found in the north-east area of the Camp, near the Nether Cave. The enchantsmith charges gold to change a selected enchantment into a different random enchantment. If a piece of gear that is already enchanted is selected, all enchantment points on that gear are returned. When a piece of gear has been altered by the enchantsmith, it gains a purple "custom" tag. The custom tag does not affect anything about the piece of gear, it is just a label. Pricing The base price to reroll an enchantment depends on the power level of the item, the price rises as the power level rises. Once rerolling an enchantment, the price to reroll a second time is 10% higher than the previous price. So if the price was fifty gold at first, it would be fifty-five gold for a second reroll. The price rises by 10% upon each reroll until reaching the maximum price of one hundred gold. Stacking enchantments If an item has a stackable enchantment in the gilded slot, it does not normally appear in any other enchantment slot on that item. However, by rerolling the item with the enchantsmith, stackable enchantments can appear multiple times even when in the gilded slot. This allows some enchantments to stack up to four times, which is not possible through any other method. Sounds Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Ambience Oneshot,? Buy,? Close (Cancel the trade),? Haggle (Player ask for trade),? Data values In the game files, the enchantsmith is referred to internally by the name EnchanterMerchant and the name Enchanterlogist. In the game files, the enchantsmith's book is referred to internally by the name EnchanterBook and the name EnchanterBook2. In the game files, the enchantsmith's cape is referred to internally by the name Enchanterlogist_Cape. In the game files, the enchantsmith's hanging banner is referred to internally by the name Enchanterlogist_SewThing and the name EnchanterMerchantCloth. Achievements Main article: Achievement Achievement: Customized Customer, In-game description: Make use of the Enchantsmith's service 3 times., Actual requirements (if different): —, Gamerscore earned: 10G, Trophy type (PS): Bronze History Minecraft Dungeons: 1.10.2.0, The enchanter merchant has been added into the game files. Minecraft Dungeons: August 14, 2021, A reddit user found the game files for the enchanter merchant and modded it back into the game, revealing that the official in-game name for the NPC is the enchantsmith. In addition, an unused non hostilechickencan be seen next to the enchantsmith’scamp stall. Minecraft Dungeons: 1.14.1.0, Changed the texture file of the enchantsmith. Minecraft Dungeons: October 12, 2022, The enchantsmith is officially announced in an article announcing Fauna Faire. Minecraft Dungeons: Minecraft Live 2022, The enchantsmith is shown at Minecraft Live, it is confirmed that it uses gold to re-roll enchantments. Minecraft Dungeons: 1.16.2.0, Removed the textures for the enchantsmith's book hat, cape, and sew thing. Minecraft Dungeons: Added the enchantsmith. Minecraft Dungeons: 1.17.0.0, Base charge price (Item level 1) decreased from 100 to 20. Minecraft Dungeons: Price increase per re-roll increased from 5% to 10%. Minecraft Dungeons: Item power scaler decreased from 1 to 0.65. Minecraft Dungeons: Maximum re-roll price decreased from 250 to 100. Trivia The enchantsmith has a pet chicken that can be seen next to its camp stall. Due to its later introduction, it is the only villager merchant that is not present in the final Broken Citadel cutscene. In the Fauna Faire seasonal adventure, the sew thing, signature books hat and cape have been removed. Gallery Renders The enchantsmith's final and official design. title: Dungeons:End Wilds
text: Part of: Other Dimensions Minimum difficulty: II Unlock predecessor: The Stronghold Raid captains: Can very rarely have all six captains Daily trials: Can have daily trials Is This the End? Far from home and surrounded by the endless Void, the only path ahead takes you deeper into the unknown. Don't mistake the disquieting beauty of the wilderness as peace - the enemies that lurk in the shadows will strike if you let your guard down. End Wilds is the fifth other dimensions mission in Minecraft Dungeons, and is part of the Echoing Void DLC. It is unlocked after the completion of The Stronghold, and completing End Wilds unlocks Broken Citadel. title: Story Mode:EnderCon
text: Details Type: Event (EnderCon) Settlement (Endercon's host town) Staff: Announcer Lydia Owen Reuben First appearance: The Order of the Stone Latest appearance: The Order of the Stone EnderCon is an annual town fair featuring a building competition and a marketplace. Its most recent event included an appearance by Gabriel the Warrior and marked the first sighting of the wither storm. The name of the town hosting the event is unconfirmed. History EnderCon was an annual event held in the town. Petra, Jesse, and, depending on player choices, Reuben, arrived at EnderCon. If Jesse instructed Axel and Olivia to stay behind at the building competition to save their fireworks dispenser build, they would later see their creation featured as the winning entry. Alternatively, if Jesse chose to take their friends along after their build was set on fire, the rainbow beacon build of the Ocelots would be showcased instead. After arriving, Petra and Jesse met Axel and Olivia for a brief discussion before heading to a dark alley to conduct a transaction with Ivor. The deal fell through when Ivor scammed Petra and fled. Jesse and Petra pursued him through the town, later regrouping with Axel and Olivia to assist in the chase. During this pursuit, Jesse mistakenly identified someone else as Ivor. If Reuben was not accompanying Jesse when they entered EnderCon, Jesse would later find him at the butcher's shop. If Reuben was with Jesse, he would be captured by the butcher regardless of the prior path. In either case, Jesse would resolve the situation by bargaining or threatening the butcher to secure Reuben's release. After spotting Ivor near the entrance to the hall, the group attempted to follow him but was stopped by an usher due to their lack of tickets. Noticing the usher's fear of chickens, the group decided to create a distraction. They collected slimeballs to craft a slime block, though the materials fell short until Lukas provided a final slimeball. Jesse used the completed slime block to reach and destroy a nearby chicken machine, causing chaos as chickens swarmed the usher. This allowed the group to enter the hall. Once inside, the wither summoned by Ivor grew uncontrollably, causing widespread destruction. The group was forced to flee the hall and became separated. After regrouping with Gabriel at the marketplace, they escaped the town together. Location The host town is located somewhere in the Overworld, near the abandoned bridge and the woods. Architecture The host town's buildings are constructed from various materials, including clay bricks, sandstone, and stone bricks, all featuring windows. The pavements are made of smooth stone, while the sidewalks are built with stone brick stairs and stone bricks. Lampposts are composed of wooden fences topped with redstone lamps and daylight detectors. The town entrance features an archway made of bricks with wooden fences serving as gates. Marketplace stalls are constructed from wooden planks and fences and are typically decorated with banners and paintings. Culture EnderCon's host town is a town where residents participate in various activities, including the EnderCon Building Competition, an event where individuals showcase their building skills. Beyond this, little is known about the town's culture, and there are no detailed descriptions of daily life or cultural traditions. Administration There is no detailed information available regarding the administration of EnderCon's host town. Its leadership structure is not shown, and it is unclear how decisions are made or who holds official authority. Points of interest Building competition Main article: EnderCon Building Competition The event is held every year. It consists of teams creating their builds. The winner's build gets featured at EnderCon and the team gets to visit Gabriel. Hall Main article: Hall Near the marketplace, there is a hall where Gabriel is scheduled to appear as the event's guest. Hall's basement Main article: Basement The basement that Ivor uses as his hideout. Marketplace The marketplace at Endercon includes a variety of stalls and attractions, contributing to the event's overall atmosphere. Notable features include: Game stalls: Several booths offering games and activities for visitors. Mask stand: A booth selling masks. Food stalls: Offering food to visitors. Map booth: A booth where attendees can obtain maps of the event. As Axel points out, free cake is often available nearby. DJ booth: A booth where a DJ plays music. Crafting station: A booth where visitors could use a crafting table. Butcher shop: Managed by Otis, where visitors could get porkchops. Donkey stand: A stall featuring donkeys. Slime booth: Ivy, with the help of her henchman, runs the slime booth where she distributes slimeballs to visitors. Chicken machine: Sigge is the owner of the chicken machine. Interactables Free-roam segment 1 This segment occurs before approaching the hall area. Axel Talk to (A chicken drops after stepping in redstone mechanism.) Axel: Thought you could just wiggle

title: Story Mode:EnderCon Building Competition
text:
Instructions: Information about builds, team names, build names, story, etc.
Details
Type: Event
Staff: Announcer
First appearance: The Order of the Stone
Endercon Building Competition [ sic ] is an event and a location in Episode 1 of Minecraft: Story Mode. The event is held every year. It consists of teams creating their builds. The winner's build gets featured at EnderCon.
History
An annual building competition is organized before the main event, with the winner's build being displayed at the site of the event, and the winner being able to meet different keynote speakers, such as Gabriel the Warrior. In Endercon, if Jesse chooses to go find Reuben alone and let Axel and Olivia save their build, their build would win. However, they are not able to meet Gabriel, since tickets for his keynote had already been sold out, and they have to make a distraction to sneak in. Otherwise, if Jesse persuades Axel and Olivia to go find Reuben with them, their build would not win due to it catching fire and burning down.

title: Dungeons:Endersent
text: Health points: 3500 Binding Eye: 4000 Blight Eye: 4000 Ravenous Eye: 4000 Reaping Eye: 4000 Savage Eye: 4000 Spiked Eye: 3500 Behavior: Hostile Classification: Ender, Powerful Attack strength: Deadly Escape: 400 Teleport Smash: 300 Basic Attack: 140 An endersent is a powerful ender mob that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. Six enchanted endersent can be found in the mainland guarding eyes of ender gating access to the End Wilds through The Stronghold. Lore The Vengeful Heart of Ender sent six enchanted endersent to the Overworld to guard six eyes of ender within void-torn areas across the mainland. These endersent are unique in that they each go by unique titles: the Savage Eye, the Ravenous Eye, the Blight Eye, the Reaping Eye, the Binding Eye, and the Spiked Eye. The following poem is associated with the enchanted endersent. Endersent poem transcript If the way to the End you seek You'll need six of something with which to peek. Defeat Endersent and you will free, What you need to use as a key. One Savage, one Ravenous, one Blight, One Reaping, one Binding, and one Spiked. Survive the Stronghold, place the eyes, Repair the portal and the path will arise! Spawning Creeper Woods Deepwood Brook (after the Spiked Eye's death) Pumpkin Pastures Old Town Hall Shelter Tunnel (after the Savage Eye's death) Soggy Swamp Sunken Watchtower (after the Blight Eye's death) Cacti Canyon Undercroft Undercroft Crypt (after the Binding Eye's death) Desert Temple The Trial of the Nameless Sinister Shrine (after the Reaping Eye's death) Highblock Halls Hideaway Wrecked Workshop (after the Ravenous Eye's death) End Wilds Broken Citadel Tower Behavior Endersent can deal the highest damage output of any powerful mob within the game, dealing more damage than redstone golems and even endermen. However, they do not have the largest health pool of all powerful mobs. As melee mobs, endersent are capable of picking up key golems. They have three behaviors, the latter two are performed once they had either taken melee damage, or were hit by projectiles seven times or more. Basic attack Endersent attack with their arms, performing either a swing or a slam, dealing 140 damage. Deadly escape Endersent smash the ground with both hands, dealing 400 damage as it teleports away, leaving behind three to six watchlings. After the watchlings left behind during the escape are defeated or after a certain amount of time passes (whichever is sooner), the endersent will reappear. Teleport smash If the Endersent's target is out of melee range, it will raise one arm at them before teleporting away and reappearing behind said target, following up almost immediately by smashing the ground with both arms and dealing 300 damage, though less damage overall than its Deadly Escape attack. After reappearance, the endersent's slam will land after 0.45 seconds. Health Endersent have 3500 HP in the code. Enchanted Endersent, with the exception of the Spiked Eye, have 4000 HP in the code, while the spiked eye still has 3500 HP. On Default I, all mobs have 9.6% health and 12% damage of what is set in the code. Sounds Endersent use the enderman boss soundtrack. Music Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Endersent theme pre, bgm_mob_eventEndermanPre-001 Endersent theme loop, bgm_mob_eventEndermanLoop-001 Endersent theme post, bgm_mob_eventEndermanPost-001 Endersent Sounds: Sound, Description, Identifier Attack,? Deadly escape, D6_sfx_mob_endersentDeadlyEscape Death, D6_sfx_mob_endersentDeath Hurt, D6_sfx_mob_endersentHurt Idle smash, D6_sfx_mob_endersentIdleSmash Idle, D6_sfx_mob_endersentIdleVocal Step, D6_sfx_mob_endersentStep Stun, D6_sfx_mob_endersentStun Teleport smash, D6_sfx_mob_endersentTeleportSmash Enchanted endersent Enchanted endersent: Savage Eye, Arena: Shelter Tunnel Enchanted endersent: Ravenous Eye, Arena: Wrecked Workshop Enchanted endersent: Blight Eye, Arena: Sunken Watchtower Enchanted endersent: Reaping Eye, Arena: Sinister Shrine Enchanted endersent: Binding Eye, Arena: Undercroft Crypt Enchanted endersent: Spiked Eye, Arena: Deepwood Brook Each of the six eyes of ender are guarded by enchanted endersent. These special endersent cannot be refought once their eyes of ender have been collected. They spawn in the six endersent sightings found in various mainland missions. Heroes must defeat them to obtain their eyes of ender in order to activate the end portal in The Stronghold. These enchanted endersent do not spawn again once the eyes are collected. Its place is taken by non-enchanted endersent with no eye of ender. On each path leading to the enchanted endersent, one or two endermen may spawn in small ambushes. If the mission was already completed, raid captains may also spawn near the arenas. Savage Eye The Savage Eye in its arena. The Savage Eye is encountered in Shelter Tunnel, a part of the Old Town Hall endersent sighting within Pumpkin Pastures. It possesses the following enchantments: Critical Hit Frenzied To find it, heroes must journey to the stone brick rampart where the rune chamber is. After sighting this structure, heroes may find the Savage Eye's arena. However, the arena can rarely spawn later on during the mission. Ravenous

title: Dungeons:Enderling
text:
The term " enderling " refers to a class of enemies in Minecraft Dungeons related to endermen. They spawn in every mission set in The End.
Lore
They consisted of many different subspecies of endermen that resided within the End, and it did not take the Heroes long to meet them. Like endermen, enderlings used their teleportation to their advantage. Despite this, however, the Heroes prevailed against them, making their way to the Broken Citadel, where they put a final end to the Orb of Dominance once and for all.

title: Dungeons:Enigma Resonator
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Melee Weapon Ranged Weapon
Property: +1 soul gathering
Soul gear only: Yes
In-game description Gives a chance to deal triple damage based on the number of souls you have.
Enigma Resonator is an enchantment that can be found within the various melee and ranged weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Obtaining
Enigma Resonator has a chance to appear in any enchantment slot on a soul melee or ranged weapon, which includes the following weapons:
Melee weapons
Soul Knife
Eternal Knife
Truthseeker
Soul Fists
Soul Scythe
Frost Scythe
Jailor's Scythe
Skull Scythe
Moon Daggers
Ranged weapons
Soul Bow
Bow of Lost Souls
Nocturnal Bow
Shivering Bow
Soul Crossbow
Feral Soul Crossbow
Voidcaller
Soul Hunter Crossbow
Enchantments on soul items can be swapped out for Enigma Resonator by rerolling them at the Enchantsmith.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 0-15% chance to trigger, Cost: 1 enchantment point, Cost (Gilded): 2 enchantment points
Tier: II, Effect: 0-20% chance to trigger, Cost: 2 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 3 enchantment points
Tier: III, Effect: 0-25% chance to trigger, Cost: 3 enchantment points, Cost (Gilded): 4 enchantment points
Built-into
Enigma resonator tier I is built into the following weapons:
Moon Daggers
Soul Fists
Feral Soul Crossbow
Soul Hunter Crossbow
Mobs
Mobs can never be enchanted with Enigma Resonator.
Stacking
Enigma Resonator is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
Usage
Any ranged weapon or melee weapon enchanted with Enigma Resonator has a chance to deal three times more damage based on how many souls the hero currently has. The chance for Enigma Resonator to deal more damage increases with each tier. Enigma Resonator can appear only on weapons that have soul gathering as one of their properties.
Enigma Resonator is separate from Critical Hit. It is possible to deal 9× damage if both enchantments are active. The sounds and visual effects are the same.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Enigma Resonator.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Enigma Resonator" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.

Benedict the Eversource is a chicken that has the ability to lay spawn eggs of almost any kind of mob. She once belonged to the Old Builders, but is shown as now belonging to Isa.
Appearance
Benedict looks like an ordinary chicken, but she has grey eyebrows, a red chain on her neck, and a golden crown on her head.

An exploding crossbow is a ranged weapon that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. Its unique variants are the imploding crossbow and the firebolt thrower.
Obtaining
Missions

Fiery Forge (Adventure)

Usage
Exploding crossbows possess high power, low speed, and a small ammo supply. The quiver starts with 20 arrows, normal arrow bundles give 10 arrows, supply chest bundles give another 20 arrows, and surprise gift bundles give 5 arrows. The exploding crossbow is a little smaller than most crossbows.
Damage caused by the arrows can be increased with enchantments, the Strength buff, and certain armor that boost ranged damage, such as hunter's armor. Also, it is the only ranged weapon in the game that is unaffected by the Deflect enchantment. The Piercing enchantment, however, does not work properly with the exploding crossbow, as the arrows are used up on impact.

title: Dungeons:Explorer
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Armor
Soul gear only: No
In-game description For every one hundred blocks explored on the map, you regain a small amount of health.
Explorer is an enchantment that can be found within the various armor of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 0.3% health regained, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: 0.7% health regained, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: 1.0% health regained, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Explorer grants the hero the ability to regain health for every one hundred blocks explored on the map, these blocks must be unexplored, therefore the hero cannot regain health when traversing already explored areas. The amount of health regained increases with each tier, at the maximum tier 1% of the hero's maximum health is regained.
Stacking
Explorer is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Explorer.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Explorer" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.

title: Legends:Extendable Wall
text:
Type: Player-built
Cost: 3 Wood (per column)
An extendable wall is a defensive structure in Minecraft Legends.
Obtaining
Extendable walls can be placed by picking a starting point and an endpoint in placement preview mode. One build allay will be used to create the entire wall, but each column in the wall will cost three wood. Extendable walls are also able to transform into stone if built in range of a masonry.
Usage
Extendable walls are a basic defensive structure that prevent enemies from entering your base. They are destroyed individually as columns.
Quotes
Name: Extendable Wall, Category: Player Building Melodies, Unlocked by: Automatically unlocked, Quote: "Creates a defensible perimeter that protects structures from enemies. You need to enter Placement Preview and pick a starting point and an ending point to build a wall. The larger the wall, the larger the cost. Walls are destroyed in segments, make sure to patch them."

title: Story Mode:Formidi-Bomb
text:
Details
Status: Used
Creator: Soren (designed) Jesse (crafted)
Owner: Soren
Obtained during: The Last Place You Look
First appearance: The Last Place You Look
Latest appearance: A Block and a Hard Place
The formidi-bomb, also referred to as the " F-bomb ", is a powerful explosive created by combining the super TNT and gunpowder. It was used against the wither storm in The Last Place You Look.
History
Magnus and Ellegaard claims that Soren has a super explosive known as the formidi-bomb. After being recruited, Soren gives Jesse the crafting materials for creating it.
In the process of crafting the bomb when confronting the wither storm, Jesse is pulled into the tractor beam along with the ingredients and the crafting table. While suspended, they crafted the bomb. The bomb subsequently detonates, seemingly destroying the wither storm.
Properties
According to Soren, attempting to craft the bomb is very dangerous, as the ingredients are highly unstable when put together. Because of this, the bomb is automatically primed upon being crafted and is set to explode in around half a minute. The explosive power of it is capable of temporarily disabling the wither storm, however it is unable to destroy command blocks.
Obtaining
Formidi-Bomb
Episodes
The Last Place You Look — obtained by crafting.
Appearances
The Last Place You Look
Trivia
Magnus and Axel call the formidi-bomb the "F-Bomb", a joke which alludes to a common curse word.
The formidi-bomb consumes the crafting table that is used to create it.
Gallery
Jesse holding the formidi-bomb
The formidi-bomb being pulled

title: Dungeons:Nameless One
text: Health points: 6500 Behavior: Hostile Classification: Undead, Boss Attack strength: 250 THE NAMELESS ONE The Nameless One is a boss variant of a necromancer. It summons its skeleton vanguard to attack heroes before joining the fray and targeting you with large, slow-moving projectiles. It can also confuse heroes by creating mirror images of itself. The Nameless One is the necromancer boss variant found at the end of the Desert Temple mission of Minecraft Dungeons. It once ruled a long-forgotten nameless kingdom, and wields an enchanted staff used to perform necromancy. Spawning Desert Temple ( Boss ) Tower ( Boss ) Biography Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Dungeons that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk! In The Rise of the Arch-Illager, when Archie is leading the Illagers to the Squid Coast, he decides to take a wide arc around the Desert Temple. Unknown to Archie, despite this, the Nameless One knows of the abilities of the Orb of Dominance and sends husks and skeletons to follow the Illagers under the cover of darkness. After the Illagers launch their raid on Squid Coast and Archie is confronted by Yumi, the Undead army appears. They launch their own raid, attacking Illagers, villagers, and heroes alike. Archie is shot twice by two skeletons and falls unconscious. Archie wakes up in the midst of being carried to the desert temple and the leading necromancer knocks him out with its staff. He wakes in a prison cell held by a different necromancer who leads him captive to the throne room. In the throne room, Archie meets the Nameless One in person. The Nameless One makes it clear that it desires the Orb of Dominance to make the Undead army immune to sunlight. Archie makes it clear that he will not part with the Orb of Dominance. With both of them trying to gain the upper hand in the tense conversation, they eventually come to an agreement: Archie can use the Orb of Dominance to make the Undead immune to the sunlight and the Nameless One will allow Archie to command the Undead army. When the heroes first arrive at the Desert Temple, the in-game description of the mission implies that the Nameless One and Archie turned on each other. However, it is vague on which one of the two was the first to betray the other. The description seems to lean more on Archie being the betrayer and desiring the Nameless One’s staff. In the Desert Temple, the heroes see the Nameless One asleep on its throne, surrounded by several sleeping vanguards. When the heroes attempt to grab its staff, it awakens and attempts to fend off the heroes. Once the Nameless One is defeated, the heroes are free to destroy the staff and leave the Desert Temple. Behavior The Nameless One has five behaviors. Awake source When the hero first initiates the boss fight, the Nameless One slowly rises from its tomb-like throne, opening its eyes. Basic attack The Nameless One fires large, slow-moving green projectiles towards its target current location. Deals heavy damage if it hits. This is its most common attack. Summon After a charging animation, the Nameless One summons a wall of seven vanguards between itself and the hero. Used more sparsely. Strong attack The Nameless One summons one to seven false kings, which fire one green beam before disappearing. If there are two or more false kings, they will teleport to surround the hero and all fire green beams at the hero. Teleport When not attacking, it teleports away from the hero and hovers in one spot. But when the hero attacks it first, then it attacks. False king A false king is a mirror image of the Nameless One that is summoned to confuse the hero. They often teleport in a ring formation around the hero and fire one green beam at them, before disappearing. It is not possible to attack these false kings. Health The Nameless One has 6,500 HP in the code, the first health of any boss than the unbreakable ones. On Default I, all mobs have 9.6% health and 12% damage of what is set in the code.
